info.name: OpenTable API Documentation
info.description:
OpenTable's API ecosystem is designed to empower restaurants and their partners to extend and enhance the core functionality of OpenTable. By providing robust tools for booking management, real-time guest and reservation data, our APIs are at the heart of delivering exceptional dining experiences. We offer a suite of capabilities that allow developers to innovate, customize, and create solutions that complement our platform. Whether you’re looking to streamline operations or enhance customer engagement, OpenTable APIs are here to help.

We value our partnerships with developers and restaurant operators and are committed to fostering successful collaborations. For partnership inquiries, please fill out our application form.

For information about API availability, outages, maintenance, and other service-related incidents, please subscribe to updates at **status.opentable.com**. This is the best way to stay informed about operational events that may impact our APIs.

# Platform Policy

**Permitted Use of APIs**

You may use the OpenTable application programming interface (“API”) and any data provided through an API only in accordance with your applicable agreement with OpenTable (“Terms”), this Platform Policy and OpenTable’s applicable API documentation and policies. In this Platform Policy, “you” means the entity which agreed to the Terms with OpenTable. In event of any conflict between this Platform Policy and the Terms, this Platform Policy will control as relates to the API. Otherwise, the Terms continue to apply to the API and any activity under this Platform Policy.

You will only access (or attempt to access) an API by the means described in the documentation for that API. If OpenTable assigns you credentials (e.g. client IDs), you must use them only with the applicable APIs. You may not share your credentials with third-parties or service providers without OpenTable’s consent (and if such parties require access you must request individual credentials for them). We may also require rotation of credentials, and it is the partners obligation to update accordingly. Your API usage rights are limited, non-exclusive, non-sublicensable and non-transferable.

OpenTable may set and enforce limits on your use of the APIs (e.g. limiting the number of API requests that you may make or the number of users you may serve), in its sole discretion. You agree to, and will not attempt to circumvent, such limitations documented with each API. If you would like to use any API beyond these limits, you must obtain OpenTable’s express written consent (and OpenTable may decline such request or condition acceptance on your agreement to additional terms and/or charges for that use). In any event, Client may not use the OpenTable APIs in any way which might, in OpenTable’s sole discretion, overburden, impair or disrupt the OpenTable APIs or OpenTable Network.

You agree not to (and not to authorize any third party): 1. Use the APIs other than as permitted; 2. Modify or create any derivative works of the APIs; 3. Distribute, sell or sublicense the APIs or use them on behalf of third parties (unless expressly permitted in the Terms); 4. Introduce any viruses, worms, Trojan horses, or other harmful code to OpenTable via the APIs; 5. Attempt to interfere with the proper working of the APIs or OpenTable products, services or systems or compromise their integrity or security; 6. Scrape any OpenTable website or system for data, or otherwise seek to access data or OpenTable materials through means not approved by OpenTable in writing; or 7. Use the APIs in a manner or for a purpose that violates laws or third-party rights; 8. Access the APIs for competitive analysis or disseminate performance information (including uptime, response time and/or benchmarks) relating to the APIs or OpenTable products, services or systems.

**Data and Security**

You may use any data made available through the APIs solely as permitted in the Terms or otherwise expressly permitted in writing by OpenTable. OpenTable does not grant you any other rights to data.

Without limiting any security requirements in the Terms, you agree to provide the level of protection to any such data as required under applicable law and to employ, at your own cost, appropriate physical, technical and organizational security measures designed to protect such data from unauthorized access, destruction, use, modification or disclosure.

**Changes, Suspension and Termination**

From time to time in its discretion, with or without notice, OpenTable may change the APIs (including any API documentation) or related OpenTable products or services, discontinue any or all data available through the APIs or to specific recipients, or place limits on access to the APIs. Future versions of the APIs may not be compatible with systems used with previous versions. OpenTable typically makes these changes as part of its overall API program and is unable to provide individual notices of the changes.

Please design the APIs backwards compatible. OpenTable periodically updates its APIs to add or remove attributes to the payloads.

Except as set forth in the Terms, OpenTable may suspend or terminate your access to the APIs (in whole or in part) in its discretion, for any or no reason and with or without notice.

OpenTable will have no liability resulting from taking any actions described in this Section.

**No Support or Maintenance**

You understand that you are responsible for all development and costs associated with integrating with the APIs.

OpenTable has no obligation to provide any maintenance or support for the APIs or to fix any errors or defects. If OpenTable in its discretion provides any updates, modifications, enhancements, and other new releases to the APIs, such materials will be deemed included in the “APIs” under this Platform Policy unless OpenTable specifies otherwise.

**OpenTable Rights**

OpenTable owns all rights, title and interest (including intellectual property rights) in and to the API. OpenTable reserves all rights not expressly granted.

**Disclaimer**

EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET OUT IN THE TERMS, OPENTABLE MAKES NO SPECIFIC PROMISES ABOUT THE APIS. FOR EXAMPLE, OPENTABLE DOES NOT MAKE ANY COMMITMENTS ABOUT THE DATA OR CONTENT ACCESSED THROUGH THE APIS, THE SPECIFIC FUNCTIONS OF THE APIS, OR THEIR RELIABILITY, AVAILABILITY, SECURITY OR ABILITY TO MEET YOUR NEEDS. OPENTABLE PROVIDES THE APIS “AS IS” AND “WITH ALL FAULTS”. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY PROVIDED FOR IN THE TERMS, TO THE EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, OPENTABLE AND ITS THIRD PARTY LICENSORS DISCLAIMS ALL WARRANTIES, GUARANTEES, CONDITIONS, REPRESENTATIONS, AND UNDERTAKINGS, WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR STATUTORY, INCLUDING IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, TITLE, NON-INFRINGEMENT AND FITNESS FOR ANY PURPOSE.

Last Updated: Dec 20 2024

## Terms of Use and Privacy Policy

[Terms of Use](https://www.opentable.com/legal/terms-and-conditions)

[Privacy Policy](https://www.opentable.com/legal/privacy-policy)

## Brand Assets

Affiliates must comply with the OpenTable brand guidelines when linking to the OpenTable site.

OpenTable assets including the OpenTable logo, Reserve Buttons, and Brand Story can be found here: [https://www.opentable.design/](https://www.opentable.design/)

# Platform Basics

OpenTable uses OAuth 2.0 as the primary authorization mechanism. This means that an access token must be obtained and submitted with all requests. See [Authorization](https://dev.opentable.com/partner-portal/documentation#authorization) section for more details. OpenTable’s Partner APIs can only be accessed via **HTTPS**. This applies to all environments.

## Status Page

We require all partners to subscribe to the OpenTable API Status Dashboard to be notified of upcoming maintenance or outages.

[https://status-api.opentable.com/](https://status-api.opentable.com/)  
[https://status-api.opentable.com/subscribe](https://status-api.opentable.com/subscribe)

### Content Negotiation

Data is sent and received in JSON format unless otherwise specified in this documentation. Clients should specify **application/json** in the **Accept** header for all requests to the server.

### Compression

OpenTable’s Partner Network APIs support LZ4 encoding. Client applications should specify **lz4** via the **Accept-Encoding** HTTP header.

The partner system should be able to send and respond with lz4 compressed content to improve performance and should be implemented by your partner server implementation and client implementation.

### Unique Request Ids

All POST, PUT, and PATCH HTTP requests should contain a unique X-Request-Id header which is used to ensure idempotent message processing in case of a retry.

## SSL-TLS Support

OpenTable uses Akamai as a CDN partner. Akamai’s SSL-TLS profile supports strong ciphers only. We will be keeping our ciphers current and you may be asked to update these as required.

OpenTable **supports TLS 1.3** for all API communication. Ensure your systems are upgraded to maintain secure connectivity.

# **Getting Started**

To obtain access to OpenTable APIs, it is requisite to register and secure approval as an integration partner. This can be requested via [https://www.opentable.com/restaurant-solutions/api-partners/become-a-partner/.](https://www.opentable.com/restaurant-solutions/api-partners/become-a-partner/.)

Upon submission, your application will undergo a comprehensive review by our partnerships team, and you will be notified upon the completion of this review process. Once approved, you will be granted access to a Sandbox environment to try out the integration.

# Environments

OpenTable has two primary integration environments; Sandbox (pre-production) and Production. Partners should test their integrations in Sandbox (pre-production) where the changes are transient and do not affect live production customers or restaurants. Once partners have completed their integrations they may deploy them into the production environment.

## **Accessing APIs in Sandbox (Pre-Production) Environment**

All approved API partners will be granted access to the OpenTable Sandbox environment, which can be utilized for the discovery and testing of API integrations. The following APIs are currently available in the Sandbox Environment for approved partners:

- [Authorization](https://docs.opentable.com/#9205636d-5325-4b1c-adfa-5a7600de6fa8)
    
- [Directory API](https://docs.opentable.com/#c43e6c99-e40c-415a-b38b-3527753b7ac4)
    
- [Sync API (All Tiers)](https://docs.opentable.com/#5d2e3a06-fca9-4d01-a17b-e0aa57e4bdf5)
    

Further details regarding the Restaurant IDs pertinent to accessing these APIs within the Sandbox Environment will be provided in the partner welcome email.

The documentation also contains curated postman collections to implement common business use cases. Developers are advised to leverage the same in order truly explore the capabilities of OpenTable APIs

**Note:** The sandbox environment is specifically designed to facilitate development and testing experiences with OpenTable APIs. It is imperative that these APIs are not employed for any live integrations, as the responses are curated for a sandbox experience and do not impact actual restaurant operations or diner experiences.

## Accessing APIs in Production Environment

Access to our APIs is subject to approval and requires the execution of a formal agreement outlining the terms of use. Before gaining access, all potential partners must undergo a review process to ensure compatibility with our platform and compliance with our business policies.

Not all APIs are available for every type of partnership. Eligibility for specific APIs is determined based on the nature of the partnership, use case, and technical feasibility. Additionally, the cost of access may vary depending on the API or the selected API tier. Pricing and availability will be communicated during the approval and contracting process.

This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute an offer of access to our APIs. Access to production data will only be granted after the agreement is signed and all necessary approvals are obtained.

item.name: Integrations Marketplace
Partner Onboarding requirements
item.description:
### **Partner Marketing Requirements**

To facilitate the tile creation, partners are required to send us content, logos, and redirect links.

### **Marketing Content**

For the Value Proposition page, OpenTable requires comprehensive content that highlights the value of the partner's product and its integration with OpenTable.

Here's what OpenTable needs from you:

- **Logos:** They should be without a background or border. The allowed format is SVG.
    
    - 64×64 pixels - circular.
        
    - 256x256 pixels - circular.
        
    - 200×48 pixels - rectangular.
        
- **Login or sign up Screenshot:** Should have dimensions of 400x300 pixels. The allowed formats are PNG and SVG. (only applicable for OAuth onboarding).
    
- **Product Image:** Should have dimensions of 400x400 pixels. The allowed formats are PNG and SVG.
    
- **Hero Title**
    
- **Hero description**
    
- **Secondary Title**
    
- **Secondary Description:** Maximum of four statements.
    
- **Signup URL**
    
    - Label: e.g Not a member?
        
    - URL Label: e.g check here
        
    - URL
        
- **Step Details**: Maximum of six steps outlining the onboarding procress.
    

**Connected Page Details**

- **Description**
    
- **Partner Support Email**
    
- **Learn more / Partner website**
    

Please note, OpenTable may make modifications to the content to align with brand standards and character counts.

item.name: Standard Onboarding - OAuth Flow
item.description: # **OAuth Flow** This is our preferred and recommended onboarding flow. In this flow, the same information is passed in both the JSON request body and the query parameters. ## **Step 1: User Authentication** This flow requires 1 to 1 mapping. If the login (step 1) is for multiple locations, we expect the partner to allow the user to choose the correct location. **Process:** Restaurant is redirected to the partner login/connect link to allow the user to begin the authentication flow. **GET request with following parameters:** - **client_id:** Partner must provide it to OpenTable - **redirect_uri** - Pre production uri: [https://gc-pp-rs.otenv.com/redirect](https://gc-pp-rs.otenv.com/redirect) - Production uri: [https://guestcenter.opentable.com/redirect](https://guestcenter.opentable.com/redirect) - **state:** This may be used to pass session information through. For non POS integrations such voice AI, we only pass the following fields in a JSON format: - RID (restaurant id) - origin (you, the partner) - destination: ixmarketplace - isStandardPartner: true or false - partnerID State example ``` json { "rid": 1234, "origin": "", "destination": "ixmarketplace", "isStandardPartner": true, "partnerId": 123 } ``` ## **Step 2: User Confirmation and Authorization** **Process:** Partner asks the user to confirm the connection and authorize the requested features to OpenTable. If the user accepts, they should be redirected to the provided redirect_uri with the specified parameters. **Response from step 1 must have:** **code:** This is traded for an API Access Token **state:** The state parameter provided to the login endpoint. ## **Step 3: Obtaining Access Token** **Process:** OpenTable obtains an access token for the onboarding. **POST request with the followiing parameters:** - **client_id:** Must match the client_id provided in step 1. - **client_secret:** The secret key for your application. - **code:** The authentication code returned in step 2. - **grant_type:** Must be authorization_code - **redirect_uri:** Must match the redirect_uri provided in step 1 **Response:** The response to this request should be a JSON object with the following values: - **access_token:** A token that may be used to make Partner API requests. - **expires_in:** The remaining lifetime of the provided token. Need to be returned in seconds. If the Access token does not expire, this is not needed. - **refresh_token** : A refresh token that may be used to obtain a new access token. If the Access token does not expire, this is not needed. - **token_type:** Bearer - **Location:** Partner must provide it to OpenTable. This can be anything, as long as it's unique per RID since OpenTable uses it for internal mapping. - location_id - location_name How OpenTable expects the response: ``` json { "access_token": "", "refresh_token": "", "token_type": "Bearer", "expires_in": 7200, "location": { "location_id": "abc1234", "location_name": "Standard Test Location" } } ``` --- ## **Token Renewal:** **Parameters for Renewing Token:** - **client_id:** Must match the client_id provided in step 1. - **client_secret:** The secret key for your application. - **refresh_token:** The refresh token returned in the previous step. - **grant_type:** Must be refresh_token Response sample ``` json { "access_token": "", "refresh_token": "", "token_type": "Bearer", "expires_in": 7200 } ``` ## **Token Revocation** **This will be used to disconnect restaurants from the integration. Not to be confused with offboarding end point.** **API Endpoint:** **Parameters:** - **client_id**: Must match the client_id provided in step 1. - **client_secret:** The secret key for your application. - **token:** The token to be revoked. **Response:** The response to this request shall be 200 OK for success. ### Onboard Endpoint (Optional) This is used to **confirm** that the RID is onboarded (not to onboard the RID) Response sample ``` json POST / Authorisation : Bearer Request Body { "rid": , "partner_identifier": "" } ``` ### Offboard endpoint (Optional) This is used to **confirm** that the RID is offboarded (not to offboard the RID, this is done by the Revoke endpoint) Response sample ``` json POST / Authorisation : Bearer Request Body { "rid": , "partner_identifier": "" } ``` ## Summary For pre production and production, this is what OpenTable needs from you: - Client ID\* - Client Secret\* - Access token API\* - Revoke token API\*: Used to disconnect the restaurant from the integration - API host\*: API domain - OAuth host\*: Can be the same as or different from, the API domain. - Partner's login URL\* - Location API\*: Only Applies if you're POS partner - Refresh token API (Optional): If the access token doesn't expire, this is not needed. - Onboard API (Optional): Used to send confirmation when RID is onboarded. - Offboard API (Optional): Used to send confirmation when RID is offboarded. #### Examples **OAuth Host:** `www.example-OAuth-Host.com` - **Access token API:** `/oauth/token` - **Full path:** `www.example-OAuth-Host.com/oauth/token` - **Revoke token API:** `/oauth/revoke` - **Full path:** `www.example-OAuth-Host.com/oauth/revoke` - **Refresh token API:** `/oauth/refresh` - **Full path:** `www.example-OAuth-Host.com/oauth/refresh` **API Host:** `www.example-API-Host.com` - **Location API:** `/apihost/location` - **Full path:** `www.example-API-Host.com/apihost/location` - **Onboard API:** `/apihost/onboard` - **Full path:** `www.example-API-Host.com/apihost/onboard` - **Offboard API:** `/apihost/offboard` - **Full path:** `www.example-API-Host.com/apihost/offboard` ## **Additional Details, Success and Error Codes** Applies to all endpoints - **Message**: Successful - **Reason**: Successful - **Status**: 200 - **Message**: Bad Request - **Reason**: Your request is invalid - **Status**: 400 - **Message**: invalid_token - **Reason**: Invalid or Expired Token Provided - **Status**: 401 Unauthorized - **Message**: Forbidden - **Reason**: No access available under your account - **Status**: 403 Forbidden

item.name: POS Onboarding - OAuth Flow
item.description:
# **OAuth Flow**

This is our preferred and recommended onboarding flow.

**Redirect URI**

- Pre-Production: `https://gc-pp-rs.otenv.com/pos/restaurants/onboard/oauth/callbacks`
    
- Production: `https://guestcenter.opentable.com/pos/restaurants/onboard/oauth/callbacks`
    

## APIs requiered from partner

### Partner Access Token

**Without PKCE**

``` bash
curl --location --request POST '{{OAUTH_HOST}}/{{TOKEN_PATH}}?code={{AUTH_CODE}}&grant_type=authorization_code&client_id={{CLIENT_ID}}&redirect_uri={{REDIRECT_URI}}' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: Basic {{BASE64_CREDENTIALS}}' \
--data-raw '{
  "code": "{{AUTH_CODE}}",
  "grant_type": "authorization_code",
  "client_id": "{{CLIENT_ID}}",
  "client_secret": "{{CLIENT_SECRET}}",
  "redirect_uri": "{{REDIRECT_URI}}"
}'

 ```

**WIth PKCE enabled**

There is no `client_id` and `client_secret` in the body. Instead a `code_verifier` is used

``` bash
curl --location --request POST '{{OAUTH_HOST}}/{{TOKEN_PATH}}?code={{AUTH_CODE}}&grant_type=authorization_code&client_id={{CLIENT_ID}}&redirect_uri={{REDIRECT_URI}}' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: Basic {{BASE64_CREDENTIALS}}' \
--data-raw '{
  "code": "{{AUTH_CODE}}",
  "grant_type": "authorization_code",
  "code_verifier": "{{PKCE_CODE_VERIFIER}}",
  "redirect_uri": "{{REDIRECT_URI}}"
}'

 ```

**Expected Response**

The `expires_in` and `refresh_token` are optional.

``` json
{
  "access_token": "eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...",
  "token_type": "Bearer",
  "expires_in": 3600, 
  "refresh_token": "def50200abc..."
}

 ```

### Location API

Once the user logs into the Partner using OAuth Creds, the user should be redirected back OT4R, we then show a location Selector Page for POS

``` bash
curl --location --request GET 'https://{{API_HOST}}/{{LOCATION_PATH}}' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer {{ACCESS_TOKEN}}' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json'

 ```

**Expected Response**

``` json
[
    {
      "name": "Downtown Burger Co",
      "partner_identifier": "LOC-001",
      "street1": "123 Main St",
      "street2": "Suite 100",
      "city": "New York",
      "state": "NY",
      "country": "US",
      "zip": "10001"
    },
    {
      "name": "Uptown Burger Co",
      "partner_identifier": "LOC-002",
      "street1": "456 Park Ave",
      "street2": null,
      "city": "New York",
      "state": "NY",
      "country": "US",
      "zip": "10022"
    },
    ...
]

 ```

### Onboarding API

``` bash
curl --location --request POST 'https://{{API_HOST}}/{{ONBOARD_PATH}}' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer {{ACCESS_TOKEN}}' \
--data-raw '{
  "rid": "{{RID}}",
  "partner_identifier": "{{PARTNER_IDENTIFIER}}"
}'

 ```

**Expected Response**

We expect a successful response status 200 from the partner when the activation is done successfully. However message as the below sample will be useful for debugging.

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Location linked successfully"
}

 ```

### Offboarding API

``` bash
curl --location --request POST 'https://{{API_HOST}}/{{OFFBOARD_PATH}}' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer {{ACCESS_TOKEN}}' \
--data-raw '{
  "rid": "{{RID}}",
  "partner_identifier": "{{PARTNER_IDENTIFIER}}"
}'

 ```

**Expected Response**

We expect a successful response status 200 from the partner when the deactivation is done successfully. However message as the below sample will be useful for debugging.

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Location unlinked successfully"
}

 ```

### Refresh Token API

Only needed if your token expires.

``` bash
curl --location --request POST 'https://{{OAUTH_HOST}}/{{REFRESH_PATH}}' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{
  "client_id": "{{CLIENT_ID}}",
  "client_secret": "{{CLIENT_SECRET}}",
  "refresh_token": "{{REFRESH_TOKEN}}",
  "grant_type": "refresh_token"
}'

 ```

**Expected Response**

``` json
{
  "access_token": "eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...",
  "token_type": "Bearer",
  "expires_in": 3600,
  "refresh_token": "new_refresh_token_xyz..."
}

 ```

item.name: Onboarding - Unique Identifier
item.description:
### **Immediate Onboarding via Partner Unique Identifier**

If OAuth is not an option, you may choose to use a Partner Unique Identifier (e.g. unique account number, URL, string) to facilitate the onboarding process immediately.

### **Step 1: User Obtains partner unique Identifier**

**Process:** The partner shows the user where to get the Unique Identifer

### **Step 2: OpenTable sends the Partner unique identifier details via API call and validates the account**

**Process:**

- **Unique ID Submission:** User inputs the identifier (e.g., Account URL or ID).
    
- **Duplicate Prevention:** OpenTable validates that the ID is not currently provisioned elsewhere in the OpenTable ecosystem.
    
- **External Verification:** OpenTable triggers a `POST` request to the Partner's validation endpoint. The Partner must return a `200 OK` (or specific success payload) to verify the ID is valid and active on their end.
    

### **Step 3: Partner responds with the Restaurant’s details associated with the unique identifier**

**Process:** Similar to OpenTable’s validation, the partner should confirm internally that the identifier is not already provisioned for another restaurant. Additional internal validation checks by the partner should be considered as applicable. This is the second endpoint required for the onboarding process.

**Suggested URL:** `opentable/reservation_partner?partner_identifier=`

**Request headers: Content-Type: application/json**

**Authorization: bearer or basic**

**Response:**

- **name:** The restaurant’s name as stored by the partner.
    
- **partner_identifier:** The restaurant’s unique partner identifier.
    
- **street1:** The restaurant’s street address.
    
- **street2:** The restaurant's suite or unit number (if applicable)
    
- **city:** The restaurant’s city.
    
- **state:** The restaurant’s state.
    
- **country:** The restaurant’s country, expressed as full country name**.**
    
- **zip:** The restaurant’s ZIP code.
    

``` json
{
    "name": "Restaurant Name",
    "partner_identifier": "1234-5678-9009-1234567890",
    "address": {
        "street1": "123 Main St.",
        "street2": "Suite 100",
        "city": "Any Town",
        "state": "CA",
        "country": "US",
        "zip": "55512"
    }
}

 ```

**Responses:** The following responses are expected to be returned from the partner. A corresponding message will surface to the OpenTable for Restaurants user.

- **HTTP 200: “**Success” (error message not applicable)
    
- **HTTP 404:** “This identifier is not valid. Please try a different identifier.”
    
- **HTTP 409:** “The identifier you entered is already active within OpenTable. Please enter a different identifier.”
    
- **HTTP 503:** “We were not able to verify this identifier. Please try again.”
    

### **Step 4: OpenTable for Restaurants User Validation**

**Process:**

- OpenTable displays restaurant's details from the partner and OpenTable which includes restaurant name, physical address
    
- User compares and confirms the match.
    

### **Step 5: OpenTable, upon confirmation of match, sends a follow-up API call to partner for onboarding.**

**Process:** Upon the OpenTable for Restaurants user confirming the account details between OpenTable and the partner match, OpenTable calls the partner with an activation request.

#### **Partner Activation: POST**

A partner endpoint called by OpenTable, triggered by the OpenTable for Restaurants user submission, to onboard the integration on the partner’s end.

**Suggested URL:** `opentable/reservation_partner`

**Request headers:** Content-Type: application/json

**Authorization:** bearer or basic

**Response:**

**Status code:** 200

- **rid:** The restaurant’s unique OT identifier
    
- **partner_identifier:** The restaurant’s unique partner identifier
    

### **Step 6: Partner automatically executes configuration needs, onboarding, and completes the integration.**

### **Step 7: Partner responds to API requests with a signal that integration is completed and integration is functional and online.**

### **Offboarding: Partner will also need to make available an API for offboarding the restaurant integration.**

As part of the expected functionality restaurants can find in the Integrations Marketplace, we allow restaurants to offboard to an integration.

#### **Partner Deactivation: POST**

**Process:** A Partner endpoint called by OpenTable to offboard the integration on the Partner end. Upon receiving the deactivation request, the Partner programmatically executes necessary processes to automatically deactivate the integration on its end.

To confirm a successful deactivation, OpenTableʼs call response is a status code 200. This endpoint uses an access token obtained from the Client Credentials Flow.

**Suggested URL:** `https://partner-base-url/opentable/api/v1/disconnect-restaurant`

**Request headers:** Content-Type: application/json

**Authorization:** bearer

**Note:** for the bearer token, the partner can either provide an endpoint to get this token via basic client credentials (client_id & client_secret) or x-api-key - {client secret provided uniquely to OpenTable}

**Request Payload**

- **rid:** The restaurant’s unique OT identifier
    

**Sample payload**

``` json
{
"rid": "123456"
}

 ```

**Response:** Status Code 200

### **Additional Details, Success and Error Codes**

- **Message**: Successful
    
    - **Reason**: Successful
        
    - **Status**: 200
        
- **Message**: Bad Request
    
    - **Reason**: Your request is invalid
        
    - **Status**: 400
        
- **Message**: invalid_token
    
    - **Reason**: Invalid or Expired Token Provided
        
    - **Status**: 401 Unauthorized
        
- **Message**: Forbidden
    
    - **Reason**: No access available under your account
        
    - **Status**: 403 Forbidden

OpenTable strives to provide integrations that allow restaurants to maximize their time, revenue and customer loyalty. We offer restaurants a number of integration options and continue to grow that landscape daily. ### **The Restaurant Experience** Partnerships are meant to optimize the restaurant’s experience and available functionality. OpenTable offers a main hub: The Integrations Marketplace. Located within the restaurant’s web portal, restaurants will be able to learn about partnership opportunities, onboard an integration or offboard an integration. As part of OpenTable standards, we expect partners to leverage one of the available Onboarding Options. Partners will have a dedicated tile, which will walk the restaurant through the flow. _Detailed below: The Integrations Marketplace_ _Detailed below: Active Integrations for a Specific Restaurant_ _Detailed below: Click on the Tile and easily offboard_ ### **Restaurant Onboarding Scenarios** Opentable offers 2 options for the integration onboarding flow: Oauth (preferred) or Unique Identifier. For both implementations the expectation is to expose an easy and immediate opportunity to onboard the partnership functionality. #### **Oauth Flow Scenario:** _Detailed_ _example_ _from popmenu below: Page 1, Oauth Flow Scenario, Value Proposition Page._ _Detailed example below: Page 2, Oauth Flow Scenario, Overview of steps_ _Detailed example below: Page 3, Oauth Flow Scenario, Redirect to partner log-in._ _Detailed example below: Page 4, Oauth Flow Scenario, Partner Authentication. For non-POS onboarding such as Voice AI. The user might only see "add/choose location" If a user has multiple locations with you, the partner, the restaurant should be able to choose the correct location._ _Detailed below: Page 5, Oauth Flow Scenario, Redirect and confirmation_ ### **Unique Identifier Scenario:** _Detailed below: Page 1, Unique Identifier Scenario, Value Proposition Page._ _Detailed below: Page 2, Unique Identifier Scenario, Overview of Steps, where to find information_ _Detailed below: Page 2, Unique Identifier Scenario, Once information is verified and details confirmed with the partner. Partner restaurant and OpenTable restaurant details shown side by side for visual confirmation._ _Detailed below: Page 3, Unique Identifier Scenario, Redirect and confirmation_ ### **Lead Captures for Partners** Often, restaurants will discover new partner opportunities or discover new integration opportunities in the Integrations Marketplace. For restaurants that are not currently leveraging the partner, there are 2 options that allow for lead tracking and new restaurant acquisition: ### **Option A: A redirect link for lead generation** - Requires lead generation tracking to be shared with OpenTable - Link is displayed in the value proposition page - This option is most optimal when choosing Unique Identifier Onboarding Flow, but can also be used for the Oauth Onboarding Flow. _Detailed below: Option A, Redirect link for lead generation, Value Proposition Page._ ### **Option B: Sign Up flow during authentication** - The Sign Up flow will also require lead generation tracking - This would show as part of the authentication page on the partner experience page - This option is most optimal when choosing the Oauth Onboarding Flow _Detailed below: Option B, Page 1, Sign Up Flow, Value Proposition Page._ _Detailed below: Option B, Page 2, Sign Up Flow, Overview of Steps_ _**Detailed below: Option B, Page 3, Sign Up Flow, Option shown in the redirected window**_ ### **Links** If Option A for the lead generation flow is selected, the partner is required to send us a URL. The URL is required to be trackable by the partner and reporting should be accessible to OpenTable.

item.name: Authorization
item.description: ## OpenTable OAuth 2.0 Authorization Documentation OpenTable utilizes OAuth 2.0 for authorizing access to protected resources. The authorization process involves the following steps: 1. Request client ID and secret. 2. Obtain an access token using the issued client credentials. 3. Use the access token to access protected resources. ### Obtaining an Access Token #### Environment and OAuth Base URL - **Pre-production:** [https://oauth-pp.opentable.com](https://oauth-pp.opentable.com/) - **Production:** [https://oauth.opentable.com](https://oauth.opentable.com/) Clients can obtain an access token using the OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow. #### Access Token URI - **URI:** `{{base-url}}/api/v2/oauth/token?grant_type=client_credentials` #### Request Parameters - **grant_type:** OAuth grant type. Should be `client_credentials` ### Submitting Client Credentials Client credentials are submitted in the Authorization header as defined in the OAuth specification. Follow these steps: 1. Concatenate client ID and client secret using a “:” and base64 encode the result. 2. Set the header `Authorization: Basic` **Note:** For OAuth token POST requests, include the Content-Length header even when the request body is empty. Set Content-Length to 0. No update is required for GET requests for OAuth tokens. ## Authorizing Tokens #### Obtain an Access Token - Set the header `Authorization: Basic` #### Send the Request - If the token is valid and not expired, the resource server will return an appropriate response. - If the token is not valid, the resource server responds with the appropriate HTTP status code (e.g., 400, 401, 403, or 405) and an error code. ### Token Renewal Token expiry is included in the response. To get a new token, follow the process of Submitting Client Credentials to obtain a new one. ### Responses - If the access token is present and valid, the resource server will return an appropriate response. - If the access token is missing, HTTP 400 Bad Request is returned. - If the access token is invalid or expired, HTTP 401 Unauthorized is returned.

request.description:
## Overview

This documentation outlines the process for using the Availability Search API to retrieve reservation times and metadata for one restaurant.

## Endpoint

**URL**: `{{base-url}}/v2/availability-metadata/{{rid}}`

**HTTP Method**: GET

### Path Parameter

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `rid` | Integer | A unique, static identifier for each restaurant. Each restaurant will have its own unique ID. |

### Query Parameters

The API does not use query parameters for this endpoint.

**Response Elements**

The API response includes the following elements:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `data` | Object | See `Data` section |

### Data

The `data` object includes:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `environments` | Array of Strings | Values will be either `Outdoor` or `Indoor`. |
| `attributes` | Array of Strings | Values will be either `bar`, `counter`, `highTop`, `outdoor`, or `default`. |
| `dining_areas` | Array of Objects | Detailed information about available dining areas (see `Dining Areas` section). |

### Dining Areas

Each dining area object includes:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Integer | Identifier of the dining area. |
| `name` | String | Name of the dining area. |
| `description` | String | Description of the dining area. |
| `environment` | String | Environment type (e.g., `Indoor`, `Outdoor`). |

item.description:
## _**Search APIs**_

## Introduction

The **Single Search API** provides developers with a streamlined way to retrieve restaurant reservation availability using OpenTable's platform. This API enables partners to perform highly customizable searches based on parameters such as date, time, party size, table type, and more. By leveraging this endpoint, developers can enhance user experiences by seamlessly integrating availability searches into their applications.

## Overview

The **Single Search API** allows you to:

- Search for available reservations at a specific restaurant.
    
- Customize searches with parameters like date, time range, party size, and table type.
    
- Retrieve detailed availability, including dining area information and cancellation policies.
    
- Include special options like experiences and credit-card-secured reservation times.
    

# API Documentation for Availability Search API

## Overview

This documentation outlines the process for using the Availability Search API to retrieve reservation times for one restaurant. It includes the endpoint details, parameters, and examples of requests and responses.

## Endpoint

**Single Search URL**: `{{base-url}}/v2/availability/{{rid}}`

**Method**: GET

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `rid` | Integer | A unique, static identifier for each restaurant. |

### Query Parameters

The API supports the following query parameters to customize the search:

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `start_date_time` | String | The local date and time to begin the search, formatted in ISO 8601. Must align with 15-minute intervals (e.g., 7:00, 7:15). Example: `2020-12-01T19:00`. |
| `forward_minutes` | Integer | The number of minutes forward from the start time to search for availability. Acceptable intervals are up to 720 minutes. |
| `backward_minutes` | Integer | The number of minutes backward from the start time to search for availability. Acceptable intervals are up to 720 minutes. |
| `party_size` | Integer | Specifies the number of diners for the reservation. |
| `require_attributes` | String | Specifies the table type(s) for the search. Possible values: `default`, `hightop`, `bar`, `counter`, `outdoor`. Exclude to default to `default`. Including this parameter ensures specific table types are considered. |
| `include_credit_card_results` | Boolean | When set to `true`, returns availability requiring credit card confirmation. |
| `include_experiences` | Boolean | Optional (defaults to `false`). Returns availability for special dining experiences. |

## Response Elements

The API response includes the following elements:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `rid` | Integer | The unique restaurant identifier. |
| `party_size` | Integer | The number of diners in the reservation request. |
| `times` | Array of Strings | Available reservation times. |
| `times_available` | Array of Objects | Detailed availability for each time. |
| `no_availability_reasons` | Array | Reasons for unavailable times, such as `NoTimesExist`, `BelowMinPartySize`, etc. |
| `href` | String | URL for further details or actions related to the listing. |

### Times Available

Each time slot includes:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `time` | String | Available reservation time. |
| `availability_types` | Array of Objects | Types of availability (e.g., `Standard`, `Experience`). |

### Availability Types

Each availability type provides:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `type` | String | Type of availability. |
| `cancellation_policy` | Object | Details of the cancellation policy. |
| `dining_area` | Array | Information about available dining areas. |
| `experience_cancellation_policies` | Array of Objects | Information about available dining areas. |

### Cancellation Policy

Details about reservation cancellation policies include:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `type` | String | Policy type (`Deposit`, `Hold`). |
| `id` | String | ID of the cancellation policy. See Cancel Policy endpoint for details |
| `amount` | Integer | Amount of the deposit |
| `denominator` | Integer | Used to specify the unit of the amount, typically set to 100 for currency values. |
| `currency` | String | The currency in which the deposit amount is specified, e.g., USD |
| `deposit_type` | String | Type of deposit (PerGuest). |

### Dining Area Attributes

Dining area details include:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Integer | Unique identifier for the dining area. |
| `booking_url` | String | Deep-link to OpenTable make reservation URL. The partner will need to add its referral id to the ref query parameter of this URL. (Use of the booking_url to deep-link to OpenTable is for affiliate partner use only and not for use by restaurant partners) |
| `booking_restref_url` | String | Deep-link to OpenTable make reservation URL for the restaurant group. (Use of this link is restricted to restaurant groups and is not to be used by affiliate partners) |
| `attributes` | Array of Strings | The table type or seating option returned for the specific availability. Return types are, `default`, `hightop`, `bar`, `counter`, `outdoor`. |
| `experience_ids` | Array of Integers | IDs of associated experiences in the dining area (if applicable). |
| `environment` | String | Environment type. Will be either `Indoor` or `Outdoor`). |

### Experience Cancellation Policies

Details about reservation cancellation policies include:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `experience_id` | Integer | ID of associated experience. |
| `type` | String | Type of cancellation policy (e.g., `Hold`, `Prepayment`). |
| `id` | String | Id of the cancellation policy. |

### Common Errors

| API Endpoint | Reason for the Error | Error Message | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **All Endpoints** | Invalid or Expired Token provided | invalid_token | 401 Unauthorized |
| **Single Search** | No Times Exist | “no_availability_reasons”: \[“NoTimesExist”\] There simply are no available times for the requested date, time, and time window of interest. There is no indication of blocked inventory. | 200 OK |
| **Single Search** | Same Day Cut Off | “no_availability_reasons”: \[“SameDayCutoff”\] There is a cutoff time for same-day reservations, and this search request occurs after the cutoff time. | 200 OK |
| **Single Search** | Party Size is above max set by restaurant | “no_availability_reasons”: \[“AboveMaxPartySize\] The requested party size is above the maximum allowed for online reservations. | 200 OK |
| **Single Search** | Party Size is below the minimum set by restaurant | “no_availability_reasons”: \[“BelowMinPartySize\] The requested party size is above the maximum allowed for online reservations. | 200 OK |
| **Single Search** | No Authorization Token provided | Authorization token is required to access this resource | 403 Forbidden |
| **Single Search** | Illegal Party Size | Party size must be from 1 to 20 | 400 Bad Request |
| **Single Search** | No Party Size in request | Required request parameter 'party_size' for method parameter type Integer is present but converted to null | 400 Bad Request |
| **Single Search** | Party Size is not an Integer | party_size should be of type Integer | 400 Bad Request |
| **Single Search** | No Start_Date_Time in request | Required request parameter 'start_date_time' for method parameter type LocalDateTime is not present" | 400 Bad Request |
| **Single Search** | Invalid Date/Time in the request | start_date_time should be of type LocalDateTime | 400 Bad Request |
| **Single Search** | Invalid Forward Minutes | forward_minutes should be of type Integer | 400 Bad Request |
| **Single Search** | Invalid Backward Minutes | backward_minutes should be of type Integer | 400 Bad Request |

item.name: Experiences

request.description:
## Overview

This documentation provides details on the Experiences API for retrieving detailed information about various experiences offered by a restaurant.

---

# Experiences API

## Endpoint

**URL**: `{{base-url}}/v2/experiences/{{rid}}/active`

**HTTP Method**: GET

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `rid` | String | A unique, static identifier for each restaurant. Each restaurant will have its own unique ID. |

### Key Features

- **Experience Details**: Name, description, type, and ID of the experience.
    
- **Add-Ons**: Optional items or services, such as wine upgrades or truffle additions.
    
- **Pricing Information**: Includes price per person and additional charges like gratuity.
    
- **Service Charges**: Information about mandatory fees associated with the experience.
    

## Response Schema

### Experience Data

### Root Response Body

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `data` | Array | An array containing one or more experience/reservation objects. |

### Object: data

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `add_ons` | Object | Details about available add-ons, including groups and limits. See Add-ons Object section. |
| `add_ons_summary` | Object | A high-level summary of the add-ons available for this experience. See Add-ons Summary Object section. |
| `bookable` | Boolean | Indicates if the experience is currently available for booking (e.g., true). |
| `currency` | String | The currency code for the experience price (e.g., USD). |
| `description` | String | A detailed description of the experience. |
| `experience_id` | Number | The unique identifier for the experience. |
| `name` | String | The public-facing name of the experience. |
| `rid` | Number | The unique reservation identifier. |
| `type` | String | The user-friendly type of the experience (e.g., Special menu). |
| `type_enum` | String | The internal enumeration value for the experience type (e.g., PRIX\\_FIXE). |
| `type_id` | Number | The internal ID for the experience type. |
| \`version | Number | The version number of this experience's data structure. |
| `price_info` | Object | Detailed pricing information, including payment requirements, gratuity, and individual price options. See Price Info Object section. (Optional) |
| `experience_images` | Array | A list of image assets associated with the experience (may be empty). |
| `policyInformation` | Object | Container for references to external policy document IDs. See Policy Information Object section. (Optional) |

### **Add-Ons Object (add_ons)**

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `groups` | Array | A list of categorized groups containing individual add-on items. See Add-on Group Object section. |
| `max_per_reservation` | Integer | Maximum number of add-ons per reservation (if `0`, no limit). |

### **Add-Ons Group Object (add_ons.groups\[\]**

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `description` | String | A descriptive label for the add-on group (e.g., Experience Add-ons). |
| `items` | Array | A list of individual add-on options available in this group. See Add-on Item Object section. |
| `name` | String | The name of the add-on group. |

### **Add-on Item Object (add_ons.groups\[\].items\[\])**

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `description` | String | The detailed description of the add-on item (e.g., test). |
| `id` | String | A unique ID for the add-on item (UUID format) |
| `max_per_reservation` | Interger | The maximum quantity of this specific item that can be added to a reservation. |
| `name` | String | The name of the specific add-on item (e.g., Test addon). |
| `price_per_item` | Object | The pricing structure for a single unit of this add-on item. See Price Per Item Object section. |

### **Price Per Item Object (price_per_item)**

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `currency_code` | String | The currency code for the price (e.g., USD). |
| `min_unit_amount` | Integer | The base price of the item in the smallest unit (e.g., cents). |
| `multiplier` | Integer | The factor to convert the unit amount to the standard price (e.g., 100 for cents). |
| `service_charges` | Array | A list of mandatory or optional fees applied to the base price. See Service Charge Object section. (Optional) |

### **Service Charge Object (service_charges\[\])**

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `serviceChargeId` | Integer | Unique identifier for the service charge. |
| `label` | String | Display name for the service charge (e.g., Additional fees). |
| `numerator` | Integer | The numerator used in calculating the percentage/fractional charge. |
| `denominator` | integer | The denominator used in calculating the percentage/fractional charge (e.g., 10000 for basis points). |
| `mandatory` | Boolean | If true, the charge must be applied. |
| `taxable` | Boolean | If true, the charge is subject to tax. |
| `description` | String | A short explanation of the charge (e.g., Cleaning). |

### **Add-ons Summary Object (add_ons_summary)**

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `available` | String | Indicates the availability state of add-ons (e.g., OPTIONAL, NONE). |
| `count` | Integer | The total number of unique add-ons available. |

### **Price Info Object (price_info)**

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `priceType` | String | PER_PARTY OR PER_PERSON , indicates whether the price is charged per party, regardless of the number of people in the group, or if the price is calculated on a per-person basis. |
| `prePaymentRequired` | Boolean | True if prepayment is required , false otherwise |
| `currencyCode` | String | The currency code for the pricing (e.g., USD). |
| `multiplier` | integer | Numerical factor used to convert a decimal value into its integer representation. For example, with a multiplier of 100, a decimal amount of $10.22 would be represented as 1022. |
| `gratuity` | Object | Details about mandatory or recommended gratuity. See Gratuity Object section. (Optional) |
| `prices` | Array | A list of different menu or price options available for the experience. See Individual Price Option Object section. |

### **Gratuity Object (price_info.gratuity)**

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `label` | String | Locale based laebl |
| `numerator` | Integer | Numerator of gratuity, e.g. 350 for 3.50% if denominator is 10,000 |
| `denominator` | integer | The denominator used for percentage calculation (e.g., 10000). |
| `mandatory` | Boolean | If true, the gratuity is required. |
| `taxable` | Boolean | If true, the gratuity is subject to tax. |

### **Policy Information Object (policyInformation)**

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cancellationPolicy` | Object | Reference to the cancellation policy ID. See Policy ID Object section. (Optional) |
| `bookingPolicy` | Object | Reference to the booking policy ID. See Policy ID Object section. (Optional) |
| `tncPolicy` | Object | Reference to the Terms & Conditions policy ID. See Policy ID Object section. (Optional) |

### **Policy ID Object (\*Policy.id)**

Used by `cancellationPolicy`, `bookingPolicy`, and `tncPolicy`.

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | string | The versioned ID referencing the full text of the policy (e.g., 6700aaebd168ff2f0dc1412b:v1) |

---

# Experience Total API

## Overview

The Experience Total API allows you to calculate and retrieve detailed cost information for restaurant booking experiences. This includes costs for add-ons, tips, service fees, and taxes. It supports specifying details such as party size, experience version, and price breakdowns for comprehensive booking insights.

---

## Endpoint

**URL**: `{{base-url}}/v2/booking/{{RID}}/reservations/experiences/total`

**Method**: POST

---

## Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `rid` | String | A unique, static identifier for each restaurant. Each restaurant will have its own unique ID. |

---

## Request Body

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `experience_id` | Integer | The unique identifier for the experience. |
| `version` | Integer | The version number of the experience. |
| `party_size` | Integer | The number of people in the party. |
| `tip_percent_list` | Array of Integers | Percentage of tips to be added. |
| `add_ons` | Array of Objects | Details of additional items added to the experience, including item ID and quantity. |
| `party_size_per_price_type` | Array of Objects | Details of party size per price type, including the ID of the price type and the count of people. |

---

### Add-Ons Array

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `item_id` | String | The unique identifier for the add-on item. |
| `quantity` | Integer | The quantity of the add-on item. |

---

### Party Size Per Price Type Array

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Integer | The ID of the price type. |
| `count` | Integer | The count of people for the corresponding price type. |

---

### Totals Array

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `tip_amount` | Integer | The total tip amount. |
| `tip_percent` | Float | The percentage of the tip. |
| `tip_amount_with_taxes` | Integer | The tip amount, including taxes (if applicable). |
| `service_fee_amount` | Integer | The total service fee amount. |
| `add_on_subtotal_amount_with_taxes` | Integer | The subtotal amount for add-ons, including taxes. |
| `add_on_subtotal` | Integer | The subtotal amount for add-ons. |
| `item_subtotal_amount` | Integer | The subtotal of item prices. |
| `taxes_on_tip_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on tips. |
| `taxes_on_service_fees_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on service fees. |
| `taxes_amount` | Integer | The total taxes amount. |
| `total_amount` | Integer | The total cost, including taxes, service fees, tips, and add-ons. |
| `taxes_on_item_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on items. |
| `taxes_on_add_ons_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on add-ons. |
| `item_subtotal_amount_without_taxes` | Integer | The subtotal of item amounts, excluding taxes. |
| `is_tip_mandatory` | Boolean | Indicates if a tip is mandatory. |
| `subtotals_per_price_type` | Array | Subtotals for each price type applied to the booking (detailed below). |
| `taxes` | Array | Tax details applied to the booking (detailed below). |

---

### Subtotals Per Price Type Array

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Integer | The unique identifier of the price type. |
| `count` | Integer | The count of people for the corresponding price type. |
| `price` | Integer | The unit price for this price type. |
| `subtotal` | Integer | The subtotal for this price type. |
| `price_all_inclusive` | Boolean | Indicates whether the price includes all taxes and fees. |
| `subtotal_with_tax_and_fees` | Integer | The subtotal including taxes and fees. |

---

### Taxes Array

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `tax_id` | Integer | The unique identifier for the tax. |
| `tax_percent` | Float | The percentage of the tax. |
| `tax_on_item_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on item amounts. |
| `tax_on_tip_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on tip amounts. |
| `tax_on_service_fees_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on service fees. |
| `tax_subtotals_per_price_type` | Array | Subtotals of taxes per price type (detailed below). |
| `tax_subtotal` | Integer | The total subtotal of taxes. |
| `tax_total` | Integer | The total taxes. |

---

### Tax Subtotals Per Price Type Array

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Integer | The unique identifier of the price type. |
| `tax_percent` | Float | The percentage of the tax for this price type. |
| `tax_on_item_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on items for this price type. |
| `tax_on_service_fees_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on service fees for this price type. |
| `tax_subtotal` | Integer | The subtotal of taxes for this price type. |

---

## Response

### Main Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Integer | Unique identifier for the booking. |
| `version` | Integer | Version of the payload format. |
| `add_ons` | Array | List of add-ons associated with the booking (see Add-Ons Array). |
| `currency` | String | The currency used for the transaction (e.g., "USD"). |
| `currency_denominator` | Integer | The denominator used for currency (e.g., 100 for cents). |
| `totals` | Array | Total costs associated with the booking (see Totals Array). |
| `has_mandatory_tip` | Boolean | Indicates whether a tip is mandatory for the booking. |
| `is_tip_taxable` | Boolean | Indicates whether the tip is taxable. |
| `prices` | Array | Pricing details for different price types applied to the booking (see Prices Array). |
| `restaurant_id` | Integer | The unique identifier of the restaurant associated with the booking. |
| `booking_type` | String | Type of booking (e.g., "Table"). |
| `ticketed` | Boolean | Indicates whether the booking has been ticketed. |
| `service_fees` | Array | List of global service fees applied to the entire booking (see Service Fees Array). |

---

### Add-Ons Array

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `item_id` | String | The unique identifier for the add-on item. |
| `quantity` | Integer | The quantity of the add-on item. |
| `line_total` | Integer | The total cost for the add-on item. |
| `price` | Integer | The price per unit of the add-on item. |
| `name` | String | The name of the add-on item. |
| `line_subtotal_with_taxes` | Integer | The subtotal cost for the add-on item including taxes. |
| `line_subtotal_with_taxes_and_fees` | Integer | The subtotal cost for the add-on item including taxes and fees. |
| `service_fees` | Array | List of service fees associated with the add-on (see Service Fees Array). |
| `taxes` | Array | List of taxes applied to the add-on (see Taxes Array). |

---

### Service Fees Array

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `service_fee_percent` | Float | The percentage of the service fee. |
| `service_fee_amount` | Integer | The amount of the service fee. |
| `label` | String | The label for the service fee (e.g., "Service charge"). |
| `mandatory` | Boolean | Indicates whether the service fee is mandatory. |
| `taxable` | Boolean | Indicates whether the service fee is taxable. |
| `description` | String | A description of the service fee. |
| `tax_on_service_fee_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on the service fee. |

---

### Taxes Array

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `tax_id` | Integer | The unique identifier for the tax. |
| `label` | String | The label for the tax. |
| `tax_percent` | Float | The percentage of the tax. |
| `tax_on_item_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on the item. |
| `tax_on_tip_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on the tip. |
| `tax_on_service_fees_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on the service fees. |
| `tax_subtotal` | Integer | The subtotal tax amount. |
| `tax_total` | Integer | The total tax amount. |
| `tax_subtotals_per_price_type` | Array | Subtotals of taxes per price type (see Tax Subtotals Per Price Type Array). |

---

### Tax Subtotals Per Price Type Array

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Integer | The unique identifier of the price type. |
| `tax_percent` | Float | The percentage of the tax for this price type. |
| `tax_on_item_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on items for this price type. |
| `tax_on_service_fees_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on service fees for this price type. |
| `tax_subtotal` | Integer | The subtotal of taxes for this price type. |

---

### Totals Array

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `tip_amount` | Integer | The total tip amount. |
| `tip_percent` | Float | The percentage of the tip. |
| `tip_amount_with_taxes` | Integer | The tip amount, including taxes (if applicable). |
| `service_fee_amount` | Integer | The total service fee amount. |
| `add_on_subtotal_amount_with_taxes` | Integer | The subtotal amount for add-ons, including taxes. |
| `add_on_subtotal` | Integer | The subtotal amount for add-ons. |
| `item_subtotal_amount` | Integer | The subtotal of item prices. |
| `taxes_on_tip_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on tips. |
| `taxes_on_service_fees_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on service fees. |
| `taxes_amount` | Integer | The total taxes amount. |
| `total_amount` | Integer | The total cost, including taxes, service fees, tips, and add-ons. |
| `taxes_on_item_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on items. |
| `taxes_on_add_ons_amount` | Integer | The tax amount on add-ons. |
| `item_subtotal_amount_without_taxes` | Integer | The subtotal of item amounts, excluding taxes. |
| `is_tip_mandatory` | Boolean | Indicates if a tip is mandatory. |
| `subtotals_per_price_type` | Array | Subtotals for each price type applied to the booking (see Subtotals Per Price Type Array). |
| `taxes` | Array | Tax details applied to the booking (see Taxes Array). |

---

### Subtotals Per Price Type Array

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Integer | The unique identifier of the price type. |
| `count` | Integer | The count of people for the corresponding price type. |
| `price` | Integer | The unit price for this price type. |
| `subtotal` | Integer | The subtotal for this price type. |
| `price_all_inclusive` | Boolean | Indicates whether the price includes all taxes and fees. |
| `subtotal_with_tax_and_fees` | Integer | The subtotal including taxes and fees. |

item.description: # **Description** **OVERVIEW** Experiences allow restaurants to use their availability for special events. From happy hour to winery tours, $1 oysters to exquisite 12-course tasting menus and more. When restaurants offer Experiences on OpenTable, the Consumer API v2 will allow you to book, confirm and even collect deposits. Availability for the experiences are real-time and reservations are confirmed in the system once the reservation is completed. You can choose to show only standard availability or also include experiences by using the `include_experiences` quey parameter. **HOW WILL THIS IMPACT RESTAURANTS?** While these APIs are optional, restaurants that offer experiences will have inventory dedicated to the experiences. Here is how it looks on OpenTable.com for a restaurant with an experience and standard availability.

item.name: Slot Locks

request.description:
## Overview

The Slot Lock API provides the ability to create, manage, and retrieve reservations for a restaurant. This documentation outlines the payload structure, attributes, and their descriptions, along with sample requests and responses for seamless integration.

---

## Request Details

### Endpoint

**URL:** `{{base-url}}/v2/booking/{{rid}}/slot_locks`

**Method: POST**

### Headers

| **Header** | **Value** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Specifies that the request body contains JSON data. |
| `Authorization` | `Bearer [AccessToken]` | A valid access token for authenticating the request. |

---

## Request Body

### Attributes

| **Attribute** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `party_size` | integer | Number of people in the reservation party. _Required_ |
| `date_time` | string | Date and time of the reservation in ISO 8601 format. _Required_ |
| `reservation_attribute` | string | Type or attribute of the reservation (e.g., `default`). |
| `experience` | object | Details about the dining experience. _Optional, only required if experience will be booked._ |
| `dining_area_id` | integer | Identifier for the dining area. _Optional, only required if specific dining area will be booked._ |
| `environment` | string | Type of environment (e.g., `Indoor`, `Outdoor`). _Optional, only required if specific environment will be booked._ |

### Experience Object Attributes

_Note: This Data will be gathered from the GET Experiences API response. All attributes are required when booking an experience._

| **Attribute** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Unique identifier for the experience. |
| `version` | integer | Version of the experience. |
| `party_size_per_price_type` | array | Array detailing the party size per price type. _Optional, applicable only if returned in GET Experiences API_ |
| `add_ons` | array | Array of add-on items for the experience. _Optional, applicable only if returned in GET Experiences API_ |

### Party_Size_Per_Price_Type Array Object Attributes

_Note: This Data will be gathered from the GET Experiences API response. Optional, applicable only if returned in GET Experiences API_

| **Attribute** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Unique identifier for the price type. |
| `count` | integer | Count of items for the price type. |

### Add_Ons Array Object Attributes

_Note: This Data will be gathered from the GET Experiences API response. Optional, applicable only if returned in GET Experiences API_

| **Attribute** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `item_id` | string | Unique identifier for the add-on item. |
| `quantity` | integer | Quantity of the add-on item. |

---

---

## Notes

- Ensure the `restaurant_id`, `party_size`, `date_time`, and `experience` attributes are accurate for successful reservation creation.
    

## Response

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| expires_at | string | The expiration date and time of the slot lock in ISO 8601 format. The slot lock expires within 5 minutes. |
| reservation_token | string | A unique token required for the subsequent reservation creation request. |

### Notes

- The `expires_at` timestamp indicates when the slot lock expires. Ensure the reservation is completed before this time.
    
- The `reservation_token` is required for the reservation creation request and ensures the slot remains locked during the reservation process.
    
- If the `expires_at` time is reached without completing the reservation, the slot will be released.
    
- Tokens should be treated securely and not exposed to unauthorized users.

# Delete Slot Lock API Documentation

## Overview

The Delete Slot Lock API allows external partners to remove a reservation lock (slot lock) for a specific availability at a restaurant. By unlocking the slot, it becomes available for other customers to book.

---

## Endpoint

**URL:** `{{base-url}}/v2/booking/{{rid}}/slot_locks/{{reservation_token}}`

**Method:** DELETE

---

## Request Details

## Path Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `rid` | integer | Unique identifier for the restaurant. |
| `reservation_token` | string | The reservation token provided by the booking API when a slot is locked. |

### Query Parameters

The API does not use query parameters for this endpoint.

---

## Response Details

### Error Responses

| **Status Code** | **Response Body** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `400` | `{ "errors": [ { "message": "reservation_token or restaurant id is invalid" } ], "requestid": "" }` | The `reservation_token` or `rid` is invalid. |
| `500` | `{ "errors": [ { "message": "Unexpected http status code received: GatewayTimeOut" } ] }` | An unexpected error occurred on the server. |

---

---

## Notes

- Ensure the `reservation_token` and `rid` are accurate to avoid errors.
    
- The reservation token is provided by the booking API when a slot is locked.

item.description: # **Description** **OVERVIEW** Slot Locks allow for the inventory that has been chosen by the booker to be locked for a total of 5 minutes. This allows that no other user can book the reservation while the initial booker completes their transaction. If a booking is attempted after the slot lock expiry, the booking can be re-tried, but availability is not guareenteed. Availability has already been verifies via the APIs, this step will block the availability for the diner and allow for booking. It is important to delete unused slot locks as it may block other bookers from accessing availability during the slot lock window. The five minute window is hard coded and cannot be edited. **HOW WILL THIS IMPACT RESTAURANTS?** Restaurant availability will be blocked for that specific user for the allotment of the slot lock window.

item.name: Make Reservation

request.description:
## Endpoint

**URL:** {{base-url}}`/v2/booking/{{rid}}/reservations`

**Method:** POST

This endpoint creates a new reservation for a restaurant. It expects a JSON payload containing all the required information for the reservation.

---

## Request Body

The request payload consists of the following fields:

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `reservation_token` | String | A unique token identifying the temporary hold on a table from a previous call. |
| `first_name` | String | The first name of the guest making the reservation. |
| `last_name` | String | The last name of the guest making the reservation. |
| `email_address` | String | The email address of the guest. |
| `phone` | Object | The guest's phone number (see **Phone Object**). |
| `reservation_attribute` | String | The seating option (default, hightop, bar, counter, outdoor). |
| `special_request` | String | Any special requests for the reservation. (75 character max) |
| `credit_card` | Object (optional) | Credit card details for the reservation (see **Credit Card Object**). |
| `login_name` | String (optional) | Used for concierge/referral details. |
| `restaurant_email_marketing_opt_in` | Boolean or null (optional) | If true, opts the guest into email marketing by the restaurant. |
| `opentable_marketing_opt_in` | Boolean or null (optional) | If true, opts the guest into email marketing from OpenTable. Setting as null doesn't change opt-in / opt-out status |
| `sms_notifications_opt_in` | Boolean (optional) | If true, the guest opts in to receive SMS notifications. |
| `dining_area_id` | Integer (optional) | The unique identifier for the dining area. _Optional, only required if specific dining area will be booked._ |
| `environment` | String (optional) | The environment for the dining experience (e.g., Indoor, Outdoor). _Optional, only required if specific environment will be booked._ |
| `experience` | Object (optional) | Details about the dining experience. _Optional, only required if experience will be booked._ (see **Experience Object**). |

---

### Phone Object

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `number` | String | The phone number of the guest. |
| `country_code` | String | The country code for the number. |
| `phone_type` | String | The type of phone (e.g., Mobile). |

---

### Credit Card Object

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `token` | String | A token representing the credit card. |
| `last4` | String | The last four digits of the credit card. |

---

### Experience Object

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Integer | The unique identifier of the experience. |
| `version` | Integer | The version of the experience. |
| `party_size_per_price_type` | Array of Objects | Party sizes for each price type (see **Party Size Object**). |
| `add_ons` | Array of Objects | List of add-ons selected for the reservation (see **Add-Ons Object**). |

---

### Party Size Per Price Type Object

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Integer | The unique identifier of the party size. |
| `count` | Integer | The number of guests for this price type. |

---

### Add-Ons Object

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `item_id` | String | The unique identifier for the add-on item. |
| `quantity` | Integer | The quantity of the add-on item selected. |

---

## Response

Upon successful creation of a reservation, the API returns the reservation details along with a unique reservation ID and confirmation details.

### Response Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `message` | string | Booking policy details for the reservation. Same as the booking policy message for the reservation. |
| `confirmation_number` | integer | Unique identifier for the confirmed reservation. |
| `offer_confirmation_number` | integer | Unique identifier for any special offer linked to the reservation. |
| `date_time` | string | Date and time of the reservation in ISO 8601 format. |
| `party_size` | integer | Number of guests included in the reservation. |
| notes | string | Special requests or notes provided during the reservation. |
| `manage_reservation_url` | string | URL to manage the confirmed reservation online. |
| `cancel_cutoff_date_utc` | string | Timestamp of last possible time when the record can be cancelled (UTC time). |

### Notes

- The `message` provides important booking policy information such as grace periods and contact instructions.
    
- The `manage_reservation_url` can be shared with guests to modify or cancel the reservation if needed.
    
- The `confirmation_number` and `offer_confirmation_number` serve as unique identifiers for tracking purposes.
    
- Ensure the provided `date_time` and `party_size` align with the guest’s booking details.
    

### Common Errors

| API Endpoint | Reason for the Error | Error Message | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **All Endpoints** | Invalid or Expired Token provided | invalid_token | 401 Unauthorized |
| **Slot Lock** | No available partysize for the time submitted | SlotNotAvailableError - Failed to obtain a valid slot lock ID | 404 Not Found |
| **Slot Lock** | Party Size missing | party_size should not be null | 400 Bad Request |
| **Slot Lock** | Date_Time missing | date_time should not be null | 400 Bad Request |
| **Slot Lock** | Restaurant ID missing | restaurant_id should not be null | 400 Bad Request |
| **Slot Lock** | Unsupported partysize entered (1-20 is required) | SlotNotAvailableError - Failed to obtain a valid slot lock ID | 404 Not Found |
| **Slot Lock** | Invalid Restaurant ID | MissingResource - Resource not found Restaurant, ID = 140608123 | 400 Bad Request |
| **Slot Lock** | Failed to obtain a valid Slot Lock | SlotNotAvailableError - Failed to obtain a valid slot lock ID | 404 Not Found |
| **Make Reservation** | Restaurant ID missing | restaurant_id should not be null | 400 Bad Request |
| **Make Reservation** | Reservation Token missing | reservation_token is required if slotlock flow is used. date_time and party_size is required if slotlock flow is _not_ used. | 400 Bad Request |
| **Make Reservation** | First Name missing | first_name should not be empty | 400 Bad Request |
| **Make Reservation** | Last Name missing | last_name should not be empty | 400 Bad Request |
| **Make Reservation** | Email address missing | email_address should not be empty | 400 Bad Request |
| **Make Reservation** | Phone Number missing | ValidationError - PhoneRequired: phone Not Provided, country US, type Mobile | 400 Bad Request |
| **Make Reservation** | Phone Number Country Code missing | ValidationError - NoCountryID: phone 1113332222, country Not Provided, type Mobile | 400 Bad Request |
| **Make Reservation** | Invalid/Expired Reservation Token or reservation slot was consumed by restaurant directly | reservation_token is invalid | 400 Bad Request |
| **Make Reservation** | Invalid/Inapplicable Reservation Token | SlotStatusError - NoTimesReasons: NotFarEnoughInAdvance | 400 Bad Request |
| **Make Reservation** | Invalid Restaurant ID | reservation_token is invalid | 400 Bad Request |
| **Make Reservation** | Already a reservation for that diner within a 2 +/- hour window | DinerHasOverlappingReservations - Request violated business rule: DinerHasOverlappingReservations | 409 Conflict |
| **Make Reservation** | Email address invalid | \[Email\] Email address is not from a known domain ‘opentable.con’ | 400 Bad Request |
| **Make Reservation** | Phone number too long | ValidationError - IllegalPhoneNumberLength: phone 11122233333333333, country US, type Mobile | 400 Bad Request |
| **Make Reservation** | Credit Card required for reservation | SlotStatusError - CreditCardRequired | 409 Conflict |
| **Make Reservation** | Inactive Restaurant | MissingResource - Resource not found Restaurant, ID = 214390 | 404 Not Found |
| **Make Reservation** | Restaurant is having connectivity issues | ReservationTransactionError - Restaurant not in Active state | 409 Conflict |
| **Make Reservation** | Restaurant does not accept online reservations | SlotStatusError - NoTimesReasons: NotActive | 409 Conflict |
| **Make Reservation** | Error with the OpenTable Partner service and Admin Cache needs to be refreshed | GeneralError - Failed to calculate points: OTAPI: Partner.Load() - Partner ID not found: 5342 | 409 Conflict |
| **Make Reservation** | The email address and person associated to the diner making the reservation has been deactivated by OpenTable. | UserFraudStatusError - User with GPID=XXXXXXXXXX is not Active | 409 Conflict |
| **Cancel Reservation** | Reservation has already passed and no longer can be canceled | MissingResource - Reservation details \[RID = 214390, CONF_NUM = 53\] | 404 Not Found |
| **Cancel Reservation** | Reservation has a credit card on file and the deadline to cancel online has already passed. | CreditCardCutoff - CreditCardCutoff | 409 Conflict |
| **Cancel Reservation** | Reservation time has passed and you can not cancel the reservation. | SlotStatusError - InThePast | 409 Conflict |
| **Modify Reservation** | Attempt to modify a reservation to a party size higher that the restaurant allows | SlotStatusError - NoTimesReasons: AboveMaxPartySize” | 409 Conflict |
| **Experiences** | CreditCardTokenSCARequired | SCA Required | 400 Bad Request |
| **Experiences** | Required Long parameter 'rid' is not present | If the RID Parameter is not provided or invalid | 400 Bad Request |
| **Experiences** | Invalid restaurant ID: -1 | If the RID Parameter is not provided or invalid | 400 Bad Request |
