{ "message": "We have a 5 minute grace period. Please call us if you are running later than 5 minutes after your reservation time.

We may contact you about this reservation, so please ensure your email and phone number are up to date.

Your table will be reserved for 1 hour 30 minutes for parties of up to 2; 2 hours for parties of up to 4; 2 hours 30 minutes for parties of up to 6; and 3 hours for parties of 7+.", "confirmation_number": 1751, "offer_confirmation_number": 0, "date_time": "2025-10-13T16:00", "party_size": 2, "notes": "This is my special request", "manage_reservation_url": "https://www.opentable.com/book/view?rid=1038007&confnumber=1751&token=01EMM9tRYsZ5LWf59HhG_iCHIzHSs1Spu-9JvrwKx0nzI1" }

item.description: # **Description** **OVERVIEW** This will book and confirm the reservation. It confirms the slot lock and must be made within 5 minutes of slot lock (expires_at slot lock parameter). **Requirements** 1. Request body must be encoded in UTF-8 format. StringEntity encodes as ISO-8859-1 as default if you do not specify the encoding format. ISO-8859-1 is not the supported format for JSON. 2. Responses are compressed using gzip and added a “Content-Encoding: gzip” header in the response to indicate that the response body has been compressed. Supporting “Content-Encoding” allows servers to compress responses, saving bandwidth. This will improve performance, especially on slower networks. Modern web browsers and HTTP clients all support common compression formats like gzip and deflate. 3. Optional request header "Accept-Language: \[Locale\]" (default is en-US) for email communications sent in alternate language & locale. **References:** - RFC | [https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc4627.txt](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc4627.txt) - Wikipedia | [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JSON](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JSON) “JSON exchange in an open ecosystem must be encoded in UTF-8” **Supported Accept Language values:** | **Locale Code** | **Language - Region** | | --- | --- | | `en-US` | English - United States | | `ja-JP` | Japanese - Japan | | `de-DE` | German - Germany | | `fr-FR` | French - France | | `es-ES` | Spanish - Spain | | `es-MX` | Spanish - Mexico | | `zh-TW` | Chinese - Taiwan | | `nl-NL` | Dutch - The Netherlands | | `en-AU` | English - Australia | | `en-CA` | English - Canada | | `en-IE` | English - Ireland | | `en-GB` | English - United Kingdom | | `fr-CA` | French - Canada | | `it-IT` | Italian - Italy | | `pt-BR` | Portuguese - Brazil | | `pt-PT` | Portuguese - Portugal | **HOW WILL THIS IMPACT RESTAURANTS?** With this API, restaurants can receive online reservations via the API. Reservations will automatically show in the reservation list and any applicable deposit, experience or booking policy can also be referenced. ## Credit Card Processing As part of the **Make Reservation** request, the below process can be followed to vault a credit card with Stripe, our partner used for all credit card related transactions. Additional Credit_Card Request Body Fields (added to the **make reservation** request) | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | `credit_card:token` | string | The “id” returned from vaulting the credit card using the Stripe API “create a card token” POST, [Stripe API Documentation](https://stripe.com/docs/api/tokens/create_card). | | `credit_card:last4` | string | The “last4” returned from vaulting the credit card using the Stripe API “create a card token” POST, [Stripe API Documentation](https://stripe.com/docs/api/tokens/create_card). | **Stripe API details and tokenization** In order to generate the required **credit_card** parameters for the **make reservation** request, you will need to use the process detailed by Stripe in their API documentation, [https://stripe.com/docs/api/tokens/create_card](https://stripe.com/docs/api/tokens/create_card), to generate the Stripe token “id” and “last4”. We have included the basic requirements, however these are subject to change, are not inclusive of what is supported, and are not supported directly by OpenTable. Please review the Stripe documentation for further details and information. As part of the Stripe API call, you will set the content-type as x-www-form-urlencoded and the authorization is the bearer token provided by OpenTable. For this you will need the OpenTable Stripe Publishable Key. Please contact the API team at OpenTable and they will securely supply the pre-production and production Stripe keys. | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | `card\[number\]` | string | The credit card number this token will represent, without any separators. | | `card\[exp_month\]` | number | Two-digit number representing the card’s expiration month. | | `card\[exp_year\]` | number | Two- or four-digit number representing the card’s expiration year. | | `card\[cvc\]` | number | Card security code. | ``` json Sample Request Body curl -X POST '[https://platform.otqa.com/booking/reservations'](https://platform.otqa.com/booking/reservations') \-H 'authorization: Bearer \[Access Token\]' \-H 'cache-control: no-cache' \-H 'content-type: application/json' \-d ' Sample Response: 200 OK { "restaurant_id": 334879, "reservation_token": "\[Reservation Token from Lock\]", "first_name": "John", "last_name": "Smith", "email_address": "[john.smith@sample-email.com](https://mailto:john.smith@sample-email.com)", "phone": { "number": "4153444200", "country_code": "US", "phone_type": "Mobile" }, "reservation_attribute": "outdoor", "special_request": "Please seat us at a round table.", "credit_card": { "token": "tok_XXXXXXXXX", "last4": "4242" }, "restaurant_email_marketing_opt_in": false, "is_third_party": false } ``` The Stripe Request will generate a response with many details, however for OpenTable booking purposes, only “**id**” and “**last4**” are required for the basic make reservation request. Refer to the Stripe documentation with any other questions around the full response payload. **Stripe and SCA (Strong Customer Authentication)** In some instances, when a credit card is tokenized and vaulted with Stripe, there is an additional authentication step required from the diner, known as Strong Customer Authentication, or **SCA**. To read more about Stripe and SCA, you can view their documentation here, [https://stripe.com/docs/strong-customer-authentication](https://stripe.com/docs/strong-customer-authentication). In instances of required SCA, when the partner submits the **make reservation** request, if Stripe responds to the OpenTable credit card vaulting that SCA is needed, OpenTable will return a 424 Failed Dependency error message (example provided). When SCA is required, the partner must present a UI element to the diner to approve the credit card transaction (see below Stripe APIs). **OpenTable Requirements for SCA verification for credit card holds** In the 424 Failed Dependency error response, OpenTable will return the following pieces of information. You will need to store this for your follow-up API calls to Stripe and your new **make reservation** request to OpenTable. | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | `stripeToken` | string | The original token from the Stripe credit card tokenization that was submitted in the make reservation request. | | `clientSecret` | string | Used to call the Stripe SCA API endpoints to retrieve the `setup_intent` for the follow-up make reservation request to OpenTable. | | `paymentMethod` | string | Used when making the follow-up make reservation request to OpenTable. | | `connectedAccountPk` | string | This will be used at the authorization header bearer token for the Stripe APIs (below) in lieu of the OpenTable Stripe authorization bearer token (and the previously used OpenTable Stripe authorization header bearer will not be used). | Once you present the UI element for the diner to approve the transaction, call the Stripe APIs (below), Stripe will return the **setup_intent**. You will use this in the follow-up modified **make reservation** request to OpenTable. ``` json OpenTable response for SCA Required 424 error for credit card holds: { "errors": [ { "message": "CreditCardTokenSCARequired", "details": { "stripeToken": "tok_Fp8cLrD3NSkDRz", "clientSecret": "seti_1FJULqCpqyzgzbXS1NOKWBib_secret_Fp8dlPMpxYWak99cjjth3UM0GKvFPWU", "paymentMethod": "pm_1FJULqCpqyzgzbXSWawUCC4n", "connectedAccountPk": "pk_test_GvfiPXSbQ3eo6R4eKo8NL5Pk00qeYMWQuz" } } ], "requestid": "3c2e3a10-38a6-40d8-bffe-71ede613b3a9" } ``` **OpenTable Make Reservation SCA parameters for credit card holds** After allowing the diner to accept the credit card transaction and completing the Stripe SCA APIs, using the original stripe **token**, with the **last4**, and the **paymentMethod** returned from the 424 Failed Dependency error message, you will use the **setupIntent** from the SCA response and add those to the **make reservation** body (in the same method as you added the original **credit_card** body). **Stripe APIs to use for SCA verification** The below are Stripe resources used to call Stripe and get back the requirements to submit back to OpenTable for the **make reservation** request. - Stripe JavaScript API | [https://stripe.com/docs/js/setup_intents/confirm_card_setup](https://stripe.com/docs/js/setup_intents/confirm_card_setup) - Stripe iOS SDK | [https://stripe.dev/stripe-ios/docs/index.html](https://stripe.dev/stripe-ios/docs/index.html) **OpenTable Requirements for SCA verification for prepaid experiences** In the 424 Failed Dependency error response, OpenTable will return the following pieces of information. You will need to store this for your follow-up API calls to Stripe and your new **make reservation** request to OpenTable. | **Field** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | `stripeToken` | string | The original token from the Stripe credit card tokenization that was submitted in the make reservation request. | | `clientSecret` | string | Used to call the Stripe SCA API endpoints to retrieve the `payment_intent` for the follow-up make reservation request to OpenTable. | | `paymentMethod` | string | Used when making the follow-up make reservation request to OpenTable. | | `connectedAccountPk` | string | This will be used as the authorization header bearer token for the Stripe APIs instead of the OpenTable Stripe authorization bearer token. | Once you present the UI element for the diner to approve the transacation, call the Stripe APIs (below), Stripe will return the **payment_intent**. You will use this in the follow-up modified **make reservation** request to OpenTable. ``` json OpenTable response for SCA Required 424 error for prepaid experiences: { "errors": [ { "message": "CreditCardTokenSCARequired - SCAForPayment", "details": { "stripeToken": "tok_0Mf90jjrulGLaJAMMKERcecp", "clientSecret": "pi_3Mf94PBf02EU9tgq0MEhjyCw_secret_LYhAPaXbcCphUGOQKgzjiMalN", "paymentMethod": "pm_1Mf94PBf02EU9tgqSbtMncPh", "connectedAccountPk": "pk_test_51HHB6EBf02EU9tgqyXTYwje0cm3NrKU1JaahO4DKBLoHt4jix8X80b57UfFzToCBjc4GRXWiKjdFsYTrQNgLC3oS007wF7q7GZ" } } ], "requestid": "ec416096-cf30-42ce-a517-9d67fb526a23" } ``` **OpenTable Make Reservation SCA parameters for prepaid experiences** After allowing the diner to accept the credit card transaction and completing the Stripe SCA APIs, using the original stripe **token**, with the **last4**, and the **paymentMethod** returned from the 424 Failed Dependency error message, you will use the **paymentIntent** from the SCA response and add those to the **make reservation** body (in the same method as you added the original **credit_card** body). **Stripe APIs to use for SCA verification for prepaid experiences** The below are Stripe resources used to call Stripe and get back the requirements to submit back to OpenTable for the **make reservation** request. - Stripe JavaScript API | [https://docs.stripe.com/js/payment_intents/confirm_card_payment](https://docs.stripe.com/js/payment_intents/confirm_card_payment) - Stripe iOS SDK | [https://stripe.dev/stripe-ios/docs/index.html](https://stripe.dev/stripe-ios/docs/index.html)

item.name: Policies

request.description:
## Booking Policies API Documentation

### Endpoint

`{{base-url}}/v2/booking-policies/{{rid}}/{YEAR-MONTH-DAY}/{TIME}/{PartySize}`

### Path Parameters

- **rid (restaurant id)**: A static identifier for each restaurant.
    
- **date (YEAR-MONTH-DAY)**: Specifies the booking date. Example: `2024-12-01`.
    
- **time (TIME)**: Specifies the time of the reservation. Must be in 15-minute intervals (`7:00`, `7:15`, `7:30`, `7:45`).
    
- **PartySize**: Specifies the number of guests for the booking policy query.
    

### Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `dinerContext` | string | Must be set to `details` only. |

### Response Elements

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Policies` | array | Contains available policies |

### Policies Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Message` | string | Describes any available messages. |
| Type | string | Usually `General`. Represents the policy type. |
| `Language` | array | Specifies the language details of the policy. |

### Language Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Code | string | Language code. |
| Region | string | Region associated with language. |
| IETF | string | IETF language code format. |

## Cancellation Policies API Documentation

### Endpoint

**URL:** `{{base-url}}/v2/cancellation-policies/{rid}/{cancelationid}`

**Method:** GET

### Description

The cancellation policy endpoint allows you to retrieve the cancellation policy for a reservation based on the restaurant ID and cancellation ID. The `cancelationid` is obtained from the response of the GET Availability call.

### Sample Request

``` bash
curl --location '{{base-url}}/v2/cancellation-policies/{rid}/{cancelationid}' \
--header 'Authorization: bearer '

 ```

### Response

``` json
{
    "partySize": 5,
    "policyType": "Deposit",
    "depositDetails": {
        "amount": 1500,
        "currency": "USD",
        "denominator": 100,
        "type": "PerGuest"
    },
    "cutOff": {
        "cutoffType": "DaysBefore",
        "daysBeforeCutoff": 2
    }
}

 ```

### Response Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `partySize` | integer | The number of guests for the reservation. |
| \`policyType\` | string | Type of cancellation policy. |
| `depositDetails` | object | Details of the deposit policy. |
| `depositDetails.amount` | integer | Deposit amount in minor currency units. |
| `depositDetails.currency` | string | Currency of the deposit amount. |
| `depositDetails.denominator` | integer | Conversion factor for minor units. |
| `depositDetails.type` | string | Specifies if the deposit is per guest. |
| `cutOff` | object | Cutoff policy details. |
| `cutOff.cutoffType` | string | Type of cutoff applied. |
| `cutOff.daysBeforeCutoff` | integer | Number of days before cutoff. |

### Notes

- Ensure you use a valid `cancelationid` retrieved from the Make Reservation call.
    
- The cutoff policy specifies the period within which cancellations are allowed.
    
- The deposit amount is specified in minor currency units (e.g., cents for USD).

item.description: **OVERVIEW** Booking Policies allow the restaurants to expose any specific details they would like the diner to know when they book the reservation. These details should be exposed when the diner is confirming the reservation so that they understand the terms of the reservations. These can be as simple as "We offer valet parking..." to "Deposit of $50.00 will be collected if there is a no show". A few notes: - The custom details added by restaurants will have a maximum of 500 characters. - Booking and Cancellation Policies can vary by date and party size. - If no policies are available, the APIs will return an empty response. Below is an example of how these details are exposed in OpenTable.com during the booking path.

item.name: Modify or Cancel Reservations

request.description:
## Modify Reservation API Documentation

### Endpoint

**URL:**`{{base-url}}/v2/booking/{{rid}}reservations/{id}`  
**Method:** PUT

- The `id` parameter is a unique combination of the `Restaurant_ID` and `Confirmation_Number` provided in the request URL, formatted as `{rid}-{confirmation_number}`.
    
- **Prior Actions:**
    
    - Perform a search request to verify availability for new time/date (Availability API)
        
    - Provision/lock request to lock the new desired time. (Slot Lock API)
        
    - If a credit card is required for the new reservation, please refer to the Make Reservation Credit Card details (Credit Card Processing Details)
        

## Request Body

The request payload consists of the following fields:

## Reservation Details Table

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `party_size` | integer | Number of people in the reservation party. Required |
| `date_time` | string | Date and time of the reservation in ISO 8601 format. Required |
| `reservation_token` | string | A unique token identifying the temporary hold on a table from a previous call. Required |
| `reservation_attribute` | string | The seating option (default, hightop, bar, counter, outdoor). Required if using |
| `special_request` | string | Any special requests for the reservation. _Optional_ |
| `credit_card` | object | Credit card details for the reservation (see Credit Card Object). _Optional_ |
| `login_name` | string | Used for concierge/referral details. _Optional_ |
| `restaurant_email_marketing_opt_in` | boolean | If true, opts the guest into email marketing by the restaurant. _Optional_ |
| `sms_notifications_opt_in` | boolean | If true, the guest opts in to receive SMS notifications. _Optional_ |
| `dining_area_id` | integer | The unique identifier for the dining area. _Optional_ |
| `environment` | string | The environment for the dining experience (e.g., Indoor, Outdoor). _Optional_ |
| `experience` | object | Experience details for the reservation (see Experience Object). _Optional_ |

## Credit Card Object

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `token` | String | A token representing the credit card. |
| `last4` | String | The last four digits of the credit card. |

---

### Experience Object

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Integer | The unique identifier of the experience. |
| `version` | Integer | The version of the experience. |
| `party_size_per_price_type` | Array of Objects | Party sizes for each price type (see **Party Size Object**). |
| `add_ons` | Array of Objects | List of add-ons selected for the reservation (see **Add-Ons Object**). |

---

### Party Size Per Price Type Object

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Integer | The unique identifier of the party size. |
| `count` | Integer | The number of guests for this price type. |

---

### Add-Ons Object

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `item_id` | String | The unique identifier for the add-on item. |
| `quantity` | Integer | The quantity of the add-on item selected. |

---

## Response

Upon successful creation of a reservation, the API returns the reservation details along with a unique reservation ID and confirmation details.

### Response Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `message` | string | Booking policy details for the reservation. Same as the booking policy message for the reservation. |
| `confirmation_number` | integer | Unique identifier for the confirmed reservation. |
| `offer_confirmation_number` | integer | Unique identifier for any special offer linked to the reservation. |
| `date_time` | string | Date and time of the reservation in ISO 8601 format. |
| `party_size` | integer | Number of guests included in the reservation. |
| `notes` | string | Special requests or notes provided during the reservation. |
| `manage_reservation_url` | string | URL to manage the confirmed reservation online. |
| `cancel_cutoff_date_utc` | string | Timestamp of last possible time when the record can be cancelled (UTC time). |

### Notes

- **Default Behavior:** If the `reservation_attribute` is not provided, the default seating will be used.
    
- **Experience:** The `experience` object supports modifying experiences and add-ons for enhanced booking flexibility.

response.body:
{ "message": "We have a 5 minute grace period. Please call us if you are running later than 5 minutes after your reservation time.

We may contact you about this reservation, so please ensure your email and phone number are up to date.

Your table will be reserved for 1 hour 30 minutes for parties of up to 2; 2 hours for parties of up to 4; 2 hours 30 minutes for parties of up to 6; and 3 hours for parties of 7+.", "confirmation_number": 1751, "date_time": "2025-11-13T16:00", "party_size": 2, "notes": "Window Table" }

request.description:
## Cancel Reservation API Documentation

### Endpoint

**URL:** `{{base-url}}/v2/booking/{{rid}}reservations/{id}`

**Method:** PUT

### Description

The Cancel Reservation API allows users to cancel an existing reservation by providing the unique identifier for the reservation. No other status changes are supported.

### Request

The `id` parameter is a unique combination of the `Restaurant_ID` and `Confirmation_Number` provided in the request URL, formatted as `{rid}-{confirmation_number}`.

### Request Body Fields

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | string | Unique combination of `Restaurant_ID` and `Confirmation_Number` provided as a part of the URL. |
| `status` | string | Must be set to `CancelledWeb` to cancel the reservation. No other status values are supported. |

### Notes

- **Request Requirements:** The `id` must be obtained from the original reservation creation process and stored in your system of record.
    
- **Cancel Response:** A successful cancellation request will return a `200 OK` status with an empty body.
    
- **No Other Status Changes:** Only the status `CancelledWeb` is supported for this API call.

item.description:
**OVERVIEW**

This API allows you to Modify or Cancel reservations that have been previously booked via this API. Please note that if the reservation is booked outside of this API, you may not be able to modify or cancel.

To perform these queries, you will need the reservation ID. This can be found in the original Make Reservation response or via the Get Reservations query.

You may also choose to update or cancel reservations, via linking to the reservation details view. The reservation details view URL is returned in **manage_reservation_url** response field of **make** and **get** reservation endpoints. The same link works for web, mobile web, and the OpenTable Android and iOS apps.

request.description:
## Get Reservation API Documentation

### Endpoint

**URL:** `{{base-url}}v2/booking/{rid}/reservations/{rid}-{confirmationId}`

**Method:** GET

### Description

The Get Reservation API allows you to retrieve detailed information about a specific reservation for a restaurant. This information includes reservation status, party size, reservation attributes, pricing details, experiences, and additional add-ons.

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `rid` | string | The unique identifier for the restaurant, provided by OpenTable. |
| `confirmationId` | string | The confirmation number of the reservation, unique per booking. |

### API Overview

This API returns a structured payload containing the following key sections:

- **General Reservation Details:** Status, environment, confirmation number, restaurant ID, date/time, party size, and a URL to manage the reservation.
    
- **Experience Object:** Provides a detailed breakdown of the experience including pricing, taxes, service fees, and add-ons.
    
- **Add-Ons:** A list of additional items purchased during the reservation with individual pricing, taxes, and service fees.
    

## General Reservation Details

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `confirmation_number` | integer | The confirmation number associated with the reservation. |
| `restaurant_id` | integer | The unique identifier of the restaurant. |
| `date_time` | string | The date and time of the reservation in ISO 8601 format. |
| `party_size` | integer | The number of people in the reservation party. |
| `manage_reservation_url` | string | A URL to the reservation management page on the OpenTable platform. |
| `reservation_attribute` | string | The type or attribute of the reservation (e.g., Default). |
| `status` | string | Status of the reservation. Possible values: “Pending”, “Seated”, “AssumedSeated”, “CancelledWeb”, “CancelledRestaurant”, “Cancelled”, “NoShow”. |
| `notes` | string | Any special notes or requests added to the reservation (e.g., Window Table). |
| `dining_area_id` | string | The ID for the dining area booked. |
| `environment` | string | The type of environment for the reservation (e.g., Indoor, Outdoor). |
| `experience` | object | Contains all of the experience details. |
| `credit_card_last_four` | string | The last four digits of the credit card used for payment (if applicable). |
| `cancel_cutoff_date_utc` | string | Timestamp of last possible time when the record can be cancelled (UTC time). |
| `language` | string | The language locale used for the reservation (e.g., en-US). |

### Experience Object

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | The unique identifier for the experience. |
| `version` | integer | The version number of the experience object. |
| `name` | string | The name of the experience. |
| `description` | string | A description of the experience. |
| `currency` | string | The currency used for the reservation (e.g., USD). |
| `currencyDenominator` | integer | A factor to convert the amount to the appropriate currency units. |
| `prepaid` | boolean | Indicates if the experience required pre-payment. |
| `price` | integer | The base price of the experience, excluding fees/taxes. |
| `experienceSubtotalAmount` | integer | The subtotal amount for the experience before taxes and fees. |
| `itemSubtotalAmount` | integer | Subtotal of the selected price items (Adult, Child, etc.). |
| `addOnSubtotalAmount` | integer | Subtotal of all added add-ons. |
| `subtotalAmount` | integer | Sum of all experience and item subtotals (pre-tax/fee). |
| `taxesAmount` | integer | The total amount of all collected taxes. |
| `totalAmount` | integer | The grand total amount charged, including all fees and taxes. |
| `tipAmount` | integer | The calculated tip amount (if applicable). |
| `tipPercent` | integer | The applied tip percentage (e.g., 0.0 for 0%). |
| `serviceFeeAmount` | integer | The total amount of all service fees applied. |
| `taxesOnServiceFeesAmount` | integer | The portion of total taxes applied specifically to service fees. |
| `taxesOnTipAmount` | integer | The portion of total taxes applied specifically to the tip. |
| `addOnSubtotalWithoutTaxesAmount` | integer | The subtotal of add-ons, before taxes are applied. |
| `taxesOnItemAmount` | integer | The portion of total taxes applied specifically to item subtotals. |
| `taxesOnAddOnsAmount` | integer | The portion of total taxes applied specifically to add-on subtotals. |
| `hasMandatoryTip` | boolean | Indicates if a tip/gratuity was mandatory. |
| `isTipTaxable` | boolean | Indicates if the calculated tip is subject to tax. |
| `prices` | Array | List of all price options defined for the experience. See Price Item Object section. |
| `subtotalsPerPriceType` | Array | Itemized breakdown of the total count and subtotal for each selected price type. See Subtotals Per Price Type Object section. |
| `addOns` | Array | List of actual add-on items added to this specific reservation. See Add-On Reservation Item Object section. |

### Price Item Object (experience.prices\[\])

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Unique ID for the price option. |
| `title` | string | Title of the price option (e.g., Adult, Child). |
| `description` | string | Description of the price item (e.g., Adult price). |
| `price` | integer | The unit price of this item, in the smallest currency unit. |

### Subtotals Per Price Type Object (experience.subtotalsPerPriceType\[\])

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Unique ID for the price option (references prices\[\].id). |
| `count` | integer | The number of guests or parties charged this price type. |
| `price` | integer | The unit price of this item, in the smallest currency unit. |
| `subtotal` | integer | The extended price for this item type (count \* price). |
| `prce_all_inclusive` | boolean | Indicates if this price already included taxes and fees. |

### Add-On Reservation Item Object (experience.addOns\[\])

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `itemId` | string | The unique ID of the selected add-on item (UUID format). |
| `quantity` | integer | The quantity of this add-on item reserved. |
| `lineTotal` | integer | The total cost of this line item (quantity \* price), before fees/taxes. |
| `price` | integer | The unit price of the add-on item. |
| `name` | string | The display name of the add-on. |
| `description` | string | The description of the add-on. |
| `lineSubtotalWithTaxes` | integer | The final price of the line item including all taxes and service fees. |
| `taxes` | Array | Itemized list of taxes applied to this add-on. See Tax Detail Object section. |
| `serviceFees` | Array | Itemized list of service fees applied to this add-on. See Service Fee Detail Object section. |

### Tax Detail Object (experience.addOns\[\].taxes\[\])

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | string | Unique identifier for the tax type. |
| `label` | string | Display name of the tax (e.g., Tax). |
| `tax_percent` | integer | The tax rate applied (e.g., 10.0). |
| `tax_subtotal_amount` | integer | Tax calculated on the pre-tax subtotal (if applicable). |
| `tax_on_item_amount` | integer | Tax calculated on the item price component. |
| `tax_on_tip_amount` | integer | Tax calculated on the tip component. |
| `tax_on_service_fees_amount` | integer | Tax calculated on the service fee component. |

### Service Fee Detail Object (experience.addOns\[\].serviceFees\[\])

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `service_fee_percent` | integer | The service fee rate applied (e.g., 15.0). |
| `service_fee_amount` | integer | The total monetary amount of the service fee applied to this item. |

### Notes

- Ensure you use a valid `rid` and `confirmationId` when making the request.
    
- The API provides a comprehensive breakdown of pricing, taxes, and additional fees for accurate reservation management.
    
- All amounts are presented in minor currency units (e.g., cents).

item.name: Private Dining Lead API

request.description:
# API Path

/v1/privatedining/restaurant/{{RID}}/leads

Method: POST

## Eligibility Rules

- Access is **restricted to restaurant IDs (RIDs) with an active Private Dining subscription**
    
- Requests for ineligible RIDs will be rejected
    

---

## Request Parameters

### Path Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `rid` | string | Yes | OpenTable restaurant ID used to route the lead to the correct venue |

---

### Request Body (JSON)

#### Required Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `firstName` | string | Guest first name |
| `lastName` | string | Guest last name |
| `email` | string | Guest email address. Must be a valid email format (business rule validation enforced) |
| `phoneNumber` | string | Guest phone number |
| `eventDate` | string (`yyyy-mm-dd`) | Event date. **Must be a future date** |
| `eventTime` | string (`HH:mm`, 24-hour) | Event time |
| `partySize` | number | Number of guests. Minimum value is `1` |
| `eventType` | string (enum) | Type of event |

---

#### `eventType` Enum Values

- `Anniversary`
    
- `BabyShower`
    
- `BarMitzvah`
    
- `Birthday`
    
- `BridalWeddingShower`
    
- `CharityEvent`
    
- `Class`
    
- `CocktailParty`
    
- `Conference`
    
- `CorporateEvent`
    
- `DinnerParty`
    
- `Engagement`
    
- `FilmingLocation`
    
- `Fundraiser`
    
- `Graduation`
    
- `HolidayEvent`
    
- `LaunchParty`
    
- `Meeting`
    
- `Other`
    
- `PrivateDining`
    
- `RehearsalDinner`
    
- `SocialEvent`
    
- `SpecialOccasion`
    
- `WeddingReception`
    

---

#### Optional Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `flexibleDate` | boolean | Set to `true` if the event date is flexible or not yet fixed |

---

## Notes & Validation Rules

- `eventDate` must be in the future
    
- `eventTime` must use **24-hour format**
    
- `email` is validated for proper format
    
- `partySize` must be ≥ 1
    
- Requests for RIDs without an active Private Dining subscription will fail

item.description:
# Overview

  
  
The Private Dining Lead API allows partners to create and submit private dining inquiries on behalf of guests directly to an OpenTable restaurant. Each lead is routed using the OpenTable restaurant ID (RID), ensuring the request reaches the correct venue and its private dining team.  
  
This endpoint is designed to support high-value event inquiries such as celebrations, corporate events, and group dining, helping restaurants capture demand beyond standard reservations.  
  
Posting leads is restricted to restaurants with an active Private Dining subscription.

request.description:
## Overview

The Slot Lock API provides the ability to create, manage, and retrieve reservations for a restaurant. This documentation outlines the payload structure, attributes, and their descriptions, along with sample requests and responses for seamless integration.

---

## Request Details

### Endpoint

**URL:** `{{base-url}}/v2/booking/{{rid}}/slot_locks`

**Method: POST**

### Headers

| **Header** | **Value** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Specifies that the request body contains JSON data. |
| `Authorization` | `Bearer [AccessToken]` | A valid access token for authenticating the request. |

---

## Request Body

### Attributes

| **Attribute** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `party_size` | integer | Number of people in the reservation party. _Required_ |
| `date_time` | string | Date and time of the reservation in ISO 8601 format. _Required_ |
| `reservation_attribute` | string | Type or attribute of the reservation (e.g., `default`). |
| `experience` | object | Details about the dining experience. _Optional, only required if experience will be booked._ |
| `dining_area_id` | integer | Identifier for the dining area. _Optional, only required if specific dining area will be booked._ |
| `environment` | string | Type of environment (e.g., `Indoor`, `Outdoor`). _Optional, only required if specific environment will be booked._ |

### Experience Object Attributes

_Note: This Data will be gathered from the GET Experiences API response. All attributes are required when booking an experience._

| **Attribute** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Unique identifier for the experience. |
| `version` | integer | Version of the experience. |
| `party_size_per_price_type` | array | Array detailing the party size per price type. _Optional, applicable only if returned in GET Experiences API_ |
| `add_ons` | array | Array of add-on items for the experience. _Optional, applicable only if returned in GET Experiences API_ |

### Party_Size_Per_Price_Type Array Object Attributes

_Note: This Data will be gathered from the GET Experiences API response. Optional, applicable only if returned in GET Experiences API_

| **Attribute** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | integer | Unique identifier for the price type. |
| `count` | integer | Count of items for the price type. |

### Add_Ons Array Object Attributes

_Note: This Data will be gathered from the GET Experiences API response. Optional, applicable only if returned in GET Experiences API_

| **Attribute** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `item_id` | string | Unique identifier for the add-on item. |
| `quantity` | integer | Quantity of the add-on item. |

---

---

## Notes

- Ensure the `restaurant_id`, `party_size`, `date_time`, and `experience` attributes are accurate for successful reservation creation.
    

## Response

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `expires_at` | string | The expiration date and time of the slot lock in ISO 8601 format. The slot lock expires within 5 minutes. |
| `reservation_token` | string | A unique token required for the subsequent reservation creation request. |

### Notes

- The `expires_at` timestamp indicates when the slot lock expires. Ensure the reservation is completed before this time.
    
- The `reservation_token` is required for the reservation creation request and ensures the slot remains locked during the reservation process.
    
- If the `expires_at` time is reached without completing the reservation, the slot will be released.
    
- Tokens should be treated securely and not exposed to unauthorized users.

## Cancellation Policies API Documentation

### Endpoint

**URL:** `{{base-url}}/v2/cancellation-policies/{rid}/{cancelationid}`

**Method:** GET

### Description

The cancellation policy endpoint allows you to retrieve the cancellation policy for a reservation based on the restaurant ID and cancellation ID. The `cancelationid` is obtained from the response of the Make Reservation call.

### Sample Request

``` bash
curl --location '{{base-url}}/v2/cancellation-policies/{rid}/{cancelationid}' \
--header 'Authorization: bearer '

 ```

### Response

``` json
{
    "partySize": 5,
    "policyType": "Deposit",
    "depositDetails": {
        "amount": 1500,
        "currency": "USD",
        "denominator": 100,
        "type": "PerGuest"
    },
    "cutOff": {
        "cutoffType": "DaysBefore",
        "daysBeforeCutoff": 2
    }
}

 ```

### Response Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `partySize` | integer | The number of guests for the reservation. |
| `policyType` | string | Type of cancellation policy. |
| `depositDetails` | object | Details of the deposit policy. |
| `depositDetails.amount` | integer | Deposit amount in minor currency units. |
| `depositDetails.currency` | string | Currency of the deposit amount. |
| `depositDetails.denominator` | integer | Conversion factor for minor units. |
| `depositDetails.type` | string | Specifies if the deposit is per guest. |
| `cutOff` | object | Cutoff policy details. |
| `cutOff.cutoffType` | string | Type of cutoff applied. |
| `cutOff.daysBeforeCutoff` | integer | Number of days before cutoff. |

### Notes

- Ensure you use a valid `cancelationid` retrieved from the Make Reservation call.
    
- The cutoff policy specifies the period within which cancellations are allowed.
    
- The deposit amount is specified in minor currency units (e.g., cents for USD).

item.name: Migration Guide Overview
item.description: For partners looking to migrate from Online Booking API v1 to Online Booking API v2, we have a detailed migration guide available upon request. We have also included some of the overview details below. Please note that any new implementation of our APIs may require additional contracting and certification. Please work with your OpenTable team for details. # Migration Overview ## What's Changed? - Availability response is nested: availability_types\[\] with dining_area, environment, and policy refs. - Search window expanded to ≤720 minutes. - New endpoints: availability-metadata, booking-policies, cancellation-policies, experiences totals. - Slot lock path changed and adds DELETE to release. - Make reservation path changed - Booking supports Accept-Language, gzip, SMS opt-in, dining_area_id, environment. - Link policy split: booking_url (affiliate) vs booking_restref_url (restaurant group). ## **Breaking Changes You Must Handle** 1. Parse availability_types (remove reliance on time-root attribute/availabilityType). 2. Lift validators for forward/backward minutes to ≤720. 3. Normalize attribute casing (e.g., 'highTop'). 4. Don’t hard-code policies—fetch and render via policy endpoints. 5. Implement DELETE for slot lock cancellation. ## **Minimal Code Changes to Ship** ## **Known Pitfalls** - Hard-coded policy copy, leading to mismatches. - Forgetting to DELETE slot_locks on user cancel. - Attribute casing mismatches breaking filters. - Designing APIs or schemas too rigidly, without allowing for additional attributes or fields. This can lead to breaking changes when new properties are introduced within objects or appended to the end of a response. To avoid this, clients should be built to ignore unknown attributes and treat responses as extensible rather than fixed.

item.name: Tips for Successful Implementation for Online Booking API
item.description:
# **Overview**

The OpenTable Booking API catalog (aka Consumer API) is a robust suite of tools designed to empower partners to seamlessly integrate OpenTable's reservation management functionalities into their own digital platforms. This comprehensive API solution offers a wide array of capabilities, providing restaurants with the ability to not only search for available tables in real-time across single or multiple restaurant locations but also to create, modify, and cancel reservations directly within their own website or application.

Furthermore, the Consumer API enables partners to maintain control over their booking policies and customize the user experience to align with their brand identity. By leveraging the API's flexible framework, restaurants can optimize their reservation process, enhance customer engagement, and streamline operations, ultimately driving increased bookings and improved customer satisfaction.

For questions and support, please contact [api@opentable.com](https://mailto:api@opentable.com)

# **Details and Documentation**

As of spring 2025, the most current version of Consumer API is v2, and any new implementation must adhere to those specifications. You can find the documentation for the APIs here - [docs.opentable.com](https://docs.opentable.com/#e521f540-a3db-404e-ba0d-8e0aea242c5c)

# **Helpful Tips**

- We are here to help! Reach out to us at [api@opentable.com](https://mailto:api@opentable.com) should you have any questions.
    
- **Environments:** During the onboarding process, you will only have access to our pre-production test restaurants. You will be given access to your production restaurants upon certification.
    
- **Search Window:** Although not required, the maximum number of minutes forwards and backwards to search for times is 720 +/-, giving you a full day's window -- Does your implementation allow for a maximized search window or have you accounted for actively limiting the search results?
    
- **Table Types:** Although not required, Table Types and Dining Areas are part of over 90% of our current restaurant base -- Are you exposing these options?
    
- **Credit Cards:** Although not required, Credit Card capture is required in a growing number of our restaurant base -- Do you have credit card search and capture as part of your implementation or have you accounted for actively not including it?
    
- **Email opt-in:** Do you have means to allow diners to opt-in or opt-out of email marketing from the restaurant in question? If you do not present the option to the diner and that is not part of the API payload, you will not be able to capture their marketing preference.
    
- **Reservation Notes:** Have you included a field to allow diners to key in freeform notes as requests associated with their reservation?
    
- **Experiences:** Make sure you can handle the following experiences IDs (pre prod) for RID 1040230 - The experiences are the same as production but with a different ID:
    
    - **518093:** This experience has multiple priceTitles (these titles can be anything) and a mandatory tip.
        
        - **priceId, 129261** has priceAllInclusive = false. Meaning that it has taxes and service which affects the total price
            
    - **518092:** The name is “Breakfast - Pay at the restaurant” make sure your LLM is not relying on the name to inform the diner that they must pay at the restaurant. (use the price array)
        
    - **518091:** Experiences inherit the restaurant level/default policy only when the policy is “hold”. Make sure you can handle it on Mondays where there is no policy and Friday to Sunday where there’s a “hold” policy.
        

# **Testing Environment**

Below are some Test Restaurant ID’s (RIDs) that you may utilize for the Consumer API. These are meant to be used for Pre-Production testing only. We have also included some nuances around availability and requirements for each. This should help you test numerous scenarios.

**Note we consider the following days “Special Days”:**

- Thanksgiving Day (US Observance date)
    
- Christmas Eve
    
- Christmas Day
    
- New Year's Day
    
- Memorial Day
    
- Independence Day
    
- Labor Day
    

  
**RID : 264001**

- Party Size: 1-20
    
- Shift: 6AM-2AM, Sun-Sat
    
- Special Days closed
    

  
**RID 1038007: With Credit Card Requirements and Experiences (Stripe)**

- Party Size: 1-10
    
- Shift: 24 hours
    
- Special Days open
    

**RID 1040230: With Credit Card Requirements and Experiences (Spreedly)**

- Party Size: 1-20
    
- Reservations:
    
- \- Monday: standard
    
- \- Tues to Thur: deposit | party size > 2
    
- \- Fri to Sun: credit card hold | party size > 2
    
- \- Experiences: everyday 6am-12am
    
- Shifts: 6AM-12PM, 1PM-5PM, 6PM-11.30PM.
    

  
**RID 268549:**

- Party Size: 1-10
    
- Shifts: 7AM-6PM, 8PM-12AM, Sun-Sat
    
- Special Days closed
    

  
**RID 138712:**

- Party Size: 1-6
    
- Shifts: 8AM-2AM, Sun & Sat only
    
- Special Days closed
    

# **Certification Process**

OpenTable will need to review and approve your pre-production implementation of the API prior to granting production publication. A minimum window of 14 days for OpenTable to review a pre-production submission is required. Production credentials will be provided only after QA certification of the pre-prod implementation.

**The following scenarios will be verified:**

- Verify party size is between 1-20
    
- Verify times are between 12:00 AM and 11:30 PM
    
- Diner should not be allowed to select a past date (optional, but highly recommended)
    
- Perform a search on a future date, time and party size between 1-20. This should return at least one time slot
    
- Diner should be allowed to select a date/time/party size and book a reservation that is up-to a year in advance (depends on your preferred setting: it can be as little as 1 day and up to 1 year)
    
- Verify diner must enter first name, last name, e-mail, and phone number in order to book a reservation
    
- Verify with different combinations of first, last name, phone number and email address.
    
- Verify diner is able to cancel a reservation
    
- If more than one restaurant locations is supported, verify the reservation is made correctly for the selected location, date, time and party size
    
- Perform a search on today's date and a time that has already passed. Diner should get available time slots that are in the future.
    
- Book and cancel a reservation. Confirm the diner's name, date/time/party size of the reservation is accurate. Verify e-mail confirmation is received for booking and cancellation.
    
- Book two reservations for the same date that are within 2.5 hours. Verify diner gets an appropriate error message and is directed back to the search page for the second reservation. Cancel the first reservation
    
- If there is a cut off time for cancellation, verify diner gets a cancellation error if cancellation is made after the cut off time.
    
- Email opt in checkbox present and functions correctly in OT4R when on and off
    
- Special requests coming through properly in the Guest Requests section. Make sure only notes appear when entered in web UI
    

# **Branding Requirements for Website**

- For details around our brand requirements please visit [opentable.design](https://www.opentable.design/)
    
- The restaurant website is required to display “Powered by” and the OpenTable logo on any pages/areas that include the reservation booking flow.
    
- The restaurant website is required to link to OpenTable’s Terms of Use Privacy Policy and include language requiring the user to agree as part of the reservation booking flow prior to completing the reservation.
    
    - E.g. “By clicking ‘Complete reservation’ you agree to the OpenTable [Terms of Use](https://www.opentable.com/legal/terms-and-conditions) and [Privacy Policy](https://www.opentable.com/legal/privacy-policy).”
        
    - Terms and Conditions: [https://www.opentable.com/legal/terms-and-conditions](https://www.opentable.com/legal/terms-and-conditions)
        
    - Privacy Policy:  [ https://www.opentable.com/legal/privacy-policy](https://www.opentable.com/legal/privacy-policy)

**Consumer API Availability Notice**

For existing partners, **Consumer API v1** remains available and supported. There are **no deprecation dates currently scheduled** for v1. If you are already integrated, you may continue using it as part of your existing implementation. Please coordinate with your implementation team to access the appropriate documentation or if you have any questions regarding usage.

However, we **recommend** using **Consumer API v2** for all **new integrations**, as it offers enhanced functionality, improved performance, and ongoing feature development.

**The documentation below details Consumer API v2 endpoints.**

request.description:
# Overview

This documentation outlines the process for using the Availability Search API to retrieve reservation times and metadata for one restaurant.

## Endpoint

**URL**: `{{base-url}}/v2/availability-metadata/{{rid}}`

### Path Parameter

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `rid` | Integer | A unique, static identifier for each restaurant. Each restaurant will have its own unique ID. |

## Query Parameters

The API does not use query parameters for this endpoint.

**Response Elements**

The API response includes the following elements:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `data` | Object | See `Data` section |

### Data

The `data` object includes:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `environments` | Array of Strings | Values will be either `Outdoor` or `Indoor`. |
| `attributes` | Array of Strings | Values will be either `bar`, `counter`, `highTop`, `outdoor`, or `default`. |
| `dining_areas` | Array of Objects | Detailed information about available dining areas (see `Dining Areas` section). |

### The `dining_areas` Array

Each dining area object includes:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Integer | Identifier of the dining area. |
| `name` | String | Name of the dining area. |
| `description` | String | Description of the dining area. |
| `environment` | String | Environment type (e.g., `Indoor`, `Outdoor`). |

item.description:
# Introduction

**Availability Search API** provides developers with a streamlined way to retrieve restaurant reservation availability using OpenTable's platform. This API enables partners to perform highly customizable searches based on parameters such as date, time, party size, table type, and more.

## Overview

**Availability Search API** allows you to:

- Customize searches with parameters like date, time range, party size, and table type.
    
- Retrieve detailed availability, including dining area information and cancellation policies.
    
- Include special options like experiences and credit-card-secured reservation times.
    
- This will search for any inventory available that has been categorized as "In House Only" or "All Availabilities"
    

### **Request**

**Method:** `GET`  
**Search URL:** `{{base-url}}/inhouse/v1/availability/{RID}`

#### **Path Parameter**

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `RID` | long | Restaurant ID |

#### **Query Parameters**

| Name | Type | Usage | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `party_size` | int32 | The party size for availability search. Must be between 1 and 20. | Yes |
| `start_date_time` | Date (yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm) | The restaurant local date and time to search in ISO 8601 format. Must align to a 15-minute interval. Example: `2024-12-01T18:00`. Must be within 730 days from today. | Yes |
| `forward_minutes` | int32 | The number of minutes forward from the start time to search availability (15-minute intervals, up to 180 minutes). | No (Default: 150) |
| `backward_minutes` | int32 | The number of minutes backward from the start time to search availability (15-minute intervals, up to 180 minutes). | No (Default: 150) |
| `include_experiences` | Boolean | Whether to include experience availabilities. | No (Default: false) |

**📝 Note:**

- The API considers 5 AM as the start of a new business day.
    
- Example: A request with `start_date_time = 2024-01-10T03:30` will only search availability between 01:00 and 04:45 on 2024-01-10.
    
- If no availability is found, a `200 OK` will be returned with empty arrays.
    

---

### **Response**

| Attribute | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `rid` | Restaurant Identifier |
| `restaurant_settings` | List of settings applied to the restaurant |
| `party_size` | The party size used for the availability search |
| `times` | Array of available time slots |
| `times_available` | Array of available time entities |

#### **The** **`restaurant_settings`** **Object**

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `scc_enabled` | Boolean | Indicates whether secure credit card capture is enabled for the restaurant. When `true`, the restaurant supports bookings that require credit card holds, deposits, or prepayments. |

---

### **The** **`times_available`** **Array**

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `time` | String | ISO 8601 time slot (`yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm`) |
| `availability_types` | Array | Types of availability (`Standard` or `Experience`) |

#### **The** **`availability_types`** **Array**

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `type` | String | Type of availability (`Standard` or `Experience`) |
| `cancellationPolicy` | Object | Cancellation policy information |
| `diningArea` | Array of Entities | Dining area and table type details |
| `experienceCancellationPolicy` | Array | Cancellation policy details specific to each experience (when `type` is Experience) |

---

### **The** **`cancellationPolicy`** **Object**

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `type` | String | Type of policy (`Deposit`, `Hold`, `Prepayment`) |
| `id` | String | Policy identifier |
| `amount` | int32 | Deposit amount in fractional currency (e.g., $50.49 → 5049) |
| `denominator` | int32 | Currency denominator (e.g., 1 USD = 100) |
| `currency` | String | 3-letter currency code |
| `deposit_type` | String | Calculation basis (`PerGuest`) |

---

### **The** **`dining_area`** **Array**

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Long | Dining area ID |
| `table_type` | Array of Strings | Table types (`default`, `bar`, etc.) |
| `experience_ids` | List of Longs | (For `Experience` type only) Applicable experience IDs |

#### **Understanding Dining Areas and Table types**

- **Dining Area:** is a grouping of tables within a specific physical or conceptual section of a restaurant, such as “Patio,” “Main Room,” or “Lounge.” Restaurants can create dining areas and assign tables to them.
    
    - A restaurant section without a designated **Dining Area,** will return`other`
        
- **Table Type** describes the attributes of the table itself. The available table types are: Regular (API sees `default`), High top, Counter, Bar and Outdoor
    
    - A table without a designated **Table Type,** will return `default`
        

When all the Dining Areas and Tables Types are assigned and these are not provided in the request, you will get the following error because the API has no value to default to.

**Note:** Don't forget include these in your Slot Lock and Make Reservation endpoints

``` json
{
    "errors": [
        {
            "code": "NoAvailability",
            "message": "No availability found for the given parameters"
        }
    ],
    "requestid": "53e15428-79dc-888-a55a-3e4fb50f5"
}

 ```

**📝 Note:**

To retrieve full dining area details, make a call to:  
`https://platform.opentable.com/v1/availability-metadata?rid={}`

### **Common Error Messaging**

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `errors` | Array | An array of error entities |
| `message` | String | Short description of the error |
| `requestid` | String | Unique request ID for OpenTable support reference |

---

### **Search - Common Errors**

| Reason | Message | Code | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Invalid or Expired Token | invalid_token | `invalid_token` | 401 Unauthorized |
| No Authorization Token provided | Authorization token is required to access this resource | — | 403 Forbidden |
| Illegal Party Size | Party size must be from 1 to 20 | `InvalidPartySize` | 400 Bad Request |
| No Party Size | Required Integer parameter `party_size` is not present | `MissingPartySize` | 400 Bad Request |
| Party Size not an Integer | `party_size` should be of type Integer | `InvalidPartySize` | 400 Bad Request |
| No Start Date/Time | Required parameter `start_date_time` not present | `MissingDateTime` | 400 Bad Request |
| Invalid Date/Time | `start_date_time` should be in ISO format | `InvalidDateTime` | 400 Bad Request |
| Invalid Forward Minutes | `forward_minutes` should be of type Integer | `InvalidForwardMinutes` | 400 Bad Request |
| Invalid Backward Minutes | `backward_minutes` should be of type Integer | `InvalidBackwardMinutes` | 400 Bad Request |
| Start Date too soon | Cannot search within 15 minutes of current time | `InvalidStartDateTime` | 400 Bad Request |

---

item.name: Experiences
Experience-Specific Cancellation Policies
item.description:
## Experience-Specific Policies

OpenTable supports customizable policies for individual experiences, allowing restaurants to tailor booking, cancellation, and terms and conditions to each unique offering. These policies apply only to reservations made for a specific experience and do not affect standard reservations.

### Policy Hierarchy

Restaurants can choose to:

- Define **experience-specific policies** for each experience, or use **default policies** that apply to all reservations via OpenTable for Restaurants (OT4R).
    

### Types of Experience-Specific Policies

- **Prepaid Experiences**
    
    Always use a `Prepayment` policy type.
    
- **Free and non pre paid Experiences:**
    
    - Will inherit the restaurant level/default policy only when the policy is **hold**
        

📝 **Note - Free Experiences:** they inherit the restaurant level policy when it's hold.

### Legacy Experiences

Experiences created **before the launch of experience-specific policies** will retain their original configurations. These are referred to as **legacy experiences** and do not adopt the new policy structures unless updated manually.

---

## API Changes: Availability Search

To support these policies, a new structure has been introduced in the availability search response:

- **New Field:** `availability_types.experienceCancellationPolicy`
    
    - This field is included **only when the** **`type`** **is** **`"Experience"`**. Developers should use this new structure to retrieve cancellation policies for experience-based availability.
        
    - **Experiences cannot be cancelled nor modified**
        

**Backward Compatibility Notice:**

During the transition period, the legacy `cancellationPolicy` field will continue to appear for `"Experience"` availability types. This allows developers to integrate the new field while maintaining compatibility with existing implementations.

---

### Example API Response

``` json
{
  "times_available": [
    {
      "time": "2025-03-31T11:30",
      "availability_types": [
        {
          "type": "Standard",
          "cancellationPolicy": {
            "type": "Deposit",
            "id": "605388198f76d3d97a5e6964:v13",
            "amount": 500,
            "denominator": 100,
            "currency": "USD",
            "depositType": "PerGuest"
          }
        },
        {
          "type": "Experience",
          "cancellationPolicy": {
            "type": "Deposit",
            "id": "605388198f76d3d97a5e6964:v13",
            "amount": 500,
            "denominator": 100,
            "currency": "USD",
            "depositType": "PerGuest"
          },
          "experienceCancellationPolicy": [
            {
              "experienceId": 513366,
              "type": "Prepayment",
              "id": "67d3235f4c20509ba6dce0d6:v1"
            },
            {
              "experienceId": 512230,
              "type": "Deposit",
              "id": "605388198f76d3d97a5e6964:v13",
              "amount": 500,
              "denominator": 100,
              "currency": "USD",
              "depositType": "PerGuest"
            },
            {
              "experienceId": 513364,
              "type": "Hold",
              "id": "67d31ba38762da53f4b1df07:v1"
            },
            {
              "experienceId": 512940,
              "type": "Prepayment",
              "id": "67b89d527e04fd46b4435295:v1"
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

 ```

The `experienceCancellationPolicy` object is the authoritative source for cancellation details associated with experience-based reservations and will eventually replace the general `cancellationPolicy` field.

---

request.description:
# **Active Experiences API**

### **Overview**

The **Active Experiences** endpoint allows clients to retrieve a list of active bookable experiences associated with a specific restaurant. It includes rich metadata such as descriptions, pricing, gratuity, and availability details.

**Important Note:** This endpoint **may return experiences that are not bookable by phone**. Partners must validate bookability using the availability search endpoint, based on date, time, and party size.

---

### **Request**

- **Method:** `GET`
    
- **URL:** `{{base-url}}/inhouse/v1/booking/experiences/{{rid}}`
    

#### **Path Parameter**

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `RID` | Long | Restaurant ID used to fetch associated active experiences |

---

### **Response**

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `data` | Array of Entity | Array of active experiences (see **Data Entity**) |

---

### **The** **`data`** **Array: Active Experience**

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `bookable` | Boolean | Indicates if the experience can be booked |
| `currency` | String (3-letter) | Currency of the experience price |
| `description` | String | Description of the experience |
| `experience_id` | Long | Unique identifier of the experience |
| `name` | String | Name/title of the experience |
| `rid` | Long | Restaurant identifier |
| `version` | Int | Version of the experience |
| `price_info` | Object | Nested object with pricing details (see **Price Info Entity**) |

---

### **The** **`price_info`** **Object**

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `price_type` | Enum { PER_PERSON, PER_PARTY } | Indicates pricing is per person or per party |
| `pre_payment_required` | Boolean | Indicates if prepayment is required |
| `currency_code` | String (3-letter) | Currency used for the pricing |
| `multiplier` | Int | Multiplier for price units |
| `gratuity` | Object | Gratuity details (see **Gratuity Entity**) |
| `prices` | Array | Array of price definitions (see **Prices Entity**) |

---

### **The** **`gratuity`** **Object**

**Gratuity = tip:** it is an additional charge added to the cost of an experience and It can be mandatory or optional. If it gratuity is mandantory, it should be mentioned.

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `label` | String | Description of the gratuity |
| `numerator` | Int | Numerator value of gratuity percentage |
| `denominator` | Int | Denominator for gratuity calculation |
| `mandatory` | Boolean | Indicates if gratuity is mandatory |
| `taxable` | Boolean | Indicates if gratuity is taxable |

---

### **The** **`prices`** **Array**

Experiences return an array of prices to enable flexible, category-based pricing for the same event or service. This design allows for:

- Differentiated pricing for various guest types (e.g., adults, children, seniors)
    
- Accommodation of promotional rates or packages within a single experience
    
- Greater configurability for venues to tailor pricing based on demand, time, or guest characteristics
    

By storing prices as an array, the system can seamlessly present appropriate options for each booking without requiring separate experience entries for each pricing scenario.

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `price_id` | Long | Unique price identifier |
| `price_title` | String | Title or label of the price (e.g. Adult, Child) |
| `min_unit_amount` | Int | Minimum price amount (in minor units e.g. cents) |
| `price_all_inclusive` | Boolean | Indicates if the price includes taxes and service charges. This affects the final price. Make sure you call Experience total to get the full price |

---

## **Common Error Messaging**

### **Error Response Structure**

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `errors` | Array | List of error entities |
| `message` | String | Brief error message |
| `requestid` | String | Unique identifier for troubleshooting |

---

### **Active Experiences - Common Errors**

| Reason for the Error | Error Message | Code | HTTP Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Invalid or Expired Token | `invalid_token` | `invalid_token` | 401 Unauthorized |
| No Authorization Token Provided | Authorization token is required to access this resource | — | 403 Forbidden |
| Invalid Restaurant ID | Restaurant identifier is invalid | `InvalidRid` | 400 Bad Request |

---

request.description:
# Experience Total API

## Overview

The **Experience Total** endpoint calculates the full cost breakdown for a selected experience, including optional add-ons, service fees, taxes, and gratuity, based on the party size and price types.

---

## Request

- **Method:** `POST`
    
- **URL:** `/booking/experiences/{RID}`
    

### Path Parameter

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| RID | Long | Restaurant ID |

### Request Body

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `experience_id` | Long | Experience Identifier (**required**) |
| `party_size_per_price_type` | Array | Indicates different price types. **Required** if `"price_type": "PER_PERSON"` For `"price_type": "PER_PARTY"` set to `null` or omit it. You can find this from the Get Active Experiences endpont. |
| `party_size` | Int | If using slot lock, this should match that number. **Required** only if `"price_type": "PER_PARTY"` You can find this from the Get Active Experiences endpont. |

---

## Response

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | Long | Experience Identifier |
| `subtotal_amount` | Integer | Prices of experience and add-ons (before fees, taxes, tips) |
| `service_fee_amount` | Integer | Amount of service fee charged. |
| `taxes_amount` | Integer | Amount of taxes charged |
| `tip_amount` | Integer | Tip amount to be charged (zero if tip is not mandatory) |
| `total_amount` | Integer | Total = subtotal + service fee + taxes + tip |

---

## Common Error Messaging

### Error Response Format

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| errors | Array | An array of entities with error descriptions |
| message | String | Short description of the error |
| requestid | String | Unique request ID for tracing/debugging purposes with OpenTable support |

---

## Experience Total – Common Errors

| Reason for the Error | Error Message | Code | HTTP Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Invalid or Expired Token | `invalid_token` | invalid_token | 401 Unauthorized |
| No Authorization Token Provided | Authorization token is required to access this resource | — | 403 Forbidden |
| Invalid Restaurant or Experience ID | Restaurant identifier or Experience Identifier is invalid | InvalidRidOrExperienceId | 400 Bad Request |
| Missing Experience ID | Required Long parameter ‘experience_id’ is not present | MissingExpId | 400 Bad Request |

item.description: # **Description** **OVERVIEW** Experiences allow restaurants to use their availability for special events. From happy hour to winery tours, $1 oysters to exquisite 12-course tasting menus and more. When restaurants offer Experiences on OpenTable, the Voice AI API will allow you to book, confirm and even collect deposits. Availability for the experiences are real-time and reservations are confirmed in the system once the reservation is completed. You can choose to show only standard availability or also include experiences by using the `include_experiences` query parameter. **HOW WILL THIS IMPACT RESTAURANTS?** While these APIs are optional, restaurants that offer experiences will have inventory dedicated to the experiences. Here is how it looks on OpenTable.com for a restaurant with an experience and standard availability.

item.name: Slot Lock (aka Booking Lock)

header.value: bm_sv=F4EC3569D5235DFAC0139CF3428A35BA~YAAQSXQyF78WaC+XAQAAsiBPQRzMqV54qLWVZHeugIN4t7s9K2HZdSUjX+a9IvVB8zbmegCPS3fHgHrkCqW9gqlAV8Q8yaBWUIAo2dZJj8ySmC/CUlUUSKUo12GMPVZL5dyiy9ocmZN/twTHZvjm86KAv+6TnwVx7nDM6Ripc5Al723XjVloaz73m/hlmTgYntuH+BgaieGkrWSXbio0x3zrT0oUwlvmNTIFhZ0vbVi2QqDa4Qs5eeKxJwoug5Ta5GLkpw==~1; Domain=.opentable.com; Path=/; Expires=Thu, 05 Jun 2025 18:54:39 GMT; Max-Age=2285; Secure

item.description: # **Description** ## **OVERVIEW** Slot Locks allow for the inventory that has been chosen by the booker to be locked for a total of 5 minutes. This allows that no other user can book the reservation while the initial booker completes their transaction. If a booking is attempted after the slot lock expiry, the booking can be re-tried, but availability is not guareenteed. Availability has already been verified via the APIs, this step will block the availability for the diner and allow for booking. The five minute window is hard coded and cannot be edited. ### **HOW WILL THIS IMPACT RESTAURANTS?** Restaurant availability will be blocked for that specific user for the allotment of the slot lock window. ## **Endpoint Details** - **Method:** `POST` - **URL:** `{{base-url}}/inhouse/v1/booking/{{rid}}/locks` ## **Path Parameter** | Name | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | `RID` | long | Restaurant ID | ## **Request Body** The body of the request must be a JSON object containing the following fields: | Name | Type | Description | Required | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | `restaurant_id` | Int | The unique identifier for the restaurant. Must match the `rid` in the URL | Yes | | `party_size` | Int | Party Size (must be <= 20) | Yes | | `date_time` | String | The desired date and time for the reservation, formatted as `yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm` | Yes | | `table_type` | String | The preferred type of table. If not specified, the system will default to `default`. Accepted values:`default, highTop, outdoor, bar, counter` | No | | `dining_area_id` | Int | No, if not sent, it shall default it to `default` | No | | `experience_id` | Int | This is the experience ID returned from the Availability and Active Experience endpoints | No | Example Request Body for Slot Locks ``` json { "restaurant_id": 1234, "party_size": 2, "date_time": "2025-07-28T12:45", "table_type": "default", "dining_area_id": 1 "experience_id": 542699 # If applicable } ```
