item.description: ## Description: Creates a new reservation at the specified restaurant. This endpoint supports both **standard** and **experience-based** reservations. Depending on the payload, OpenTable will validate availability, pricing, guest information, and—if applicable—any associated experience rules (e.g., prepaid charges or deposit holds). ## How will this impact restaurants? This will create a phone reservation with all applicable details in the OpenTable for Restaurants table management software. ## **Endpoint Details** - **Method:** `POST` - **URL:** `{{base-url}}/inhouse/v1/booking/{{rid}}/reservations` ## **Path Parameter** | Name | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | `RID` | long | Restaurant ID | ## **Request Body (Standard)** The body of the request must be a JSON object containing the following fields: | Name | Type | Description | Required | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | `first_name` | String | The first name of the primary diner. | Yes | | `last_name` | String | The last name of the primary diner. | Yes | | `phone_number` | Object | An object containing the diner's phone number and country code. | Yes | | `restaurant_id` | String | The unique identifier for the restaurant. Must match the `{rid}` in the URL. | Yes | | `special_request` | String | Any special requests from the diner (e.g., "allergy to nuts," "window seat if possible"). | No | | `reservation_token` | String | The unique token received from the Slot Lock endpoint. This token contains the details of the held slot (party size, date/time, etc.). | Yes | | `sms_notifications_opt_in` | Boolean | Set to true if the user agrees to receive SMS notifications about their booking. Set to false otherwise. | Yes | | `dining_area_id` | Int | This is the ID returned from the availability and metadata endpoint | No | | `table_type` | String | The same dining area can contain different table types | No | ### The `phone_number` Object | Name | Type | Description | Required | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | `number` | String | The phone number of primary contact | Yes | | `country_code` | Int | The country code of phone number from above | Yes | **Note:** please reach out to you account manager if you're interested in using this. Example Request Body for Make Reservations (Standards) ``` json { "first_name": "Savannah", "last_name": "GourmetExplorer", "phone": { "number": "4153444200", "country_code": 1 }, "special_request": "Date night", "restaurant_id": "1038007", "reservation_token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIyMTA0NzI2NzU1fDR8MjAyNC0wMy0wOVQxNjowMHwxMDM4MDA3In0.bxxF9zraEtxu9OIVMjfJm-WPZ9J2xicurQpIO8MFx384Dg4sz6zxyjs5itZt4gVvL7NiAj029cmjcqK74fiJ2Q", "table_type": "default", "dining_area_id": 1, "sms_notifications_opt_in": true } ``` ## **Request Body (with Experiences)** ### The `experience` object When a booking is for a specific experience, the `experience` object must be added to the body of the standard Make Reservation request and must include the following nested fields | Name | Type | Description | Required | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | `id` | Int | The unique identifier for the dining experience. Can be found by calling Active Experiences API | Yes | | `version` | Int | The version number of the experience being booked. Can be found by calling Active Experiences API | Yes | | `party_size_per_price_type` | Array | An array specifying how the total `party_size` is broken down into different pricing tiers or ticket types. Set this to `null` if `"price_type": "PER_PARTY"` you find this from Get Active Experiences endpoint | Yes | ### The `party_size_per_price_type` Array **Note:** For free experiences omit this array but keep the `experience` object with just the `id` and `version` Each object in the `party_size_per_price_type` array contains: | Name | Type | Description | Required | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | `id` | Int | The identifier for the specific price type (e.g., 799237 might represent an 'Adult' ticket). Can be found by calling Active Experiences API and it's named `price_id` | Yes | | `count` | Int | This is equal to `party_size`. If the experience has different prices for example, adults and kids, make sure to list them all. Can be found by calling Active Experiences API | Yes | Example Request Body for Make Reservation with Experiences - This reservation is for 6 people. ``` json { "first_name": "Savannah", "last_name": "GourmetExplorer", "phone": { "number": "4153444200", "country_code": 1 }, "special_request": "Date night", "restaurant_id": "1038007", "reservation_token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIyMTA0NzI2NzU1fDR8MjAyNC0wMy0wOVQxNjowMHwxMDM4MDA3In0.bxxF9zraEtxu9OIVMjfJm-WPZ9J2xicurQpIO8MFx384Dg4sz6zxyjs5itZt4gVvL7NiAj029cmjcqK74fiJ2Q", "table_type": "default", "dining_area_id": 1, "sms_notifications_opt_in": true, "experience": { "id": 542724, "version": 1, "party_size_per_price_type": [ { "id": 12345, "count": 4 }, { "id": 321, "count": 2 } ] } } ```

item.name: Policies

request.description:
## Booking Policies API Documentation

### Endpoint

`{{base-url}}/v2/booking-policies/{{rid}}/{YEAR-MONTH-DAY}/{TIME}/{PartySize}`

### Path Parameters

- **rid (restaurant id)**: A static identifier for each restaurant.
    
- **date (YEAR-MONTH-DAY)**: Specifies the booking date. Example: `2024-12-01`.
    
- **time (TIME)**: Specifies the time of the reservation. Must be in 15-minute intervals (`7:00`, `7:15`, `7:30`, `7:45`).
    
- **PartySize**: Specifies the number of guests for the booking policy query.
    

### Query Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| dinerContext | string | Must be set to `details` only. |

### Response Elements

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Policies | array | Contains available policies |

### Policies Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Message | string | Describes any available messages. |
| Type | string | Usually `General`. Represents the policy type. |
| Language | array | Specifies the language details of the policy. |

### Language Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Code | string | Language code. |
| Region | string | Region associated with language. |
| IETF | string | IETF language code format. |

## Cancellation Policies API Documentation

### Endpoint

**URL:** `{{base-url}}/v2/cancellation-policies/{rid}/{cancelationid}`

**Method:** GET

### Description

The cancellation policy endpoint allows you to retrieve the cancellation policy for a reservation based on the restaurant ID and cancellation ID. The `cancelationid` is obtained from the response of the GET Availability call.

### Responsne (Deposit)

``` json
{
    "partySize": 5,
    "policyType": "Deposit",
    "depositDetails": {
        "amount": 1500,
        "currency": "USD",
        "denominator": 100,
        "type": "PerGuest"
    },
    "cutOff": {
        "cutoffType": "DaysBefore",
        "daysBeforeCutoff": 2
    }
}

 ```

### Response Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `partySize` | Int | The number of guests for the reservation. |
| `policyType` | String | Type of cancellation policy. |
| `depositDetails` | Object | Details of the deposit policy. |
| `depositDetails.amount` | Int | Deposit amount in minor currency units. |
| `depositDetails.currency` | String | Currency of the deposit amount. |
| `depositDetails.denominator` | Int | Conversion factor for minor units. |
| `depositDetails.type` | String | Specifies if the deposit is per guest. |
| `cutOff` | Object | Cutoff policy details. |
| `cutOff.cutoffType` | String | Types of cut off: CancellableAnytime, DaysBefore, DayOfReservation, NotCancellable |
| `cutOff.daysBeforeCutoff` | Int | Number of days before cutoff. |

### Respone (Hold)

``` json
{
    "name": "CC Hold",
    "partySize": 2,
    "policyType": "Hold",
    "holdDetails": {
        "cancellationPenaltyAmount": 5,
        "guestCountChangePenaltyAmount": 5
    },
    "cutOff": {
        "cutoffType": "DaysBefore",
        "daysBeforeCutoff": 2
    },
    "schedules": [
        {
            "priority": 2,
            "startDate": "2026-02-13",
            "endlate": "2028-01-01",
            "startTime": "12:00",
            "endTime": "13:45"
        }
    ]
}

 ```

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `name` | String | Name of the policy |
| `partySize` | Int | The number of guests where the policy start taking place. E.g if it's 3 and your reservation for 2 people, the policy doesn't apply |
| `policyType` | String | Type of cancellation policy. |
| `holdDetails` | Object | Details of the policy |
| `holdDetails.cancellationPenaltyAmount` | Int | Panalty for cancelling outside the cancellation window or not showing up. In whole numbers. E.g. if it's 5, it's 5 USD, GBP, EUR etc |
| `holdDetails.guestCountChangePenaltyAmount` | Int | Penatly for changing the party size. In whole numbers. E.g. if it's 5, it's 5 USD, GBP, EUR etc |
| `cutOff` | Object | Cutoff policy details. |
| `cutOff.cutoffType` | String | Types of cut off: CancellableAnytime, DaysBefore, DayOfReservation, NotCancellable |
| `cutOff.daysBeforeCutoff` | Int | Number of days before cutoff. |
| `schedules.priority` | String | Priority level for this policy. Higher priority policies win if multiple apply at same time. |
| `schedules.startDate` | String | First calendar date this CC Hold policy can apply. |
| `schedules.endDate` | String | Last calendar date this CC Hold policy can apply (inclusive). |
| `schedules.startTime` | String | Earliest reservation time (inclusive) where this policy applies each active day. |
| `schedules.endTime` | String | Latest reservation time (exclusive). A booking at 13:44 is included; a booking at 13:45 is not. |

### Notes

- Ensure you use a valid `cancelationPolicy.id` retrieved from the Make Reservation or Availability call.
    
- The cutoff policy specifies the period within which cancellations are allowed.
    
- The deposit amount is specified in minor currency units (e.g., cents for USD).
    
- Amount for desposit is in fractional unit. For hold, it's in whole numbers
    
- Remember that cancelling `hold` or `deposit` reservations outside the cancellation is not supported through this API

item.description:
**OVERVIEW**

Booking Policies allow the restaurants to expose any specific details they would like the diner to know when they book the reservation.

These details should be exposed when the diner is confirming the reservation so that they understand the terms of the reservations. These can be as simple as "We offer valet parking..." to "Deposit of $50.00 will be collected if there is a no show".

A few notes:

- Booking and Cancellation Policies can vary by date and party size.
    
- If no policies are available, the APIs will return an empty response.

item.description:
# Booking – Cancel

## Overview

This API allows partners to **cancel an existing reservation**. Cancellations may be subject to cancellation policies such as credit card holds or deposits. Depending on the policy and timing, the cancellation may either be processed automatically or require human intervention.

---

## Endpoint

**Method:** `DELETE`  
**URL:** `{{base-url}}/inhouse/v1/booking/{{rid}}/reservations/{{reservation-id}}`

---

### Path Parameters

| Name | Type | Description | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| rid | Int | OpenTable Restaurant Identifier | Yes |
| id | string | Unique ID corresponding to reservation | Yes |

---

## Cancellation Policy Considerations

Cancellation behavior is determined by the **cancellation window** defined in the reservation’s policy. This window specifies the timeframe during which diners can cancel **without losing deposits or incurring charges**.

If a cancellation is attempted **after** the window has closed, the diner may forfeit their deposit or be charged, depending on the restaurant’s terms. In these cases, OpenTable may not be able to complete the cancellation directly.

| Use Case | Result |
| --- | --- |
| Canceling reservation with no policies | OpenTable handles |
| Canceling CC hold reservation **within** cancellation window | OpenTable handles |
| Canceling CC hold reservation **outside** cancellation window | Redirect to Host |
| Canceling deposit reservation **within** cancellation window | OpenTable handles |
| Canceling deposit reservation **outside** cancellation window | Redirect to Host |

When cancellation is **not supported automatically**, the API will return a `403 Forbidden` with error code `RedirectToHost`, indicating that the restaurant must be contacted directly.

---

## Response Codes

| Status Code | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 204 | Reservation canceled successfully |
| 404 | Restaurant ID or Reservation ID not valid |
| 500 | Unexpected internal server error |

---

## Error Structure

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| errors | array | An array of objects with `code` and `message` |
| message | string | Short description of the issue |
| requestid | string | Unique OT-RequestId for support/debugging |

**Example:**

``` json
{
  "errors": [
    {
      "code": "InvalidRidOrReservationId",
      "message": "Restaurant id or reservation id is invalid"
    }
  ],
  "requestid": "8414fadb-eac6-4869-80aa-3ee999974cd7"
}

 ```

---

### Common Errors

| Reason | Error Message | Code | Status Code |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Invalid or expired token | `invalid_token` | `invalid_token` | 401 Unauthorized |
| Missing authorization token | `Authorization token is required to access this resource` | — | 403 Forbidden |
| Invalid restaurant or reservation ID | `Restaurant id or reservation id is invalid` | `InvalidRidOrReservationId` | 400 Bad Request |
| Human intervention is required to cancel reservation | `Human intervention is needed to complete the request` | `RedirectToHost` | 403 Forbidden |

---

item.description:
## Booking – Lookup

### Overview

This API endpoint allows partners to search for and identify diners who have upcoming reservations at a specific restaurant. By submitting a phone number partners can retrieve a list of matching reservations. Additional optional parameters, such as reservation date or diner’s first and last name, can be included to narrow the search and improve accuracy.

---

### Endpoint

**Method:** POST

**URL:** `{{base-url}}/inhouse/v1/booking/{{rid}}/reservations/search`

---

### Path Parameters

| Name | Type | Description | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| rid | int32 | OpenTable Restaurant ID | Yes |

---

### Request Body Parameters

| Name | Type | Description | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| phone_number | string | Phone number of the diner (without country code). | Yes |
| country_code | int32 | Country calling code. | Yes |
| date | string | Reservation date in `yyyy-MM-dd` format. | No |
| first_name | string | First name of the diner. Optional; improves search specificity. | No |
| last_name | string | Last name of the diner. Optional; improves search specificity. | No |

**Notes:**

- The reservations being returned is NOT a guarantee that any given update request will succeed; the client must at least execute a subsequent availability check to find available times for the reservation. However, certain internal conditions, such as a change in cancellation policy (for example, if the time or party size change), may still cause the update to fail. Also, currently we do not distinguish in the response whether a reservation is updatable or cancellable.
    
- OpenTable will retrieve all reservations associated with the phone number provided on the specified date(00:00-23:59) in the request. It is the responsibility of the partners to accurately identify the specific reservation the diner is calling about.
    

---

### Behavior and Limitations

- The endpoint performs **exact match searches** based on the input data.
    
- Does not return first and last name.
    
- The response does **not explicitly indicate** whether a reservation is updatable or cancellable.
    

---

### Response Structure

Returns an array of matching reservations with the following attributes:

| Attribute | Description |
| --- | --- |
| confirmation_number | Restaurant-specific reservation code (not globally unique). |
| reservation_id | OpenTable’s unique reservation identifier. |
| restaurant_id | OpenTable Restaurant ID. |
| date_time | Local time of the reservation at the restaurant. |
| party_size | Number of guests. |
| special_request | Diner-provided notes or requests. |
| status | Indicates if the reservation is modifiable (`Modifiable` or `NotModifiable`). |
| experience_id | ID of the associated experience, if applicable. |

---

### Error Handling

If the request fails, the response includes:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| errors | array | Contains error objects with a `message` and optional `code`. |
| message | string | Short description of the issue. |
| requestid | string | Unique OpenTable request ID for support/debugging. |

---

### Common Errors

| Reason | Message | Code | HTTP Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Invalid or expired token | `invalid_token` | `invalid_token` | 401 Unauthorized |
| Missing authorization token | `Authorization token is required to access this resource` | — | 403 Forbidden |
| Illegal phone number format | `phone number is not valid` | `IllegalPhoneNumber` | 400 Bad Request |
| Missing phone entity | `Phone cannot be null` | `MissingPhoneNumber` | 400 Bad Request |

---

item.description:
## Booking – Change

### Overview

The Change API allows partners to **modify an existing reservation** by updating fields such as date/time, party size, or special requests. However, OpenTable cannot guarantee the success of the modification due to various constraints (e.g., restaurant closure, slot unavailability, or policy changes).

To ensure a seamless user experience, it is **strongly recommended to check availability** for the requested changes prior to invoking this endpoint. In most flows, a `search` or `lookup` call should precede a change request.

---

### Endpoint

**Method:** `PUT`  
**URL:** `/booking/{rid}/reservations/{id}`

---

### Path Parameters

| Name | Type | Description | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| rid | int32 | OpenTable Restaurant ID | Yes |
| id | string | Unique reservation identifier | Yes |

---

### Request Body Parameters

| Name | Type | Description | Required |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| party_size | int32 | Must be between 1 and 20. | Yes |
| date_time | string | New reservation time in `yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm` format. | Yes |
| special_request | string | Optional diner notes or requests. | No |

---

### Cancellation Policy Transitions

Updating a reservation may involve a change in the **cancellation policy**, which can impact whether the update is allowed automatically. Some transitions are permitted by OpenTable; others require manual intervention and will return an error.

| Original Reservation | Updated Reservation | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| No Policies | No Policies | OpenTable handles |
| No Policies | CC Hold | Redirect to Host |
| No Policies | Deposit | Redirect to Host |
| CC Hold | CC Hold | OpenTable handles |
| CC Hold | Deposit | Redirect to Host |
| CC Hold | No Policies | Redirect to Host |
| Deposit (Same Policy) | Deposit (Same Policy) | OpenTable handles |
| Deposit | CC Hold | Redirect to Host |
| Deposit | No Policies | Redirect to Host |
| Deposit | Different Deposit Policy | Redirect to Host |
| Deposit (Party Size X) | Deposit (Party Size < X) | Redirect to Host |
| Deposit (Party Size X) | Deposit (Party Size > X) | Redirect to Host |
| CC Hold (Party Size X) | CC Hold (Party Size < X) | OpenTable handles |
| CC Hold (Party Size X) | CC Hold (Party Size > X) | OpenTable handles |

When a transition is not automatically supported, the API will return a `403 Forbidden` response with error code `RedirectToHost`, indicating that **human intervention** (e.g., contact with the restaurant) is required.

---

### Response Structure

| Attribute | Description |
| --- | --- |
| confirmation_number | Restaurant-specific confirmation code (not globally unique). |
| reservation_id | Unique OpenTable reservation ID. |
| date_time | Updated reservation date and time. |
| party_size | Updated number of guests. |
| special_request | Diner’s updated special request, if provided. |

---

### Error Handling

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| errors | array | Array of error objects with `code` and `message`. |
| message | string | Short description of the issue. |
| requestid | string | Unique OpenTable request ID for debugging or support reference. |

---

### Common Errors

| Reason | Error Message | Code | HTTP Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Invalid or expired token | `invalid_token` | `invalid_token` | 401 Unauthorized |
| No authorization token provided | `Authorization token is required to access this resource` | — | 403 Forbidden |
| Invalid party size | `Party size must be from 1 to 20` | `MissingPartySize` | 400 Bad Request |
| Invalid restaurant or reservation ID | `Restaurant id or reservation id is invalid` | `InvalidRidOrReservationId` | 400 Bad Request |
| Invalid date_time format | `date_time should be of type LocalDateTime` | `InvalidDateTime` | 400 Bad Request |
| Reservation update not at least 15 minutes in the future | `You can only search/update for a slot which is 15 minutes after the current time at the restaurant` | `InvalidStartDateTime` | 400 Bad Request |
| Change in cancellation policy not automatically handled | `Need human intervention` | `RedirectToHost` | 403 Forbidden |
| Attempt to update a reservation in the past | `Cannot modify reservation in past` | `CannotModifyReservationInPast` | 400 Bad Request |

item.name: FAQs
item.description:
### **Does a reservation booked online show in the Booking Lookup Endpoint?**

_Yes, all future, active reservations, regardless of booking method, will be returned. Past or cancelled reservations will not be included._

### **Is it possible to change or cancel reservations returned in the Booking Lookup Endpoint?**

_The system returns all future reservations, but does not differentiate between updatable and cancellable reservations in the response. OpenTable retrieves all reservations associated with the provided phone number for the specified date (00:00-23:59). Partners are responsible for accurately identifying the specific reservation the diner is referencing._

_**Note: Experience reservations are not cancellable or changeable by the APIs.**_

### **Why is the API not returning availability when it is visible online?**

_Restaurants have the option to segment their inventory into categories such as “All Availability,” “Online Reservations,” “In-House Reservations,” or “Walk-In.” Ensure restaurant settings align with desired availability displays._

### **Should the experience_id always be sent?**

_The experience_id should only be sent if the diner is specifically booking an experience. Otherwise, it should be omitted._

### **What occurs if cancellation is attempted after the cancellation window?**

_The booking will not be cancelled, and a 403 RedirectToHost error will be returned._

### **Is pricing handled uniformly across all bookings?**

_No, pricing varies. Standard bookings may involve only a card hold. Experiences may require prepaid or deposit charges._

### **What details should be communicated to the diner when booking a reservation?**

_For Experiences: Communicate the experience name, description, and pricing (total or per guest). For all reservations: Communicate cancellation and refund policies._

### **What’s the difference between deposit and prepaid?**

| Type | Charge Timing | Refundable? |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Prepaid | At booking | No after window |
| Deposit | At booking | Sometimes |
| Card Hold | Only on cancel/no-show | Yes if honored |

### **Summary Table: Experience vs. Standard Bookings**

| Feature | Standard Booking | Experience Booking |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Uses `/reservations` | ✅ | ✅ |
| Requires Experience ID | ❌ | ✅ |
| Prepaid allowed? | ❌ | ✅ |
| Prone to `RedirectToHost` | ⚠ | ✅ |
| Modifiable | ✅ | ❌ |
| Requires pricing validation | ❌ | ❌ |

### How does a restaurant capture credit card details? For certain reservations, credit card details are mandatory.

_OpenTable provides a secure method for acquiring these details and manages all necessary credit card processing. A link will be provided to the guest to enter their card information and remit the deposit._

### How do restaurants check the status of credit card deposits and payments?

_Restaurants use the reservation listing in the Front of House system to see deposit status and balances, and can generate a Reservation Report to review credit card status for many reservations, using the Payment Details column to filter by Prepayment Method, Status, and Paid Total._

### How are reservations booked via the API distinguished from standard phone interactions?

_API-originated reservations are identified with an "_phone/in-housed_" tag in reservation history details. The Reservation Report displays "_phone/in-house_" as the booking source for API reservations, as opposed to "Phone/In-House" for standard bookings._

item.description: ## As a diner, I would like to cancel a reservation via the phone. APIs to use: In House Booking API: - Diner Lookup API - Cancellation Endpoint _**Note: The APIs do not currently support the ability to cancel or change Experience reservations, only Standard Reservations.**_

item.name: In House Booking APIs (aka Voice AI) - Tips for Successful Implementation
item.description:
# Overview

The In-House Booking API facilitates the seamless booking of reservations directly into a restaurant's inventory, encompassing both In-House and Online availability. This powerful API is specifically engineered to support a range of reservation management functionalities, particularly for Voice AI driven reservation systems.

Primarily developed to power Voice AI reservation systems, this API is designed for efficiency and integration with advanced conversational interfaces. Its architecture supports automated and real-time interactions, crucial for voice-based booking experiences.

Partners are expected to develop solutions for Voice AI, Onboarding and offboard functionality, with the onboarding process for restaurants to opt into your integration being crucial to our partnership. We offer two methods for this: OAuth flow or Unique Identifier flow.

Please note that we require product certification and full onboarding flow for go-live. To view detailed documentation, please visit - docs.opentable.com.

## Details and Documentation

In House Booking API (aka Voice Al API): The only APIs you have access to are under In House Booking API (aka Voice Al API) -> APIs

## **Helpful Tips**

- **Search Window:** Although not required, the maximum number of minutes forwards and backwards to search for times is 180 +/-, giving you a significant window -- Does your implementation allow for a maximized search window or have you accounted for actively limiting the search results?
    
- **Dining Areas:** Although not required, Dining Areas are part of over 90% of our current restaurant base
    
- **SMS opt-in:** Ensuring diner SMS opt-in is essential for successful reservation communications, including confirmations and credit card details. Capture this information as part of the required diner details.
    
- **Reservations/Experiences that require deposit or CC hold:** make sure to inform the diner about the amount and for deposit reservations and CC details are required for hold reservations before the reservation is made. OpenTable must send these transactional SMS
    
- **Experiences:** Make sure your LLM can handle the following experiences IDs (pre prod) for RID `1040230` - The experiences are the same production but with a different ID:
    
    - **`518093`**: This experience has multiple `priceTitles` (these titles can be anything) and mandatory tips. `priceId`, 129261 has `priceAllInclusive` = `false`. Meaning that it has taxes and service which affects the total price
        
    - **`129260`**: This experience is per party, the request body is slighty different.
        
    - **`518092`**: The name is `Breakfast - Pay at the restaurant` make sure your LLM is not relying on the name to inform the diner that they must pay at the restaurant. (use the `price` array)
        
    - **`518091`**: Experiences with no policy inherits the restaurant level/default policy only when the policy is `“hold”`. Make your LLM can handle it on Mondays where there is no policy and Friday to Sunday where there’s a `“hold”` policy.
        

## **Testing Environment**

Below are some Test Restaurant ID’s (RIDs) that you can use for API. These are meant to be used for Pre-Production testing only. We have also included some nuances around availability and requirements for each. This should help you test numerous scenarios.

**Note we consider the following days “Special Days”:**

- Thanksgiving Day (US Observance date)
    
- Christmas Eve
    
- Christmas Day
    
- New Year's Day
    
- Memorial Day
    
- Independence Day
    
- Labor Day
    

**RID : 264001**

- Party Size: 1-20
    
- Shift: 6AM-2AM, Sun-Sat
    
- Special Days closed
    

**RID 1040230: With Credit Card Requirements and Experiences**

- Party Size: 1-20
    
- Reservations:
    
    - Monday: standard
        
    - Tues to Thur: deposit | party size > 2
        
    - Fri to Sun: credit card hold | party size > 2
        
    - Experiences: everyday 6am-12am
        
    - Shifts: 6AM-12PM, 1PM-5PM, 6PM-11.30PM.
        

**RID 268549:**

- Party Size: 1-10
    
- Shifts: 7AM-6PM, 8PM-12AM, Sun-Sat
    
- Special Days closed
    

**RID 138712:**

- Party Size: 1-6
    
- Shifts: 8AM-2AM, Sun & Sat only
    
- Special Days closed
    

## Certification Process

OpenTable will need to review and approve your pre-production implementation of the API prior to granting production publication. A minimum window of 14 days for OpenTable to review a pre-production submission is required. We use RID 1040230 for our testing, so please provide a phone number for us to call.

An account to your platform will be useful to test the onboarding flow and to point you to the right transcripts in case any errors during the certification.

### Requirements:

### Scenarios and Validation:

#### **Availability Search**

- Confirm the partner can call /availability with various party sizes and date/times.
    
- Confirm response includes accurate availability including experience options when requested.
    
- Confirm response when no availability is present
    

#### **Experience Retrieval**

- Confirm partners can successfully retrieve /experiences for a restaurant.
    
- Validate correct explanation of price types, prepaid flags, and cancellation policies.
    

#### **Reservation Creation**

- Test creating standard bookings without experience fields.
    
- Test creating experience bookings with valid experience_id and correct price.
    
- Confirm booking with prepaid and deposit logic behaves as expected.
    

#### Reservation Modification

- Test modifying standard bookings with date/time/party size/special request updates.
    
- Test modifying experience bookings, including changes triggering RedirectToHost.
    
- Confirm changes fail if reservation is in the past.
    

#### Reservation Cancellation

- Test cancellation of standard bookings with no policies – expect success (204).
    
- Test cancellation of bookings with CC holds or deposits within and outside cancellation windows – confirm correct RedirectToHost errors where applicable.
    

#### Additional Validation

- Verify Lookup API behavior when multiple reservations in the future are present
    
- Verify correct attribution of reservations to partner ID
    

## Support

OpenTable is committed to ensuring that we have a successful partnership, during all phases of the partnership. Please ensure to go through the FAQs and Known Limitations before asking any questions

- **Partnership Questions:** Questions about the contract and general partnership terms, including marketing opportunities, contact your Partnership Manager
    
- **Integration Onboarding Help:** OpenTables shares a document that is accessible by partners. Questions will be answered as soon as possible, but usually take 3-5 business days.
    
- **Ongoing Support (post Go-Live):** For any technical support post implementation. Please email [api@opentable.com](https://mailto:api@opentable.com) Emails will automatically create a ticket in our support queue. Questions will be answered as soon as possible, but usually take 3-5 business days.
    
- **Outages and Maintenance Windows:** Please visit and subscribe to alerts on our status website. status-api.opentable.com

## **Overview:**

The In-House Booking API facilitates the seamless booking of reservations directly into a restaurant's inventory, encompassing both In-House and Online availability. This powerful API is specifically engineered to support a range of reservation management functionalities, particularly for Voice AI driven reservation systems.

This API also allows partners to book 2 types of reservations: Standard reservations and/or Experiences reservations.

Primarily developed to power Voice AI reservation systems, this API is designed for efficiency and integration with advanced conversational interfaces. Its architecture supports automated and real-time interactions, crucial for voice-based booking experiences.

**Key Endpoints and Functionality:**

- **Availability Search (Standard or Experiences):** Allows for querying the restaurant's availability based on specific criteria, enabling users or systems to check for suitable reservation slots.
    
- **Get Experiences (Experiences only):** Provides the capability to retrieve information about special dining experiences offered by the restaurant, potentially including events, tasting menus, or themed dinners.
    
- **Create Reservation (Standard or Experience):** Supports the creation of new reservations, whether standard dining bookings or reservations for special experiences. This endpoint is critical for registering new customers and securing their dining arrangements.
    
- **Modify Reservation (Standard Only):** Enables users or systems to alter existing reservation details, such as time, party size, or special requests, providing flexibility and accommodating changing plans.
    
- **Cancel Reservation (Standard Only):** Allows for the cancellation of previously made reservations, ensuring inventory is accurately updated and available slots are released for other bookings.
    

## Eligibility and Technical Requirements

This Integration is **available only to approved partners** that have signed the applicable paperwork and agreed to the terms and conditions. Also note before a Voice AI partner is approved, there will be a Quality Assurance process to ensure the integration is successful.

# Known limitations

- Voice AI cannot pass the 2% OpenTable service charge on to guests.
    
- A diner profile is created and identified based on phone number, first and last name.
    
- If there more than 10 guests linked to the same phone number and there are more than 10 active resersation, Lookup API will return 10 random reservations (tends to be the earliest reservations)
    
- **Blocking availability only works for online reservations, for in-house, the shift must be edited under "Availability Planning" and remove in-house or create a "Special Day" in Availability planning, then edit shift settings on that special day, then go to each table type and make that table type "walk-in" only. (Yes, this is more manual so make sure that you as the partner let your customers know this) For more information, go to ->** [https://support.opentable.com/](https://support.opentable.com/)
    
- If the customer is running, this cannot updated through this API
    
- All reservations show as `modifiable`
    
- Requires a mobile number capable of receiving SMS. Landline numbers are not supported.
    
- Ticketed experiences are not supported
    
- For credit card holds or deposits, if `"sms_notifications_opt_in: false"` the reservation gets booked but the diner doesn't get a link to enter the credit card information
    
- Experiences cannot be cancelled or modified through this API

OpenTable provides two distinct Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) for the purpose of reserving tables within OpenTable for Restaurants, its table management software.

The Online Booking API, also referred to as Consumer API, facilitates online reservation processing, contingent upon user acceptance of OpenTable's terms and conditions within a visually accessible interface. Conversely, the In-House Booking API, alternatively known as the Voice AI API, is specifically designed for environments lacking a visual interface, such as voice-activated systems. Although both APIs ultimately accomplish the same objective—the successful creation of a reservation—they differ significantly in methodology, functionality, and intended application.

It is imperative to acknowledge that the utilization of the Voice AI API for online reservations and the Consumer API for voice reservations is strictly prohibited and constitutes a violation of OpenTable's terms of service. Please work with the business development teams for access to and contracting for each of the APIs.

item.name: CRM APIs
Loyalty Programs

request.description:
# CRM v3 - Loyalty API Documentation

## Overview

The Loyalty API enables external partners to manage guest loyalty information within the OpenTable ecosystem. This API allows for adding, managing guest loyalty program data to enhance guest experiences and personalize services.

---

Endpoints: `{{base-url}}/api/v3/external/partners/{partnerId}/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guestId}/loyalties`

#### Purpose

Retrieve the loyalty details for a specific guest in a restaurant.

#### Response Body

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `loyalties` | array | Array of loyalty programs associated with the guest. |
| `accountId` | string | Unique identifier for the loyalty account. |
| `programName` | string | Name of the loyalty program. |
| `loyaltyTier` | string | Loyalty tier of the guest (e.g., `Diamond`, `Gem`). |
| `pointsBalance` | string | Current points balance of the guest in the program. |
| `flexFields` | array | Array of custom fields associated with the loyalty program. |
| `flexFields.label` | string | Label of the custom field (e.g., `Favorite Meal`). |
| `flexFields.value` | string | Value of the custom field (e.g., `Jalapeno & Tomato Pizza`). |

---

## Managing Loyalty Data

### POST: Add or Append Loyalty Data

**URL**: `{{base-url}}/api/v3/external/partners/{partnerId}/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guestId}/loyalties`

#### Purpose

Add new loyalty data or append additional loyalty programs to an existing resource.

#### Request Body

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `loyalties` | array | Array of loyalty programs to be added. |
| `accountId` | string | Unique identifier for the loyalty account. |
| `programName` | string | Name of the loyalty program. |
| `loyaltyTier` | string | Loyalty tier of the guest (e.g., `Diamond`, `Gem`). |
| `pointsBalance` | string | Current points balance of the guest in the program. |
| `flexFields` | array | Array of custom fields associated with the loyalty program. |

---

### PUT: Update Loyalty Data

`{{base-url}}/api/v3/external/partners/{partnerId}/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guestId}/loyalties`

#### Purpose

Update an existing loyalty resource by replacing the entire object. Use this method for deletions as well by omitting fields you wish to remove.

#### Request Body

The structure is identical to the POST method.

---

## Deleting Data

To delete specific loyalty data, use the PUT method with an updated object that excludes the items you wish to delete. Partial deletions are not supported; you must submit the complete object.

---

item.description: ## Description ### Overview This endpoint will allow developers to expose information about diner's loyalty program participation to the OpenTable for Restaurants table management software. These details will show in the Guestbook, Reso Card, Chit and the Owner’s App. ### How will this impact restaurants? Information about the diner’s loyalty program and status will show in the diner card, allowing for additional insight.

item.name: Tag Create, Delete and Post

request.description:
# Tag Management API Documentation

## Overview

The Tag Management API enables external partners to manage guest tags within the OpenTable system. This API allows for the creation, updating, and management of custom tags that can be associated with specific guests, providing flexibility and customization for restaurants to enhance guest relationships and experiences.

---

## Endpoint

### URL

**PUT**: `{{base-url}}/api/v3/external/partners/{partnerId}/restaurants/{restaurantId}/tags/{tagId}`

---

## Request Details

### Headers

| **Header** | **Value** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Specifies that the request body contains JSON data. |
| `Authorization` | `Bearer [AccessToken]` | A valid access token for authenticating the request. |

### Path Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `partnerId` | string | The unique identifier for the partner making the request. |
| `restaurantId` | string | The unique identifier for the restaurant where the tag is being managed. |
| `tagId` | string | The unique identifier for the tag being created or updated. |

### Request Body

The request body must be in JSON format and contain the following fields:

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `displayName` | string | The name of the tag as it will appear in the system. |
| `category` | string | The category under which the tag is grouped. Pick one among the allowed values `special_guests` or `dietary_needs` |

---

## Response Details

### Response Format

The response is returned in JSON format and confirms the successful creation or update of the tag.

#### Response Fields

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | string | The unique identifier of the tag. |
| `displayName` | string | The name of the tag. |
| `category` | string | The category under which the tag is grouped. |

---

## Error Codes

| **Status Code** | **Message** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `400` | Bad Request | The request body is invalid or missing required fields. |
| `401` | Unauthorized | Authentication failed due to an invalid or expired access token. |
| `403` | Forbidden | The client does not have permission to access the specified resource. |
| `404` | Not Found | The specified tag, partner, or restaurant could not be found. |
| `409` | Conflict | Attempt to update a secondary guest. Please update the primary guest instead. (primaryGuestId is returned to store and update) |
| `500` | Internal Server Error | An unexpected error occurred on the server. |

---

## Notes

- Ensure that the `partnerId`, `restaurantId`, and `tagId` are valid and correspond to existing records in the OpenTable system.
    
- Use meaningful `displayName` and `category` values to streamline guest classification and management.

- If a guest profile is merged and there is an attempt to update the secondary `guestId`, a 409 conflict error will occur. The error will return the `primaryGuestId` of the merged guest profile. This `guestId` which should be stored as the primary `guestId` for all future updates.

# Tag Listing API Documentation

## Overview

The Tag Listing API allows external partners to retrieve a list of all tags associated with a specific restaurant. These tags enable restaurants to classify guests based on specific attributes, preferences, or needs, facilitating a more personalized dining experience.

---

## Endpoint

### URL

**GET**: `{{base-url}}/api/v3/external/partners/{partnerId}/restaurants/{restaurantId}/tags`

---

## Request Details

### Headers

| **Header** | **Value** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Specifies that the request body contains JSON data. |
| `Authorization` | `Bearer [AccessToken]` | A valid access token for authenticating the request. |

### Path Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `partnerId` | string | The unique identifier for the partner making the request. |
| `restaurantId` | string | The unique identifier for the restaurant whose tags are being retrieved. |

---

## Response Details

### Response Format

The response is returned in JSON format and contains a list of tags associated with the specified restaurant.

#### Response Fields

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | string | The unique identifier of the tag. |
| `displayName` | string | The name of the tag as it appears in the system. |
| `category` | string | The category under which the tag is grouped. |

---

## Error Codes

| **Status Code** | **Message** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `400` | Bad Request | The request is invalid or malformed. |
| `401` | Unauthorized | Authentication failed due to an invalid or expired access token. |
| `403` | Forbidden | The client does not have permission to access the specified resource. |
| `404` | Not Found | The specified partner, restaurant, or tags could not be found. |
| `500` | Internal Server Error | An unexpected error occurred on the server. |

---

## Notes

- Ensure that the `partnerId` and `restaurantId` are valid and correspond to existing records in the OpenTable system.
    
- Tags retrieved are specific to the specified restaurant and are not shared across restaurants.

## Endpoint

### URL

**DELETE**: `{{base-url}}/api/v3/external/partners/{partnerId}/restaurants/{restaurantId}/tags/{tagId}`

---

## Request Details

### Headers

| **Header** | **Value** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Specifies that the request body contains JSON data. |
| `Authorization` | `Bearer [AccessToken]` | A valid access token for authenticating the request. |

### Path Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `partnerId` | string | The unique identifier for the partner making the request. |
| `restaurantId` | string | The unique identifier for the restaurant where the tag is being deleted. |
| `tagId` | string | The unique identifier of the tag to be deleted. |

---

## Error Codes

| **Status Code** | **Message** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `400` | Bad Request | The request is invalid or malformed. |
| `401` | Unauthorized | Authentication failed due to an invalid or expired access token. |
| `403` | Forbidden | The client does not have permission to delete the specified tag. |
| `404` | Not Found | The specified tag, partner, or restaurant could not be found. |
| `500` | Internal Server Error | An unexpected error occurred on the server. |

---

## Notes

- Ensure that the `partnerId`, `restaurantId`, and `tagId` are valid and correspond to existing records in the OpenTable system.
    
- This endpoint only deletes the specified tag and does not affect other tags or guest data.

## Endpoint

### URL

**POST**: `{{base-url}}/api/v3/external/partners/{partnerId}/restaurants/{restaurantId}/guests/{guestId}/tags`

---

## Request Details

### Headers

| **Header** | **Value** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Specifies that the request body contains JSON data. |
| `Authorization` | `Bearer [AccessToken]` | A valid access token for authenticating the request. |

### Path Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `partnerId` | string | The unique identifier for the partner making the request. |
| `restaurantId` | string | The unique identifier for the restaurant where the guest tag is being assigned. |
| `guestId` | string | The unique identifier of the guest to whom tags are being assigned. |

### Request Body

The request body must be in JSON format and contain the following field:

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `tags` | array | A list of tag IDs to assign to the guest. |

---

## Response Details

### Response Format

A successful tag assignment returns a `200 OK` status code along with the assigned tags in the response body.

#### Response Fields

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `tags` | array | A list of tag IDs that were successfully assigned to the guest. |

---

## Error Codes

| **Status Code** | **Message** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `400` | Bad Request | The request is invalid or malformed. |
| `401` | Unauthorized | Authentication failed due to an invalid or expired access token. |
| `403` | Forbidden | The client does not have permission to assign tags to the specified guest. |
| `404` | Not Found | The specified partner, restaurant, guest, or tags could not be found. |
| `409` | Conflict | Attempt to update a secondary guest. Please update the primary guest instead. (`primaryGuestId` is returned to store and update) |
| `500` | Internal Server Error | An unexpected error occurred on the server. |

---

## Notes

- Ensure that the `partnerId`, `restaurantId`, `guestId`, and `tags` are valid and correspond to existing records in the OpenTable system.
    
- Tags that do not get created via the API will not be assigned.

- If a guest profile is merged and there is an attempt to update the secondary `guestId`, a 409 conflict error will occur. The error will return the `primaryGuestId` of the merged guest profile. This `guestId` which should be stored as the primary `guestId` for all future updates.

item.description: ## Description ### Overview This endpoint within the CRM API catalog allows for creating Guest Tags and as well as the ability to assign these Guest Tags to guests. The updated guest information records associated with a specific partner's restaurant will have the data exposed within the guest data in the restaurant’s OpenTable table management software. ### How will this impact restaurants? The CRM tag creation will allow for partners to create and add new tags to guest profiles without the limitation of needing a tag to exist with the restaurant’s OpenTable for Restaurants system. Tags from CRM API will appear on the guest profile and will not be editable.

item.name: Property/Hotel Details

request.description:
## Endpoints

### PUT: Update or Add Guest Properties

**URL**: `{{base-url}}/api/v3/external/partners/{partnerId}/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guestId}/properties`

---

## Request Details

### Headers

| **Header** | **Value** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Specifies that the request body contains JSON data. |
| `Authorization` | `Bearer [AccessToken]` | A valid access token for authenticating the request. |

### Path Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `partnerId` | string | The unique identifier for the partner making the request. |
| `rid` | string | The unique identifier for the restaurant where the guest is managed. |
| `guestId` | string | The unique identifier of the guest whose properties are being updated. |

### Request Body

The request body must be in JSON format and contain the following fields:

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `properties` | array | An array of property details to be associated with the guest. |
| `reservationID` | string | Unique identifier for the reservation. |
| `propertyName` | string | Name of the property associated with the guest. |
| `resStatusCode` | string | Status code of the reservation (e.g., `RESERVED`, `CANCELLED`). |
| `resArriveDate` | string | Arrival date and time in ISO 8601 format (e.g., `2024-06-16T16:00:00.000`). |
| `resDepartDate` | string | Departure date and time in ISO 8601 format (e.g., `2024-06-20T11:00:00.000`). |
| `roomNumber` | string | Room number assigned to the guest. |
| `rateCode` | string | Rate code associated with the reservation. |
| `flexFields` | array | Array of custom fields with additional details about the reservation. |
| `flexFields.label` | string | Label of the custom field (e.g., `Promotions`). |
| `flexFields.value` | string | Value of the custom field (e.g., `Free breakfast`). |

---

## Response Details

### Response Format

A successful request returns a `200 OK` status code along with the updated properties in the response body.

#### Response Fields

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `properties` | array | Array of property details associated with the guest. |
| `reservationID` | string | Unique identifier for the reservation. |
| `propertyName` | string | Name of the property associated with the guest. |
| `resStatusCode` | string | Status code of the reservation (e.g., `RESERVED`, `CANCELLED`). |
| `resArriveDate` | string | Arrival date and time in ISO 8601 format. |
| `resDepartDate` | string | Departure date and time in ISO 8601 format. |
| `roomNumber` | string | Room number assigned to the guest. |
| `rateCode` | string | Rate code associated with the reservation. |
| `flexFields` | array | Array of custom fields with additional details about the reservation. |
| `flexFields.label` | string | Label of the custom field. |
| `flexFields.value` | string | Value of the custom field. |

---

## Notes

- `{partnerId}`, `{rid}`, and `{guestId}` are dynamic path parameters.
    
    - `partnerId`: Assigned by OpenTable during onboarding.
        
    - `rid`: Unique identifier for the restaurant, available via OpenTable.
        
    - `guestId`: Found in the Sync API guest payload; remove the preceding RID from the guest ID.
        
- To delete specific property data, submit the full object excluding the items to be removed (partial deletions are not supported).

item.description: ### **Overview** This API allows for updating guest information records associated with a partner's restaurant. The data is exposed within the guest data in the restaurant’s OpenTable table management software. ### **How will this impact restaurants?** Information about the diner’s property stay will show in the diner card, allowing for additional insight. ### **Change Log** The previous CRM API available was v2. We have also made some slight changes to v3 since we last provided documentation. The following changes have been made since: - New Endpoint URLs - Enhanced security limiting partners to only certain attributes - Removal of the Integrations Object. That information is static and will be kept stored in the OpenTable system. For adjustments to the integration name, please work with OpenTable. These changes were completed in April of 2024.

item.name: Guest Creation and ID Retrieval

request.description:
## Create Guest API Documentation

### Endpoint

`{base-url}/api/v3/external/partners/{partnerId}/restaurants/{rid}/guests`

### Method

**POST**

### Path Parameters

- **partnerId**: A static identifier for the partner assigned by OpenTable after onboarding.
    
- **rid (restaurant id)**: A unique, static identifier for each restaurant provided by OpenTable.
    

### Request Body Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| firstName | string | The first name of the guest. **Required** |
| lastName | string | The last name of the guest. **Required** |
| phoneNumbers | array | An array of phone numbers for the guest. **Required if no email is provided** |
| email | object | The email of the guest. **Required if no phone number is provided** |

### Phone Number Object Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| countryCode | string | The country code for the phone number. **Required** |
| number | string | The phone number. **Required** |
| isPrimary | boolean | Indicates if it is the primary number. Defaults to true if not specified. |

### Email Object Attributes

| Attribute | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| emailAddress | string | The guest's email address. **Required** |
| isEmailMarketingOptedIn | boolean | Indicates the email marketing opt-in preference. Defaults to false. |

### Success and Error Codes

| Message | Reason for Error | Status Code |
| --- | --- | --- |
| OK | Request successful | 200 |
| Bad Request | Your request is invalid | 400 |
| invalid_token | Invalid or expired token provided | 401 |
| Conflict | Attempt to update a secondary guest. Please update the primary guest instead. (primaryGuestId is returned to store and update) | 409 |
| Too many requests | No access available under your account | 429 |

### Notes

- Either `phoneNumbers` or `email` must be provided.
    
- If multiple `isPrimary: true` values are provided in the phone numbers array, only the first one will be retained.
    
- Ensure your `partnerId` and `rid` values are correctly assigned by OpenTable during onboarding.
    
- If a guest profile is merged and there is an attempt to update the secondary guestId, a 409 conflict error will occur. The error will return the primaryGuestId of the merged guest profile. This guestId which should be stored as the primary guestId for all future updates.

item.description: ## Description ### Overview Customer Relations Management API is a set of APIs that exposes additional guest and reservation details from other sources. The APIs will facilitate the addition of details in the diner profile and will leverage the Guest Sync API payload to accurately match and add information to specific guest profiles. This API allows for updating guest information records associated with a partner's restaurant. The data is exposed within the guest data in the restaurant’s OpenTable table management software. ### How will this impact restaurants? The Guest Creation API allows partners to create guest profiles in the OpenTable for Restaurants platform outside of the reservation making process. ### Before guest creation ### After guest creation ## Guest Matching / Creation Logic When the Guest Creation API is called the following logic will be used to match to existing restaurant guest profiles or to create a brand new profile. **Scenario 1: match** Existing guest: FirstName, LastName, Phone A, email A PartnerGuest: FirstName, LastName, Phone A, email A **Scenario 2: match** Existing guest: FirstName, LastName, Phone A, email A PartnerGuest: FirstName, LastName, Phone A, no email **Scenario 3: match** Existing guest: FirstName, LastName, Phone A, email A PartnerGuest: FirstName, LastName, no phone, email A **Scenario 4: no match, creation of a new guest** Existing guest: FirstName, LastName, Phone A, email A PartnerGuest: FirstName, LastName, Phone A, email B **Scenario 5: no match, creation of a new guest** Existing guest: FirstName, LastName, Phone A, email A PartnerGuest: FirstName, LastName, Phone B, email A ## Rate Limit: The maximum allowable rate of API calls at this endpoint is set at 10 calls per second per partner. Please note that other endpoints may have different requirements or limits. ## Change Log These changes were completed in April of 2024.

item.name: Guest Insights
item.description:
## API Documentation for Updating Guest Insights

## Overview

This documentation outlines the process for using the PUT API endpoint to update guest insights for a specific restaurant. It includes the request format, headers, and example payload, as well as the expected response structure.

## Endpoint

**URL**: `{base-url}/api/v3/external/partners/{partner_id}/restaurants/{restaurant_id}/guests/{guest_id}/insights`

**Method**: PUT

## Headers

| Header Name | Description | Example Value |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | Specifies the media type of the request body. | `application/json` |
| `Authorization` | Bearer token for authentication. | `Bearer 7b476eed-13cb-4b38-a357-6cdf9517eb48` |

## Request Parameters

### Path Parameters:

- `partner_id`: The unique identifier of the partner.
    
- `restaurant_id`: The unique identifier of the restaurant.
    
- `guest_id`: The unique identifier of the guest.
    

## Request Body

The request body must include an array of insights, each with a `label` and a `value`.

### Fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `label` | String | The label for the insight (e.g., "Average check size"). |
| `value` | String | The value associated with the label (e.g., "$62"). |

## Response

### Fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `label` | String | The label for the insight (e.g., "Average check size"). |
| `value` | String | The value associated with the label (e.g., "$62"). |

## Notes

- Ensure that the `Authorization` token provided is valid and has sufficient permissions.
    
- All `label` and `value` pairs should be meaningful and relevant to the guest insights.
    
- There is no character limit, but we recomend we keep it as succint as possible
    
- There is no limit on the amount of labels.
    
- Incorrect or missing fields may result in validation errors.
    

## Error Handling

### Common Errors:

| Status Code | Error Message | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 400 | `Invalid request format` | The request body is malformed or missing required fields. |
| 401 | `Unauthorized` | The provided token is invalid or expired. |
| 404 | `Resource not found` | The specified `partner_id`, `restaurant_id`, or `guest_id` does not exist. |

item.name: Guest Photo

request.description:
# CRM V3 - Guest Photo Upload API Documentation

## Overview

The upload of the Guest photos works in asynchronous fashion, meaning that the upload request is acknowledged but the actual upload will happen in the background. The status API will allow partners to monitor the status of the photo.

---

## Endpoint

### **URL**

**PUT**: `{base-url}/api/v3/external/partners/{partner_id}/restaurants/{restaurant_id}/guests/{guest_id}/photos`

---

## Request Details

### Headers

| **Header** | **Value** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Specifies that the request body contains JSON data. |
| `Authorization` | `Bearer [AccessToken]` | A valid access token for authenticating the request. |

### Path Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `partnerId` | string | The unique identifier for the partner making the request. |
| `restaurantId` | string | The unique identifier for the restaurant where the photo is being managed. |
| `guestId` | string | The unique identifier of the guest whose photo is being updated. |

### Request Body

The request body must be in JSON format and contain the following field:

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `url` | string | URL link to the guest photo. JPEG or PNG are only supported formats. |

## Response Details

### Response Format

The response body returned will be empty for a successful photo upoad request.

| **Status Code** | **Message** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `202` | Accepted | Photo upload request acknowledged |
| `400` | Bad Request | Returned when the photo url is empty |

### **Additional Notes**

- Supported photo formats: JPEG, PNG
    
- Minimum supported size: 512px x 512px
    
- One photo per guest (per partner, per rid): uploading a new photo for a guest, will overwrite the previously uploaded one
    
- Every new upload request will delete the previously uploaded photo for guest, regardless of the outcome of the upload (more about this below, in the `status` table)
    
- Partner needs to ensure url is valid for at leat 30 days

}

request.description:
# Guest Photo Delete API Documentation

## Overview

The deletion of the Guest photos works in asynchronous fashion, meaning that the deletion request is acknowledged but the actual deletion will happen in the background.

---

## Endpoint

### **URL**

**DELETE**: `{base-url}/api/v3/external/partners/{partner_id}/restaurants/{restaurant_id}/guests/{guest_id}/photos`

---

## Request Details

### Headers

| **Header** | **Value** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Specifies that the request body contains JSON data. |
| `Authorization` | `Bearer [AccessToken]` | A valid access token for authenticating the request. |

### Path Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `partnerId` | string | The unique identifier for the partner making the request. |
| `restaurantId` | string | The unique identifier for the restaurant where the photo is being managed. |
| `guestId` | string | The unique identifier of the guest whose photo is being updated. |

### Request Body

The request body for the DELETE call is empty.

### Response Format

The response body returned will be empty for a successful photo deletion request.

| **Status Code** | **Message** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `202` | Accepted | Photo deletion request acknowledged |
| `404` | Not Found | The partner photo for the guest doesn’t exist |

## Overview

The photo status API allows for the partner to monitor and check the status of a uploaded or deleted photo. This ApI will also return the timestamp of the latest status of the photo.

## Endpoint

### **URL**

GET: `{base-url}/api/v3/external/partners/{partner_id}/restaurants/{restaurant_id}/guests/{guest_id}/photos`

---

## Request Details

### Headers

| **Header** | **Value** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Specifies that the request body contains JSON data. |
| `Authorization` | `Bearer [AccessToken]` | A valid access token for authenticating the request. |

### Path Parameters

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `partnerId` | string | The unique identifier for the partner making the request. |
| `restaurantId` | string | The unique identifier for the restaurant where the photo is being managed. |
| `guestId` | string | The unique identifier of the guest whose photo is being updated. |

### Request Body

The request body for the GET photo status call is empty.

## Response Details

### Response Format

The response is returned in JSON format and provides the current status of the photo submitted for upload or deletion.

#### Response Fields

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | string | The status of the photo. (see below for all available statuses) |
| `updatedAt` | string | UTC timestamp of the last status update of the photo. |

#### Status Descriptions

| **Status** | **Description** | **Notes** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `PENDING_UPLOAD` | The photo has been queued for upload |  |
| `INVALID` | The upload attempt failed because of problems with the image | This can be related to problems with the image url, or the image being in the wrong format, or the image size being incorrect. |
| `IN_MODERATION` | The photo is in moderation, awaiting approval/rejection |  |
| `REJECTED` | The photo has been rejected by the moderation team |  |
| `APPROVED` | The photo is correctly uploaded, approved and ready to be displayed | The photo might not be necessarily displayed in OpenTable because it’s subject to photo display rules. |
| `DELETED` | The photo has been deleted | This can happen both whether the partner deletes it, or if OpenTable support deletes it upon restaurant request |
| `PENDING_DELETE` | The photo has been queued for deletion |  |

| **Status Code** | **Message** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `200` | OK | The status of the photo is returned |
| `401` | Unauthorized | Authentication failed due to an invalid or expired access token. |
| `404` | Not Found | The guest/partner photo for the guest doesn’t exist |

request.description:
# OpenTable Guest Sync API Documentation ## Overview The OpenTable Guest Sync API allows users to retrieve detailed information about guest profiles associated with a restaurant. --- ## Endpoint **URL**: `{{base-url}}/sync/v2/guests` **Method:** GET ## Request Details ### Headers | **Header** | **Value** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Specifies that the request body contains JSON data. | | `Authorization` | `Bearer [AccessToken]` | A valid access token for authenticating the request. | ### Query Parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | `rid` | integer | A unique, static identifier for each restaurant. Required | | `email_optin` | boolean | List searches to guests that have the email optin flag set to true or false | | `updated_after` | string | Filters guests updated after the specified ISO 8601 timestamp. (UTC time) | | `offset` | integer | The starting point for pagination. Default is `0`. | | `limit` | integer | The maximum number of guests to return per request. Default is `1000`, maximum number available for return is `2000`. | ### Response Structure The response is a JSON object containing metadata about pagination and an array of guest objects. ### Response Attributes | **Attribute** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | `hasNextPage` | boolean | Indicates if there are additional pages of results. | | `nextPageUrl` | string | URL for the next page of results, if available. | | `offset` | integer | The starting offset for the current page. | | `limit` | integer | The maximum number of records returned in this response. | | `items` | array | An array of guest objects containing detailed information about each guest. | ### Guest Object Attributes | **Attribute** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | `id` | string | Unique identifier for the guest by restaurant. | | `href` | string | A fully qualified URL for the guest. | | `rid` | integer | A unique identifier for the restaurant this guest is associated with. | | `sequence_id` | integer | Sequential identifier for the guest record within OpenTable systems. | | `gpid` | string | Globally unique OpenTable identifier for the guest. | | `first_name` | string | The first name of the guest. This field may be empty for guests associated with anonymous, walk-in or in-house reservations. | | `last_name` | string | The last name of the guest. This field may be empty for guests associated with anonymous, walk-in or in-house reservations. | | `email` | string | Guest's email address. | | `email_optin` | boolean | Indicates if the guest opted into receiving marketing emails from the restaurant. If the value is true then the email may be sent to the user on behalf of the restaurant. If the value is false then the guest should not be sent emails for marketing purposes. | | `phone` | string | Guest's primary phone number. | | `phone_type` | string | Type of the primary phone number (e.g., Mobile, Home, Work). | | `tags` | array or null | Guest tags that have been applied to the diner profile. | | `birth_date` | string or null | Guest's date of birth, if available. | | `anniversary_date` | string or null | Guest's anniversary date, if available. | | `updated_at` | string | Timestamp of the last update to the guest profile (local time). | | `forgotten` | boolean | The user has requested to have their diner info forgotten. Partners are responsible for removing the diner’s information from all systems where it may be stored. This is a requirement mandated by EU for compliance with General Data Protection Regulation GDPR. | | `archived` | boolean | This flag indicates whether the guest is archived. If true, all attributes aside from primary_guest, id and href would be null for this archived guest. | | `primary_guest` | string or null | This attribute is returned when there is another guest record in the restaurant database that has merged with this record, thus there being data on the main, or primary, record. This attribute will signal the guest_id without the leading RID designation of the primary guest record. Otherwise this attribute is null. | | `address` | string or null | The address of guest. address_line1 (string), address_line2 (string), city (string), state (string), zip (string), country (string). | | `mail_opted_in` | boolean | Indicates if the guest opted into physical mail marketing. | | `company_name` | string or null | Name of the company associated with the guest. | | `phone_numbers` | array | List of phone numbers associated with the guest, including labels, country codes, and primary flags. | | `created_date` | string | Timestamp of when the guest profile was created (local time). | | `updated_at_utc` | string | Timestamp of the last update to the guest profile (UTC time). | | `marketing_opted_out` | boolean | This flag indicates whether an OpenTable network diner has opted in or out of data sharing for purposes beyond operational needs of the restaurant. A value of ‘true’ means the diner has elected not to have their current profile and reservation history used by the restaurant or 3rd party for any additional marketing purposes. A value of ‘false’ means the diners has consented to data sharing for any use case highlighted in the OpenTable consumer privacy policy. | | `notes` | string or null | General notes about the guest. | | `notes_special_relationship` | string or null | Restaurant entered diner information highlighting any association to restaurant, chef or group (ex. friend of chef) | | `notes_food_and_drink` | string or null | Restaurant entered diner information identifying dietary considerations in service (ex. wife is gluten free) | | `notes_seating` | string or null | Restaurant entered seating preferences for diner (ex. table 42 when available) | | `created_date_utc` | string | Timestamp of when the guest profile was created (UTC time). | | `date_last_visit` | string or null | Timestamp of the guest's most recent visit (local time). | | `date_first_visit` | string or null | Timestamp of the guest's first visit (local time). | | `date_last_visit_utc` | string or null | Timestamp of the guest's most recent visit (UTC time). | | `date_first_visit_utc` | string or null | Timestamp of the guest's first visit (UTC time). | | `is_hidden` | boolean | his flag indicates if a guest has been deleted (hidden from the standard guestbook). If true, the guest’s profile has been deleted/hidden from the restaurant’s guestbook. | --- ## Error Response for Guests ### Error Messaging The error response DTO returned by the Sync API collections are in the below format. When reporting issues, always provide the OT-RequestId (located in the response header) to allow OpenTable to uniquely identify a specific request. | PARAMETER | DESCRIPTION | | --- | --- | | **timestamp**
`integer` | The unix time stamp of the error. | | **path**
`string` | The end path of the API you are calling. | | **status**
`integer` | The numerical 3 digit code associated to the error. E.g., 400, 404, 409. | | **error**
`string` | The descriptive phrase associated to the error. E.g., Bad Request, Unauthorized, Not Found. | | **message**
`string` | A short user-readable description of the error. |

request.description:
# CRM v3 - Loyalty API Documentation

## Overview

The Loyalty API enables external partners to manage guest loyalty information within the OpenTable ecosystem. This API allows for adding, managing guest loyalty program data to enhance guest experiences and personalize services.

---

Endpoints: `{{base-url}}/api/v3/external/partners/{partnerId}/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guestId}/loyalties`

#### Purpose

Retrieve the loyalty details for a specific guest in a restaurant.

#### Response Body

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `loyalties` | array | Array of loyalty programs associated with the guest. |
| `accountId` | string | Unique identifier for the loyalty account. |
| `programName` | string | Name of the loyalty program. |
| `loyaltyTier` | string | Loyalty tier of the guest (e.g., `Diamond`, `Gem`). |
| `pointsBalance` | string | Current points balance of the guest in the program. |
| `flexFields` | array | Array of custom fields associated with the loyalty program. |
| `flexFields.label` | string | Label of the custom field (e.g., `Favorite Meal`). |
| `flexFields.value` | string | Value of the custom field (e.g., `Jalapeno & Tomato Pizza`). |

---

## Managing Loyalty Data

### POST: Add or Append Loyalty Data

**URL**: `{{base-url}}/api/v3/external/partners/{partnerId}/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guestId}/loyalties`

#### Purpose

Add new loyalty data or append additional loyalty programs to an existing resource.

#### Request Body

| **Field** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `loyalties` | array | Array of loyalty programs to be added. |
| `accountId` | string | Unique identifier for the loyalty account. |
| `programName` | string | Name of the loyalty program. |
| `loyaltyTier` | string | Loyalty tier of the guest (e.g., `Diamond`, `Gem`). |
| `pointsBalance` | string | Current points balance of the guest in the program. |
| `flexFields` | array | Array of custom fields associated with the loyalty program. |

---

### PUT: Update Loyalty Data

`{{base-url}}/api/v3/external/partners/{partnerId}/restaurants/{rid}/guests/{guestId}/loyalties`

#### Purpose

Update an existing loyalty resource by replacing the entire object. Use this method for deletions as well by omitting fields you wish to remove.

#### Request Body

The structure is identical to the POST method.

---

## Deleting Data

To delete specific loyalty data, use the PUT method with an updated object that excludes the items you wish to delete. Partial deletions are not supported; you must submit the complete object.

---

item.description:
**As a restaurateur, I want to enhance the guest experience by integrating loyalty program details from my CRM system into OpenTable. By surfacing this information in the Diner Details view within OpenTable for Restaurants, staff can easily identify and better serve returning or high-value guests.**

**APIs to use:**

- **Sync API – Guests**
    
- **CRM API – Loyalties**
    

**Step 1:**

Retrieve guest data by using the **Sync API** to pull guest information from OpenTable.

**Step 2:**

Store relevant guest attributes in your system for ongoing use and matching. Recommended fields from the guest payload:

1. `id`
    
2. `first_name`
    
3. `last_name`
    
4. `email`
    
5. `phone`
    
6. `updated_at`
    

**Step 3:**

Match with loyalty records by cross-referencing the retrieved OpenTable guest data with your existing loyalty program records.

**Step 4:**

Use the CRM Loyalties endpoint to submit loyalty program details using the guest’s `guest_id` and associated `RID` (restaurant ID). This will update the guest’s profile with loyalty information, making it available to restaurant staff in-platform.
