request.description:
# OpenTable Guest Sync API Documentation ## Overview The OpenTable Guest Sync API allows users to retrieve detailed information about guest profiles associated with a restaurant. --- ## Endpoint **URL**: `{{base-url}}/sync/v2/guests` **Method:** GET ## Request Details ### Headers | **Header** | **Value** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | `Content-Type` | `application/json` | Specifies that the request body contains JSON data. | | `Authorization` | `Bearer [AccessToken]` | A valid access token for authenticating the request. | ### Query Parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | `rid` | integer | A unique, static identifier for each restaurant. Required | | `email_optin` | boolean | List searches to guests that have the email optin flag set to true or false | | `updated_after` | string | Filters guests updated after the specified ISO 8601 timestamp. (UTC time) | | `offset` | integer | The starting point for pagination. Default is `0`. | | `limit` | integer | The maximum number of guests to return per request. Default is `1000`, maximum number available for return is `2000`. | ### Response Structure The response is a JSON object containing metadata about pagination and an array of guest objects. ### Response Attributes | **Attribute** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | `hasNextPage` | boolean | Indicates if there are additional pages of results. | | `nextPageUrl` | string | URL for the next page of results, if available. | | `offset` | integer | The starting offset for the current page. | | `limit` | integer | The maximum number of records returned in this response. | | `items` | array | An array of guest objects containing detailed information about each guest. | ### Guest Object Attributes | **Attribute** | **Type** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | `id` | string | Unique identifier for the guest by restaurant. | | `href` | string | A fully qualified URL for the guest. | | `rid` | integer | A unique identifier for the restaurant this guest is associated with. | | `sequence_id` | integer | Sequential identifier for the guest record within OpenTable systems. | | `gpid` | string | Globally unique OpenTable identifier for the guest. | | `first_name` | string | The first name of the guest. This field may be empty for guests associated with anonymous, walk-in or in-house reservations. | | `last_name` | string | The last name of the guest. This field may be empty for guests associated with anonymous, walk-in or in-house reservations. | | `email` | string | Guest's email address. | | `email_optin` | boolean | Indicates if the guest opted into receiving marketing emails from the restaurant. If the value is true then the email may be sent to the user on behalf of the restaurant. If the value is false then the guest should not be sent emails for marketing purposes. | | `phone` | string | Guest's primary phone number. | | `phone_type` | string | Type of the primary phone number (e.g., Mobile, Home, Work). | | `tags` | array or null | Guest tags that have been applied to the diner profile. | | `birth_date` | string or null | Guest's date of birth, if available. | | `anniversary_date` | string or null | Guest's anniversary date, if available. | | `updated_at` | string | Timestamp of the last update to the guest profile (local time). | | `forgotten` | boolean | The user has requested to have their diner info forgotten. Partners are responsible for removing the diner’s information from all systems where it may be stored. This is a requirement mandated by EU for compliance with General Data Protection Regulation GDPR. | | `archived` | boolean | This flag indicates whether the guest is archived. If true, all attributes aside from primary_guest, id and href would be null for this archived guest. | | `primary_guest` | string or null | This attribute is returned when there is another guest record in the restaurant database that has merged with this record, thus there being data on the main, or primary, record. This attribute will signal the guest_id without the leading RID designation of the primary guest record. Otherwise this attribute is null. | | `address` | string or null | The address of guest. address_line1 (string), address_line2 (string), city (string), state (string), zip (string), country (string). | | `mail_opted_in` | boolean | Indicates if the guest opted into physical mail marketing. | | `company_name` | string or null | Name of the company associated with the guest. | | `phone_numbers` | array | List of phone numbers associated with the guest, including labels, country codes, and primary flags. | | `created_date` | string | Timestamp of when the guest profile was created (local time). | | `updated_at_utc` | string | Timestamp of the last update to the guest profile (UTC time). | | `marketing_opted_out` | boolean | This flag indicates whether an OpenTable network diner has opted in or out of data sharing for purposes beyond operational needs of the restaurant. A value of ‘true’ means the diner has elected not to have their current profile and reservation history used by the restaurant or 3rd party for any additional marketing purposes. A value of ‘false’ means the diners has consented to data sharing for any use case highlighted in the OpenTable consumer privacy policy. | | `notes` | string or null | General notes about the guest. | | `notes_special_relationship` | string or null | Restaurant entered diner information highlighting any association to restaurant, chef or group (ex. friend of chef) | | `notes_food_and_drink` | string or null | Restaurant entered diner information identifying dietary considerations in service (ex. wife is gluten free) | | `notes_seating` | string or null | Restaurant entered seating preferences for diner (ex. table 42 when available) | | `created_date_utc` | string | Timestamp of when the guest profile was created (UTC time). | | `date_last_visit` | string or null | Timestamp of the guest's most recent visit (local time). | | `date_first_visit` | string or null | Timestamp of the guest's first visit (local time). | | `date_last_visit_utc` | string or null | Timestamp of the guest's most recent visit (UTC time). | | `date_first_visit_utc` | string or null | Timestamp of the guest's first visit (UTC time). | | `is_hidden` | boolean | his flag indicates if a guest has been deleted (hidden from the standard guestbook). If true, the guest’s profile has been deleted/hidden from the restaurant’s guestbook. | --- ## Error Response for Guests ### Error Messaging The error response DTO returned by the Sync API collections are in the below format. When reporting issues, always provide the OT-RequestId (located in the response header) to allow OpenTable to uniquely identify a specific request. | PARAMETER | DESCRIPTION | | --- | --- | | **timestamp**
`integer` | The unix time stamp of the error. | | **path**
`string` | The end path of the API you are calling. | | **status**
`integer` | The numerical 3 digit code associated to the error. E.g., 400, 404, 409. | | **error**
`string` | The descriptive phrase associated to the error. E.g., Bad Request, Unauthorized, Not Found. | | **message**
`string` | A short user-readable description of the error. |

item.description:
**As a restauranteur I would like to consume all email addresses that I have stored, that have also been opted in to receive marketing emails.**

## APIs to use:

Sync API - Guests

### **Step 1:**

- Perform initial GET query with historical information.
    
- Add the `email_optin=true` parameter.
    

### Step 2:

- Store the data in your systems. We recommend you store:
    
- `first_name`
    
- `last_name`
    
- `email`
    
- `email_optin`
    
- `updated_at`
    

### Step 3:

- To keep your data up to date, we recommend using the `updated_after` parameter and simply storing any new or updated details. This includes new guests as well as updates to existing guests (i.e. possible opt-outs)

request.description:
## **Overview**

The Reservation Webhook API provides real-time updates about changes in reservation status via webhooks. These updates enable restaurants to seamlessly integrate reservation management into their systems. The webhooks send a detailed JSON payload containing reservation attributes whenever a reservation status changes.

To access Guest Data, please use the `guest` attribute to call the guest endpoint in Sync API. For more information about Sync API and other available data points via the GET query, please refer to the Sync API tier section of this Portal.

### **Change Log**

**** The v3 version of the webhook, released in February 2025 ****

## **Data Delivery Method**

### **Webhook Implementation**

Webhooks are triggered on any change in reservation status. A POST request is sent to the specified endpoint with the payload data.

#### **Configuring Your Endpoint**

- Endpoint URL: Provide an URL where webhook data will be delivered.
    
    - **Webhook URL must use https and port 443**
        
- Authentication: Basic or bearer token is required to verify authenticity of request
    
- Event Handling: Ensure your system handles retries in case of delivery failures.
    

#### **Retry Logic**

Webhook delivery uses an exponential backoff strategy. After three failed attempts, the webhook is marked as undeliverable.

## **Attributes**

The webhook payload will not include the same attributes as available in the GET query. To get the additional attributes not delivered in the webhook, Sync API reservataions endpoint will need to be called. Please see the Sync API Copper Reservations API v2 documention for full list of additional attributes.

OpenTable reserves the right to update the data sent via the webhook with additional attributes. POS partner should build their ingestion of the webhook to accept any new additional attributes in the future. Partner can choose to process or ignore the additional attributes.

The webhook payload includes the following attributes:

| **Attribute** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `sequence_id` | integer | Sequential identifier for the reservation record within OpenTable systems. |
| `guest_id` | string | The unique guest identifier for the restaurant. |
| `confirmation_id` | integer | A confirmation ID for the reservation. |
| `state` | string | The current state of the reservation. States are: NotConfirmed, LeftMessage, WrongNumber, NoAnswer, Confirmed, Late, Partially Arrived, AllArrived, Partially Seated, Seated, Appetizer, Entree, Dessert, CheckDropped, Paid, Cleared, BusTable, Done, NoShow, Cancelled, AssumedSeated, AssumedDone. |
| `table_number` | array | The table number(s) assigned to the reservation. |
| `scheduled_time` | string | The scheduled date and time of the reservation (local time). |
| `party_size` | integer | The number of guests in the reservation. |
| `server` | string | The server assigned to the reservation. |
| `updated_at_utc` | string | Timestamp of the last update to the reservation (UTC time). |
| `id` | string | A globally unique identifier for the reservation. |
| `href` | string | A fully qualified URL for the reservation. |
| `rid` | integer | A unique identifier for the restaurant this reservation is associated with. |
| `guest` | string | A fully qualified URL for the guest associated with the reservation. |
| `created_date` | string | Timestamp of when the reservation was created (local time). |
| `visit_tags` | array | Visit tags that were applied to the reservation by the restaurant (custom or default). |
| `seated_time` | string | Timestamp when the reservation state changed to `Seated` (local time). |
| `done_time` | string | Timestamp when the reservation state changed to `Done`. (local time) |
| `scheduled_time_utc` | string | The scheduled date and time of the reservation (UTC time). |
| `guest_request` | string | The special requests entered by the diner when making their reservation. |
| `venue_notes` | string | Notes entered by the venue regarding the reservation. |
| `opentable_notes` | string | The details of the special offer selected by the diner. |
| `table_category` | string | The table type, or seating option, for the reservation. Options are: “hightop,bar,counter,outdoor”. Note: If the attribute is ‘null’, the table_category will be the “default” attribute. |
| `seated_time_utc` | string | Timestamp when the reservation state changed to `Seated` (UTC time). |
| `done_time_utc` | string | Timestamp when the reservation state changed to `Done`. (UTC time) |
| `created_date_utc` | string | Timestamp of when the reservation was created (UTC time). |
| `updated_at` | string | Timestamp of the last update to the reservation (local time). |
| `origin` | string | The origin source of the reservation (e.g., `Phone`, `Web, Walk-in`). |
| `arrived_time` | string | Timestamp of when the host checked in the diner and the reservation to the status of arrived or partially arrived. (local time). |
| `arrived_time_utc` | string | Timestamp of when the host checked in the diner and the reservation to the status of arrived or partially arrived. (UTC time). |
| `deposit_details` | object | Details of any deposit made. Includes totalPaid, totalRefunded, currency, currencyDenominator, and status (e.g., Paid, FullRefund). |
| `referrer` | list of objects | The information associated to the referrer, or commonly the concierge, associated to a reservation. company: name of the company of the referrer (string), id: unique ID of the referrer in the OpenTable system (string), first_name: the first name of the referrer (string), last_name: the last name of the referrer (string), email: the email address of the referrer (string), phone: the phone number of the referrer (string). |
| `added_to_waitlist` | string | Timestamp of when a record was added to the waitlist. If the record was not added, the value will be null. (local time). |
| `added_to_waitlist_utc` | string | Timestamp of when a record was added to the waitlist. If the record was not added, the value will be null. (UTC time). |

item.description:
Webhook Flow

**As a restauranteur I would like to receive new reservations into my systems as they are made in real time.**

## APIs to use:

Sync API - Reservation Webhook

### **Step 1:**

- Provide OpenTable your https webhook URL that is accessible on port 443 for delivery
    
- Provide OpenTable basic or bearer token for authentication
    

### Step 2:

- Store the data in your systems. We recommend you store:
    
- `scheduled_time`
    
- `party_size`
    
- `guest_id`
    
- `guest`
    
- `updated_at`
    

OpenTable's reservation webhooks are event-driven, meaning they are triggered automatically whenever a reservation is created or updated. Upon these events, OpenTable will send a webhook containing the latest reservation details.

Please note that the attributes included in the webhook payload may differ from those available via the GET reservation endpoint. If additional details are needed, you can make a follow-up GET request using the provided reservation ID.

To align with your system requirements, you can choose to selectively process or ignore specific webhook event types.
Here are some ways our partners use Sync API. These are applicable to both Gold and Platinum tiers.

## Overview

Sync API empowers OpenTable restaurants and groups with direct access to raw, real-time guest and reservation data, enabling seamless integration with custom systems and tools. Designed as the API equivalent of exporting data from the OpenTable restaurant system, the Sync API provides the flexibility and precision needed for bespoke data-driven solutions. By delivering unprocessed data at the restaurant (RID) level, Sync API ensures a clear and unaltered view of operational insights without the complexities of group-level associations or pre-configured reporting frameworks.

Whether your goal is to enhance internal reporting, power personalized email marketing, or integrate data into loyalty, CRM, PMS, or forecasting systems, Sync API is the foundation for building tailored applications. While it doesn’t offer the calculated metrics of OpenTable’s native reporting features, it delivers the raw data necessary to create fully customized reporting and operational workflows. With its focus on real-time data availability, Sync API is an essential tool for restaurants aiming to leverage their data for actionable insights and optimized performance.

item.name: Profile Content API

request.description:
## Updating Profile Content

## Overview

This documentation outlines the process for using the POST API to update Profile content

## Endpoint

**URL**: {{base-endpoint}}/v2/partnerrestaurants/rid/{{RID}}

## **Method**: POST

**Path Parameters:**

- **rid (restaurant id):**
    
    - This is a static value per restaurant, and will be made available to you by OpenTable. Each restaurant will have its own unique, static value. Required
        

## **Notes:**

- Should you not have any data on one of the optional fields, please do not include it
    
- If you would like to remove any existing data and leave the field empty, it will append the attribute and delete whatever detail is inside.
    
- To modify an existing restaurant, the same endpoint for is used. Partners must supply all required information to successfully modify a restaurant in the OpenTable environment.
    

## Request Body

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| id | string | This is the unique ID the partner has in their environment of the restaurant being onboarded. OpenTable uses “restaurant ID” or “RID” as their unique ID. Required. |
| name | string | The name of the restaurant, in the submitted locale, as it appears in the partner systems and as it should appear on the OpenTable network. |
| primaryCuisine | string | The primary cuisine type that will appear on the OpenTable network. Please review the Cuisine table for additional details. |
| otherCuisines | array | Other cuisine types that will appear on the OpenTable network. Please review the master Cuisine table for additional details. |
| phone | string | The phone number of the restaurant that will appear on the OpenTable network, submitted with the country code included, in the below format. |
| currency | string | The currency of the restaurant, in [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217) format. |
| locale | string | The language code of the restaurant, as an IETF language tag. |
| website | string | The website of the restaurant. |
| timeZone | string | Time zone of the restaurant. |
| priceBandId | string | Values are 1, 2, 3, 4, where 1 = $15 and under, 2 = $16 to $30, 3 = $31 to $50, and 4 = $51 and over. Presently, OpenTable translates all values of 1 to be a value of 2 and in a range of 2 = $30 and under. |
| description | string | The description of the restaurant that will appear on the OpenTable network, in the restaurant locale declared in the payload. |
| diningStyle | string | Details the dining style of the restaurant. Please choose one of the following: Fine Dining, Casual Dining, Home Style, Casual Elegant, Elegant Dining. |
| publicTransit | string | Details any public transit stops available or near the restaurant location. Maximum 1000 characters, including spaces. |
| dressCode | string | Details any specific dress code the restaurant enforces. Please choose one of the following: Casual Dress, Jacket Required, Business Casual, Jacket and Tie Required, Formal Attire, Jacket Preferred, Smart Casual, Resort Casual. |
| privateEventDetails | string | Showcases the restaurant offerings for private event space. Maximum 350 characters, including spaces. |
| cateringDetails | string | Showcases the restaurant offering for catering. Maximum 350 characters, including spaces. Optional. |
| executiveChef | string | Name of the executive chef. Optional. |
| crossStreet | string | The cross street of the restaurant, to help diners find the location. Optional. |
| menu | string | The URL of the restaurant’s menu. (To send a full menu, you may choose to leverage OpenTable’s Menu API. This will require additional access). Optional. |
| profileImage | object | A “url” (string) image that will be displayed on the profile page of the restaurant on the OpenTable network. Requirement of Profile Image: JPEG or PNG formats, 1280 X 720 pixels minimum, 10MB Max. Optional. |
| images | array | Additional images, in the form of a “url” (string, with each image being an object) that will be displayed on the OpenTable network associated with the restaurant. No minimum or maximum requirements. Optional. |
| tags | array | A list of key phrases and descriptive terms that help describe the restaurant and assist with searching for the restaurant on the OpenTable network. Optional. |
| openingTimes | object | This object contains the “standardOpeningTimes” (string) with each day of the week’s hours of operation. Optional. |
| openingTimes → standardOpeningTimes | object | Each day of the week (Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, as a string) with “start” (string) and “end” (string) times in 24-hour format. Optional. |
| paymentOptions | array | Payment options available at the restaurant. Please choose one or more of the following: AMEX, Cash Only, Discover, MasterCard, Carte Blanche, Cheque Gourmet, Eurocheque Card, Sodexo Pass, Cash not accepted, Diners Club, JCB, Visa. Optional. |
| beverages | array | Beverages available at the restaurant. Must be one or more of the following: Beer, Cocktails, Wine, BYO Liquor, Corkage Fee, BYO Wine, Full Bar. Optional. |
| dietaryOptions | array | Dietary options at the restaurant. Must be one or more of the following: Gluten-free Options, Kosher, Vegan. Optional. |
| smokingOptions | array | Details what smoking options the restaurant offers. Must be one or more of the following: Indoor Smoking Area, Non-Smoking, Outdoor Smoking Area. Optional. |
| diversitySpotlightTags | array | Please select any categories your restaurant’s owners/operators identify as. One or more of the following: Asian and Pacific Islander, Black/African American, Latinx or Hispanic, LGBTQ, Middle Eastern, Native/Indigenous, etc. Optional. |

Cuisines:

This is a list of all the available cuisines you can use for Restaurants.

item.description: ## **Overview**  The Profile Content API allows certified partners to post profile content for specific restaurants on OpenTable. The Profile Content API is a tool provided by OpenTable that enables certified partners to manage and update profile content for specific restaurants listed on the platform. This API allows partners to directly post and maintain accurate and up-to-date information, ensuring that diners have access to the most relevant details when searching for and selecting restaurants on OpenTable. By utilizing the Profile Content API, certified partners can enhance the overall user experience for OpenTable diners, while also helping restaurants maintain a strong and engaging online presence. ## **How will this impact restaurants?**  This API allows partners to update OpenTable restaurant profile details from outside the OpenTable for Restaurants product. Note, restaurants will not be able to see when the last content update was pushed via the API.  #### ## **Change Log** This API was developed and launched in August 2024

item.name: Change Log
Online Booking API - Changes to Credit Card processing
Online Booking API v2 now supports Spreedly as a payment processor
item.description: # Online Booking API v2 now supports Spreedly as a payment processor If you are integrating with a restaurant that does **not** use Stripe as its credit card provider, you **must** use Spreedly. Continuing to send Stripe payment details for a non-Stripe restaurant will result in a **Make Reservation** request failure. Details of the change are below: Spreedly payment support is available exclusively in Booking API Version 2. This enhancement introduces secure tokenization through Spreedly’s payment gateway, affecting the Slot Lock, Make/Change, and Payment Profile endpoints. ## Affected Endpoints | Name | Endpoint | Method | Description | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Slot Lock | /v2/booking/{rid}/slotlock | POST | Locks a booking slot and returns Spreedly tokenization data. | | Make Reservation | /v2/booking/{rid}/make | POST | Creates a reservation and includes new payment fields. | | Change Reservation | /v2/booking/{rid}/change | POST | Modifies an existing reservation and includes new payment fields. | | Payment Profile | /v2/booking/{rid}/payment/profile | GET | Retrieves Spreedly tokenization credentials for use during the change flow. | ## **Slot Lock API** Overview The Slot Lock response has been extended to include a payment_method object whenever a booking requires Spreedly tokenization. ### **Response Schema** | Field | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | expires_at | string (datetime) | UTC timestamp indicating when the slot lock expires. | | reservation_token | string | Encoded reservation token used in subsequent requests. | | privileged_access | array of string | List of privileges granted for the locked slot. | | access_rule | object | null | | payment_method | object | Contains Spreedly tokenization details. | ## **Object: payment_method** | Field | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | paymentProviderType | string | Identifier of the payment provider (for example, `SPREEDLY`). | | tokenizerAuthData | object | Authorization data used to authenticate with the payment provider. | ## **Object: tokenizerAuthData** | Field | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | nonce | string | Single-use identifier used to prevent replay attacks. | | timestamp | number | UTC timestamp (in seconds) indicating when the tokenization data was generated. | | certificateToken | string | Token representing the client certificate. | | signature | string | Cryptographic signature used to verify the authenticity of the payload. | ## **Sample Response:** ``` json { "expires_at": "2025-10-30T19:45:42", "reservation_token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzUxMiJ9...", "privileged_access": [], "access_rule": null, "payment_method": { "paymentProviderType": "SPREEDLY", "tokenizerAuthData": { "nonce": "77b5d02b-a584-4fd6-ae12-e4af17622799", "timestamp": 1761853243, "certificateToken": "41NRFK9S8B900VZW8KWFZPKFC5", "signature": "C5/RgDHBWv0CTvhwV+LBSW0j+XEM9I+6nidfqRw60td5vP5..." } } } ``` ## **Notes** The payment_method object holds the necessary details for loading the Spreedly iframe, which allows for the tokenization of credit card information. For more information on Spreedly integration, please refer to [https://developer.spreedly.com/docs/iframe-payment-form](https://developer.spreedly.com/docs/iframe-payment-form) . Card tokenization can be achieved by directly calling the Spreedly payment_methods.json API, eliminating the need for a Spreedly iframe. Further details are available at [https://core.spreedly.com/v1/payment_methods.json](https://core.spreedly.com/v1/payment_methods.json) . Partners will require their own Spreedly account to access these pages. # **Make & Change Booking API** ## **Overview** The credit_card substructure has been updated with new Spreedly-specific fields for 3-D Secure (3DS) and tokenized payments. ## **Updated Request Schema within the credit_card object** | Field | Type | Required | Description | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | paymentProviderType | string | No | Specifies the payment provider. Defaults to `STRIPE` if not provided. | | redirectUrl | string | Conditional | Required for 3DS-authenticated payments. URL used for Spreedly redirection. | | mpiData | object | Conditional | Required for 3DS-authenticated payments. Contains Merchant Plug-in data. | | token | string | Yes | Tokenized payment identifier provided by Spreedly. | | last4 | string | Yes | Last four digits of the credit card number. | | paymentMethod | string | Yes | Payment method type (for example, `card`). | | setupIntent | string | Optional | Identifier for the setup intent, if applicable. | | paymentIntent | string | Optional | Identifier for the payment intent, if applicable. | ## **Object: mpiData** | Field | Type | Required | Description | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | threeDSVersion | string | Yes | Version of 3-D Secure used (for example, `1.0`, `2.1.0`). | | eci | string | Yes | Electronic Commerce Indicator (for example, `05` indicates successful authentication). | | cavv | string | Yes | Cardholder Authentication Verification Value. | | dsTransId | string | Yes | Directory Server Transaction ID. | ## **Sample Request:** ``` json { "paymentProviderType": "SPREEDLY", "redirectUrl": "https://example.com/redirect", "mpiData": { "threeDSVersion": "2.1.0", "eci": "05", "cavv": "JSJbzXT6TRQlvZDX+ZdOG4QriE=", "dsTransId": "aaa65c7b-b0fc-4e71-bd6c-29c87acad489" }, "token": "tok_1234567890", "last4": "4242", "paymentMethod": "card", "setupIntent": "seti_abcdef", "paymentIntent": "pi_abcdef" } ``` ## **Notes:** - The redirectUrl and mpiData objects appear only in 3DS-authenticated transactions. - Non-3DS transactions can omit these fields. - Partners are responsible for handling redirect flows post-3DS challenge. # **Payment Profile API** ## **Overview** New Endpoint. Introduced to allow partners to retrieve Spreedly tokenizer credentials during reservation changes. These credentials are typically only issued during the slot lock flow. ## **Endpoint Details** | Method | Path | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | GET | /v2/booking/{rid}/payment/profile | Returns Spreedly tokenizer configuration for the specified restaurant. | ## **Response Schema** | Field | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | tokenizerConfig | object | Contains payment provider–specific configuration. | | tokenizerConfig.Spreedly | object | Spreedly tokenization configuration. | ## **Object: tokenizerConfig.Spreedly** | Field | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | nonce | string | Unique single-use identifier used to prevent replay attacks. | | timestamp | number | UTC timestamp indicating when the tokenization data was generated. | | certificateToken | string | Token representing the client certificate. | | signature | string | Cryptographic signature of the tokenization request payload. | ## **Notes:** - Use this endpoint during change flows to fetch fresh Spreedly credentials. - The values should be used to re-initialize the tokenization iframe or direct API call. - All values (nonce, timestamp, certificateToken, signature) are required to authenticate with Spreedly. ## **Sample Response:** ``` json { "tokenizerConfig": { "Spreedly": { "timestamp": 1761865972, "nonce": "a20b195b-c9fc-48df-a945-4aaac69e95f3", "certificateToken": "41NRFK9S8B900VZW8KWFZPKFC5", "signature": "G4PPQnQhJcVmbjocimDMNAeSg3TzkHwp7v2Ne0g6hr6+x/gp..." } } } ``` # **Error Handling** | HTTP Status | Error Code | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | 400 | INVALID_PAYMENT_DATA | Payment fields are missing or malformed. Verify the token, nonce, and signature. | | 401 | UNAUTHORIZED_PROVIDER | Spreedly credentials are invalid or have expired. | | 403 | ACCESS_DENIED | The partner is not authorized to use Spreedly for the specified restaurant. | | 422 | PAYMENT_VALIDATION_FAILED | Spreedly tokenization verification failed due to a signature mismatch. | | 500 | INTERNAL_ERROR | An unexpected error occurred in the Partner API or payment gateway. | # **Versioning & Compatibility** - Applies to: Booking API V2 only - Legacy (V1) does not support Spreedly integration - Backward compatibility maintained for non-Spreedly bookings # **References** [https://developer.spreedly.com/docs/iframe-payment-form](https://developer.spreedly.com/docs/iframe-payment-form) [https://core.spreedly.com/v1/payment_methods.json](https://core.spreedly.com/v1/payment_methods.json)
## **Important Notice:** As of **October 6, 2025**, OpenTable will support **only TLS 1.3** for all API communication. Ensure your systems are upgraded to maintain secure connectivity.
